name: Wesley Bell
subject: Juneteenth is American History
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Peter,

Juneteenth is more than a history lesson. It’s a celebration of resilience.

Today marks the moment in 1865 when the last enslaved Black Americans in Galveston, Texas finally received word of their freedom, more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation.

Juneteenth is a stark reminder of how far we’ve come, _and how far we still have to go in the fight for true justice and equality._ As we reflect on this day, let us honor those who endured generations of injustice and those who continue to push for a future rooted in dignity, equity, and opportunity for all.

I hope you take time today to celebrate Black freedom, Black culture, and Black excellence, not just as history, **but as a living, breathing force shaping America’s future.**

Onward,

Wesley | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109873432/552530228/552493051?refcode=em25_250619_junete_enask_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250619_junete_enask_gq_act___WB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=58cf94b0-154d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNThjZjk0YjAtMTU0ZC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Uy4vlENXnDjcBVX1XnsZ7jIMmJbVwfQYnVvN_yH5A0o=)

name: Amanda K. Edwards
subject: This Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amanda Edwards for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake_june25?refcode=0619_e [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ake_june25?refcode=0619_e]
Friend,
This Juneteenth, I wanted to share a video I previously recorded with Alana Edwards Holloway right before our first Juneteenth federal holiday.
Alana is the daughter of Texas State Rep. Al Edwards, known to many as the “Father of Juneteenth” because his efforts led to Juneteenth becoming a state holiday in Texas. I hope you enjoy watching this video as much as I enjoyed this important discussion.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y-7RTXZceHE [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y-7RTXZceHE]
Thank you to Alana for sharing these amazing stories and more about the significance of Juneteenth.
Onwards,
Amanda K. Edwards

name: Eric H. Holder, Jr.
subject: 160 years ago today
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
This Juneteenth, the march toward true, lived freedom continues.
|
---

Peter,
Juneteenth marks the day the news of freedom finally reached Black Americans in Galveston, Texas — two and a half full years _after_ President Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation.
This Juneteenth, the 160th anniversary, holds a heavier weight for me. Across the country, we are witnessing attempts to suppress the ability to build a truly representative, multi-racial democracy — a direct threat to some of the very freedoms that this Freedom Day commemorates.
In Texas, where the celebration of Juneteenth was born, **partisan and racial gerrymandering lives and thrives**.
Texas was already being sued for violating the U.S. Constitution and Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act by diluting the voting power of the state’s communities of color.
And just last week, the news broke that President Trump is actively pressuring Texas lawmakers to redraw its already-gerrymandered congressional map in an effort to gain an even more extreme partisan advantage.
On this poignant and powerful anniversary, let’s recommit to the notion that **our freedom is directly proportional to our power at the ballot box**. Let us remember that Juneteenth is not only a celebration, but a reminder that the march toward true, lived freedom is far from over.
Ours is a righteous fight, and I’m proud to be part of it alongside you.
In service,
Eric H. Holder, Jr.
82nd Attorney General of the United States

clean_body: This Juneteenth, the march toward true, lived freedom continues. | Peter, Juneteenth marks the day the news of freedom finally reached Black Americans in Galveston, Texas — two and a half full years after President Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation. This Juneteenth, the 160th anniversary, holds a heavier weight for me. Across the country, we are witnessing attempts to suppress the ability to build a truly representative, multi-racial democracy — a direct threat to some of the very freedoms that this Freedom Day commemorates. In Texas, where the celebration of Juneteenth was born, partisan and racial gerrymandering lives and thrives. Texas was already being sued for violating the U.S. Constitution and Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act by diluting the voting power of the state’s communities of color. And just last week, the news broke that President Trump is actively pressuring Texas lawmakers to redraw its already-gerrymandered congressional map in an effort to gain an even more extreme partisan advantage. On this poignant and powerful anniversary, let’s recommit to the notion that our freedom is directly proportional to our power at the ballot box. Let us remember that Juneteenth is not only a celebration, but a reminder that the march toward true, lived freedom is far from over. Ours is a righteous fight, and I’m proud to be part of it alongside you. In service, Eric H. Holder, Jr. 82nd Attorney General of the United States = is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. Fellow activist – Happy Juneteenth!

In 2021, President Biden signed a bill into law that recognized Juneteenth as a federal holiday, but the history of this celebration goes back 160 years.

On June 19, 1865, _two and a half years after President Abraham Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation_ , Union troops arrived in Galveston Bay, Texas, to enforce the liberation of the state’s more than 250,000 enslaved people. **This date became known as "Juneteenth" to commemorate the end of slavery in the United States.**

Today, we celebrate Black freedom and liberation in the United States and commemorate the continued fight for equality and justice.

Recognizing this holiday has never been more critical as the Trump regime ramps up its attacks on marginalized communities, rolls back Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion initiatives, and tries to silence our voices. That is why today, we invite you to join us in this joyous celebration of freedom and family and reaffirm our commitment to engage historically disenfranchised Black, Indigenous, and other people of color, immigrant communities, and young voters to participate in the democratic process to achieve a more representative democracy.

For us, Juneteenth will always be a day of celebration and advocacy. We hope you’ll join us in honoring this day.

In solidarity,

Team NPP
---
_Donations to the New Pennsylvania Project supports our staff’s community engagement efforts including; organizing and hosting town halls, educational webinars, community conversations, and creating digital content, bi-weekly blasts and social media to reach the communities we engage year-round.
_
---

NPP centers historically disenfranchised and often neglected Black, Indigenous and other people of color, immigrant communities and the youth in our work. Through civic engagement, we ensure all eligible voters feel compelled to exercise their freedom to vote in the Commonwealth._

I supported Bernie in 2016, and many of my friends in WI-03 did the same. Most weren’t political junkies, but we all felt that Bernie was the first candidate since President Obama whose authenticity and moral clarity never wavered.
Everything he does is rooted in uplifting the working class. And if you’ve ever sat with Bernie, you know he doesn’t take kindly to empty slogans. If he's backing you, he wants to know you’ve got the backbone to take on the status quo.
That’s exactly what I’ll do in Congress.
Bernie and I both believe in expanding Medicare to cover dental, vision, and hearing. We both support raising the minimum wage and tying it to inflation. We’re both tired of seeing billionaires bankroll elections while regular people are left behind.
I know folks like to stick labels on candidates -- _moderate, progressive_ , this group or that one. The only label I accept is fighter for working families in Western Wisconsin.
We need to move past the purity tests and recognize when we’re rowing in the same direction. And Bernie and I are on the same page that we need our country to work for the people who actually keep it running.
**This endorsement is powerful. It's about unity. It's about truly living up to our values as advocates for the people. But to make this work, it needs to be backed with people power.**

If you're with us, please send in $3 or more today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109875217/552536105/131323729?refcode=em-6.19.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYmQ5MGU4OC0xNzRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-v-lCHX1TaFe9bBqqsBFTHNrYzR_BMQbJURfRMA2aWk=)

clean_body: | = Peter, I’m honored to share some big news with you: Senator Bernie Sanders just endorsed my campaign for Congress! = | | = I supported Bernie in 2016, and many of my friends in WI-03 did the same. Most weren’t political junkies, but we all felt that Bernie was the first candidate since President Obama whose authenticity and moral clarity never wavered. Everything he does is rooted in uplifting the working class. And if you’ve ever sat with Bernie, you know he doesn’t take kindly to empty slogans. If he's backing you, he wants to know you’ve got the backbone to take on the status quo. That’s exactly what I’ll do in Congress. Bernie and I both believe in expanding Medicare to cover dental, vision, and hearing. We both support raising the minimum wage and tying it to inflation. We’re both tired of seeing billionaires bankroll elections while regular people are left behind. I know folks like to stick labels on candidates -- moderate, progressive , this group or that one. The only label I accept is fighter for working families in Western Wisconsin. We need to move past the purity tests and recognize when we’re rowing in the same direction. And Bernie and I are on the same page that we need our country to work for the people who actually keep it running. This endorsement is powerful. It's about unity. It's about truly living up to our values as advocates for the people. But to make this work, it needs to be backed with people power. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | **= | = **** | | = | = **** | | = | = Thank you, Becca | = = Rebecca Cooke is a small business and nonprofit leader who has focused her career on building community and serving others. name: Dr. Carol Anderson, Fair Fight
subject: Juneteenth 2025
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight
body:
It’s Freedom Day. |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109875453/552537108/1793776944?refcode=bsc_emm_ff_20250619_T1_Juneteenth_v1s1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDMvMS84ODQ1MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiNzY0NjgwYS0xODRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c_7Uz73_vTbYAI6K4ZzcPPIFii_n4cbT1GcAw-sYSA4=&emci=b564680a-184d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b764680a-184d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=1037277)

| | Happy Juneteenth, friends! 160 years ago to the day, Black Americans in Galveston, Texas became free — nearly two and a half years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed. Although the Civil War ended months earlier, enslavers in Texas continued to hold Black people in bondage well after — that is, until the 2,000 Union troops arrived in Galveston with news of freedom. Since then, Juneteenth represents a day to celebrate the Black experience in our country and reflect on the journey towards equality. And while Juneteenth is a celebration of our ancestors' fight, **it’s also a reminder to continue their legacy and ensure that, now and for generations to come, Black people can fully embrace freedom in America. ** In the years that followed emancipation, Black people across the country were still met with Jim Crow era laws that stripped away their rights and freedoms. Tactics like poll taxes, literacy tests, and violent intimidation made it harder for Black Americans to vote and make their voices heard in our democracy. **In today’s America, the far-right engage in eerily similar tactics, including blatantly coordinating efforts to silence voters and suppress the vote of Black and brown communities. ** Bigoted lawmakers place hurdles between Black voters and the ballot box because **they’re afraid** of losing their power once Black Americans are able to fully use theirs. In honor of the freedom fighters who came both before and after June 19, 1865, let us continue to prevail. Their legacy lives on in Fair Fight’s work, and in our modern-day movement to protect the right to vote and hold anti-voter extremists accountable. In solidarity, Dr. Carol Anderson

clean_body: It’s Freedom Day. | | | Happy Juneteenth, friends! 160 years ago to the day, Black Americans in Galveston, Texas became free — nearly two and a half years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed. Although the Civil War ended months earlier, enslavers in Texas continued to hold Black people in bondage well after — that is, until the 2,000 Union troops arrived in Galveston with news of freedom. Since then, Juneteenth represents a day to celebrate the Black experience in our country and reflect on the journey towards equality. And while Juneteenth is a celebration of our ancestors' fight, it’s also a reminder to continue their legacy and ensure that, now and for generations to come, Black people can fully embrace freedom in America. In the years that followed emancipation, Black people across the country were still met with Jim Crow era laws that stripped away their rights and freedoms. Tactics like poll taxes, literacy tests, and violent intimidation made it harder for Black Americans to vote and make their voices heard in our democracy. In today’s America, the far-right engage in eerily similar tactics, including blatantly coordinating efforts to silence voters and suppress the vote of Black and brown communities. Bigoted lawmakers place hurdles between Black voters and the ballot box because they’re afraid of losing their power once Black Americans are able to fully use theirs. In honor of the freedom fighters who came both before and after June 19, 1865, let us continue to prevail. Their legacy lives on in Fair Fight’s work, and in our modern-day movement to protect the right to vote and hold anti-voter extremists accountable. In solidarity, Dr. Carol Anderson Fair Fight | = | * 1270 Caroline Street | Suite D120-430 | Atlanta, GA 30307 ©2025 Fair Fight - All rights reserved. name: Lizzie Fletcher
subject: Juneteenth and all it represents
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
Today, we celebrate Juneteenth and all it represents, in Texas and across the country.
Juneteenth has special significance for Texans because on this day in 1865—more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation was issued—Texans received word of the end of slavery in the U.S. when Major General Gordon Granger arrived in Galveston and announced that all slaves were now free and had “absolute equality” of personal rights and property rights. That moment marked a turning point in our nation's long and unfinished journey toward freedom, equality, and justice.
Since that day, Texans have recognized Juneteenth as a celebration of freedom, of progress hard-won, and of the hope that continues to guide us forward. It is a moment to honor the courage and perseverance of those who fought for justice and to recommit ourselves to that cause today.
But this Juneteenth arrives at a moment when our history is under attack—not just forgotten, but deliberately rewritten. The Trump administration’s recent executive order, cynically titled “Restoring Truth and Sanity to American History,” seeks to erase uncomfortable truths rather than confront them. It dismisses honest reflection as “divisive” and calls inclusive narratives “improper ideology.” That’s not truth. It’s propaganda.
As Americans, we have a long, complicated, and essential history. And understanding all of it—the triumphs and the failures—is how we move forward. There is nothing more American than seeking a full, honest accounting of our past so that we can live up to our founding ideals.
Juneteenth is a celebration of our ability to do that—to advance the cause of freedom, to expand opportunity, and to make meaningful progress. Today reminds us that freedom is not a moment, it is a movement. And if we are not moving forward, we are moving in the wrong direction.
Today, as we celebrate freedom and progress, we also must mobilize to protect freedom and progress and the promise of America.
Together, we can do anything,
Lizzie Signature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Honoring Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Hello, Juneteenth is a celebration of freedom – and a solemn reminder of the work still ahead. On June 19th, 1865, more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation, the last enslaved Americans in Galveston, Texas, were finally told they were free. That moment marked delayed justice, and its echoes are still felt today. During my time in the Senate, I’ve worked alongside leaders from New York’s Black and African American communities to expand access to healthcare, defend voting rights and push for equity in education, housing and public safety. Together, we’ve fought to address injustice — but this work is far from finished. That’s why, this Juneteenth, **[I’m asking you to stand with one of New York’s most consequential civil rights organizations: the New York NAACP.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109875685/552538002/186668778?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOWNhNWNhNS0xODRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qaEabvjHwvguet3-Dlk3V1BNDkt6ET_kdh5ZG5iYWXM=&emci=327c6518-184d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=29ca5ca5-184d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7659029)** | | [Support the NYS NAACP >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109875686/552538003/186668778?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyOWNhNWNhNS0xODRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qaEabvjHwvguet3-Dlk3V1BNDkt6ET_kdh5ZG5iYWXM=&emci=327c6518-184d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=29ca5ca5-184d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=7659029)
---
For more than a century, the NAACP has led the fight to dismantle racism and build a nation where equity and justice are real for everyone. The NAACP New York Branch carries that mission forward — advocating for voting rights, fair education, criminal justice reform and economic opportunity across our communities.
Let’s honor Juneteenth not just with words — but with action.
Together,
Kirsten Gillibrand

name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: I didn’t just fight for this country — I bled for it.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
I didn’t risk my life across 70 countries to come home and be told the truth no longer matters. | [](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=29b693daae4145538cb55b10b8379112&_e=VpKce1411Ncp7e2qNCFvys9-2-TDSeo0qM1arzt_-4O3_mCFUZbQ87uJJA4RWZcl6VIZu6cjc96wxKFcGJNvOvUMgx6K3IdxFn0C-MMOfJpNJ73lkv48yIiAoM7Ah4wWtGH83h_aHdOpxnPDRE-q576RHtOkFHEdV3frxshnuu1CZ6kvRZlG1h9QXtlefQN93pARYMrbBJaPMhhqo_NzBVI5MI-ciLc3PFVDq3UGnMlNBQF3gVDl7vEAA7kLWHu409KgdKijwvSFsJ0CxuGUMIK79P_7VdAMwLG0GaxzCfes-0HUbuie4usXd_dIj2O1s2ZRUib4p6EonjH7ZPSQIHCJMiM8-hh7T7bYihtClcxraHwAn8KiyTRXhoGIMIo1jMJDlhzv52PgxZiEBocOzefgwqsO3cBmJVmzrackdQiXWEjCd2jsPZgzIh3qjd1L0zyLk2AjYJXeeZjPN4QCCung7LPxgrS7Em0CKaSreIxa8VxsKr87wnl-aBtafQ1EawdEb3-bPd9m0atzOgHpfuQFP2p_ULZtwunR0p1YaFwqLBS0gNVSqnxI5fts12hwEAETwwvY6bAuHvhuyfVoD5y7lHGyp-RDpwfg0z9Fw9-U5seQxsh045OumuTUM2wbCnlVRxydkrXC1sM1BPgr4YNKZGb-RNNgHdzUONDjRJa-EYWVStHe2JnIWYFbMLjOEoEF6JWwUAuDKL5k5jGM1x1ZYm-Un0gdjT4W_GosfyH1T9KuS00zivYoqdbMA6Jj)

---

Fellow Conservative,

Figuring out where to start writing this email has been tough because the content of the copy you’re about to read is, quite honestly, absurd.

I’ve spent most of my adult life in some of the world’s most dangerous places. **I’ve seen what happens when governments collapse, the truth is suppressed, and strong men choose silence instead of standing up. But I never thought I’d see it here, not in America.**

And yet last week, sitting in a congressional hearing, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz was asked a straightforward question:

_**“Can you define what a woman is?”**_

_**He couldn’t.**_
_**He wouldn’t.**_

He dodged. He deflected. And then he sat in silence.

Let me be clear — this wasn’t a trick. This wasn’t a “gotcha” question. This was the easiest test a leader can face: tell the truth, or bow to the mob.
**
Governor Walz chose the mob.**

That moment — that silence — wasn’t just embarrassing. It was dangerous.

Because when leaders abandon truth to protect their politics, it’s the people who suffer. And right now, our daughters, wives, sisters, and grandmothers are being erased in real time—not just from locker rooms and playing fields but from language itself.

My name is Adam Schwarze.

**I’m a proud Minnesotan and a retired Navy SEAL.** I joined the Marine Corps after 9/11 and served in special operations, deploying 13 times to over 70 countries.

_**I didn’t just fight for this country — I bled for it.**_

[](http://links.justandfreegop.com/u/click?_t=8ca5ef3d31cc43bcb1fbe6968d953911&_m=29b693daae4145538cb55b10b8379112&_e=VpKce1411Ncp7e2qNCFvys9-2-TDSeo0qM1arzt_-4O3_mCFUZbQ87uJJA4RWZcl6RUzK-eOx7kQO2oVUpvkiksQJjK_Z3vpkcQdOEMUXtUXOek23FzL3A3uVvYXggEdDxRNnUBZacJBHmb8US9x1jOL-bgD1FPl7QOAJbHXqtoz-5iZKH7YwSePYK5tQSZq-FEgxsOwjtlzngzU_v0WUcyT_FA7Gb_F08R9lMWFvTzYGoUzmF2-HZ1sMe2EGSLhlEh2BH34g9Fh9J2H349578KdSP_UsYxP9X3-PyGM3Lp_T9edDH09dR4XNg5ljeTdqZbhRTMCowEMpDblaQsJLlDnsrCgYntC-dep7S96u4xoV6UD6cwcoxyQ0MjFejgB3eA3PpNNwh3gYnort4-TzAn5aFqeddduIyqYcZhZXDBR_wYSVH8rPKRhqNSg73vr2gR02RpsL1aGflJuaSxQpcZfmfiXLcGy-jKU-33225_OnfCTG3997noXD_5MdPjZnADdtTSbFyGdPZeK_wnkHtX_g6AABrT_tkjcLLyUDTxYBEyrUxXdEXPFsiqONRGxHVJlzdmaWG5OUr7dl9kKYpN_6JpOraL-iwzdABgU8p4HsmrNQimnwbRshWhpMUCkgqcSf2Y1OMWJzL5ekiroNjKa7lKG4Sk5ZfY7dfYbwWPaUQsJz6A-jYsggAXtYK597kc5eCCDME_OcASRB0QBKeT6O0qL3f4sY2qKryliTZpwv-UWpV6uK3iEqozeIUjA_MzBF-splp01LsjfdYL5K3kqDIYFCQxjkZwD6iv8vLc%3D)

And I never imagined I’d be running for public office. But something has changed. Something fundamental.

When a governor can’t say what a woman is...
When “men in women’s sports” becomes policy...
**When the truth becomes optional, and lies become virtue, that’s when I knew I had one more mission.**

I’m running for the United States Senate because I believe America is worth saving. But it’s going to take more than votes — it’s going to take backbone.

I’ve seen the cost of freedom. I’ve watched friends give everything so that future generations could live in a country built on truth. I won’t let their sacrifice be undone by a political class too cowardly to speak plainly.

clean_body: I didn’t risk my life across 70 countries to come home and be told the truth no longer matters. | Fellow Conservative, Figuring out where to start writing this email has been tough because the content of the copy you’re about to read is, quite honestly, absurd. I’ve spent most of my adult life in some of the world’s most dangerous places. I’ve seen what happens when governments collapse, the truth is suppressed, and strong men choose silence instead of standing up. But I never thought I’d see it here, not in America. And yet last week, sitting in a congressional hearing, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz was asked a straightforward question: “Can you define what a woman is?” He couldn’t. He wouldn’t. He dodged. He deflected. And then he sat in silence. Let me be clear — this wasn’t a trick. This wasn’t a “gotcha” question. This was the easiest test a leader can face: tell the truth, or bow to the mob. Governor Walz chose the mob. That moment — that silence — wasn’t just embarrassing. It was dangerous. Because when leaders abandon truth to protect their politics, it’s the people who suffer. And right now, our daughters, wives, sisters, and grandmothers are being erased in real time—not just from locker rooms and playing fields but from language itself. My name is Adam Schwarze. I’m a proud Minnesotan and a retired Navy SEAL. I joined the Marine Corps after 9/11 and served in special operations, deploying 13 times to over 70 countries. I didn’t just fight for this country — I bled for it. And I never imagined I’d be running for public office. But something has changed. Something fundamental. When a governor can’t say what a woman is... When “men in women’s sports” becomes policy... When the truth becomes optional, and lies become virtue, that’s when I knew I had one more mission. I’m running for the United States Senate because I believe America is worth saving. But it’s going to take more than votes — it’s going to take backbone. I’ve seen the cost of freedom. I’ve watched friends give everything so that future generations could live in a country built on truth. I won’t let their sacrifice be undone by a political class too cowardly to speak plainly. That’s why I need your help today. The Democratic Party isn’t just drifting — it’s sprinting toward something unrecognizable. First, they said men could use women’s bathrooms. Then they demanded that girls compete against biological males in school sports. Now, they claim they don’t even know what a woman is. And Governor Walz? He’s not a bystander. He’s a willing participant in this charade. His likely successor — my opponent — is even more radical. So if you’re like me — and Stand with me. Fight back. Let’s win this. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109875885/552538701/1328194270?source=EM_NBI_BWC_20250619_PT_Juneteenth_0_3MO_BonnieWatsonColeman_NA_NA_NA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDY4NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjYjhlOTM1My0xOTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=paflV4ucSy4sPu7ZpNfCd7Dy5RDM_OaKQfU2wTZrOcY=&emci=cfcceedc-8a4c-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=cb8e9353-194d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=30096098) | Today, on Juneteenth, we reflect on a pivotal moment in our history—the emancipation of Black Americans. More than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation was issued, freedom finally reached enslaved African Americans in Galveston, Texas, on June 19, 1865.

Juneteenth is a celebration of liberation — and a powerful reminder of the strength, resistance, and enduring contributions of Black communities.

It is also a call to action. The legacy of slavery lives on through systemic racism, mass incarceration, economic injustice, and the lack of diversity at the top of our political and economic institutions.

Juneteenth asks us to reflect on the ongoing fight for equality and to renew our commitment to building a future rooted in liberty, justice, and opportunity for all.

On this day, we remember where we’ve been and renew our commitment to a future where fairness, acceptance, and justice are guaranteed for everyone.

In solidarity,

Bonnie Watson Coleman
---

Bonnie Watson Coleman represents New Jersey's 12th district in Congress. She is a strong fighter for progressive values and has dedicated herself to the service of her community.

We've JUST learned that Trump’s allies are plotting a full-scale power grab in the MOST CRUCIAL SWING STATE of Georgia... and it’s ALREADY in motion!!!
A new report just revealed that Gov. Brian Kemp is quietly working to boost Trump’s preferred Senate candidate.
The goal? Flip Georgia red and cement Republican control of the Senate.
We’re doing everything we can to stop it — including launching a $60,000 fund to help shore up resources in Georgia and other battleground states. But we’re still $13,638 away from hitting our goal - can you help close the gap? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-georgia?refcode=061825_4]

name: NH Senate Republicans
subject: Sen. Kevin Avard: Alarm bells over China tariffs; silence on Nashua water deal
disclaimer_text: Paid for by New Hampshire Senate Republicans PAC
body:
| |
---
| [View in browser](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0yNzcwNDcxNjg5NTkzNTU4MDUyJmM9eTRqNCZlPTE5MjgmYj0xNDUzNjI5MzcxJmQ9dzh0N3A4dg==.-ujSifs3sj8IKC-3H-10Qicc47ISngh-3N4wQgkNQrg)

| |
---
| Friends, Did you see Sen. Kevin Avard's recent op-ed about the risks posed by China's land and water supply purchases in Nashua? Take a look below and considering making a [contribution to the NH Senate Republicans here. (Secure Link)](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0yNzcwNDcxNjg5NTkzNTU4MDUyJmM9eTRqNCZlPTE5MjgmYj0xNDUzNjI5MzcyJmQ9ejRiNG42bA==.Rm_v69KI3h1Tm8K5OVpMdp-h3w0Lfwehp68OunlX5pw) \- NH Senate Republicans
---
---
| |
---
|
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| Sen. Kevin Avard: Alarm bells over China tariffs; silence on Nashua water deal

---
---
| |
---
| June 17, 2025 AMERICA is under attack — again.
The culprit? Surprise, surprise: China. Two Chinese nationals were recently arrested for smuggling a toxic crop-killing pathogen into the United States. The noxious fungus also leads to reproductive defects in humans and livestock. Making matters worse, the alleged agroterrorists have been bringing the pathogen into the U.S. as far back as August 2022. Chinese officials claim no knowledge of the situation. You would be foolish to believe them. According to the U.S. Department of Justice, at least one of the Chinese nationals is a member of the Chinese Communist Party (CCP). She received funding from the Chinese government for her research on the pathogen — and its potential for terrorism. The other (her boyfriend) works at a Chinese university, conducting research on the same pathogen. While China plans attacks from afar, we have been playing catch-up at home. Under the Biden administration, then-National Security Advisor Jake Sullivan coordinated the White House’s China policy over four years. Sullivan repeatedly met with CCP officials to foster “bilateral alliances.” Xi Jinping even talked up US-China “traditional friendship” with Sullivan. Between 2017 and 2020, Sullivan worked at the consulting firm Macro Advisory Partners, advising foreign governments and companies on penetrating the U.S. market. The firm proudly touts its work for businesses with footprints in China — under the watchful eye of the CCP — although Sullivan has always played down his consulting work. Nor was Sullivan’s boss in the White House some China hawk. Between Joe Biden’s time as vice president and president, his think tank, the Penn Biden Center — received at least $61 million from Chinese individuals and entities. The newest advance guard from China? A massive CCP-linked company seeking a foothold in America’s water supply. Nashua is the first city to grant a domestic water contract to Nongfu Spring, with Nongfu announcing plans to expand aggressively in America by gaining access to our tap water for bottling operations. You might expect our congressional delegation to question the decision. But don’t expect much from Rep. Maggie Goodlander, D-NH, whose district includes Nashua and who also happens to be Jake Sullivan’s wife. Goodlander is intent on criticizing Trump’s China tariffs and Trump’s revocation of visas for Chinese students. Concern over selling access to our water supply? Crickets. Our two New Hampshire senators are more concerned with celebrating Pride Month and peddling gun control, while Nongfu hijacks the Nashua local utility. Nongfu’s $67 million purchase of 23 acres and an industrial building was cleverly timed for last January 31. On that dead zone date, the Biden team was out of office, while Trump staffers were still looking for their new desks. The Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) — our federal watchdog on Chinese investments in the U.S. — was inoperable. The Chinese already own controlling interests in thousands of American companies. Hopefully, CFIUS will exercise its power to abrogate this contract that potentially threatens our national interests. Any company subject to CCP influence that has access to America’s food or water supply ought to qualify for debarment. _Sen. Kevin Avard, R-Nashua, represents District 12 and chairs the Senate Energy and Natural Resources Committee._

name: Tim
subject: Official Voter ID Poll for Friend!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scott Digital Victory Fund
body:
Democracy was founded on a simple principle

[](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zm8o37tn00hip3g6rnb83hiuttfneqb0muh8aong_rp22sh2s8i6epb0cpimeo76c1h2bs)Friend,
Democracy was founded on a simple principle:

name: Lindsay Brown, IDAAC
subject: Honoring Juneteenth—And Continuing the Fight for Justice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
|
---
Peter, Today, on **Juneteenth** , we [honor and remember the day](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109876498/552541133/966222187?refcode=250619juneteenth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2MxNGFkNC0xYTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xw-5py_HMQRwD-Cq4N6g65GbCZswZ6keq5n0h4qtZKY=&emci=9b664233-194d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=4cc14ad4-1a4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) when the last enslaved people in Galveston, Texas were finally informed of their freedom—more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation. Juneteenth is not only a commemoration of freedom—it’s a call to action. Here in Indiana, the fight for justice and equity continues. From protecting voting rights and expanding access to healthcare, to ending racial disparities in education, housing, and our criminal justice system—**the work is far from over.** Just this year, Mike Braun and the Republicans [eliminated Diversity, Equity and Inclusion](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109876499/552541134/-1081134050?refcode=250619juneteenth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2MxNGFkNC0xYTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xw-5py_HMQRwD-Cq4N6g65GbCZswZ6keq5n0h4qtZKY=&emci=9b664233-194d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=4cc14ad4-1a4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535), reversing protections that prohibited racial discrimination in hiring for state government jobs, slashed funding for Indiana’s only Black private college, Martin University, decimated funding for public schools and countless public programs on which Hoosier families of all races depend. On top of all this, Lieutenant Governor Micah Beckwith had the audacity to [publicly praise the Three-Fifths Compromise](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109876500/552541135/656050920?refcode=250619juneteenth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2MxNGFkNC0xYTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xw-5py_HMQRwD-Cq4N6g65GbCZswZ6keq5n0h4qtZKY=&emci=9b664233-194d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=4cc14ad4-1a4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=4925535) and Indiana Republicans refused to condemn his comments. Here at the Indiana Democratic Party is committed to building a state where freedom is real and full for every Hoosier, regardless of race or zip code. But we can’t do it without you. **[Will you make a contribution today to help us elect Democrats up and down the ballot who will fight for true equity and justice?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109876501/552541136/1764064610?refcode=250619juneteenth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Y2MxNGFkNC0xYTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Xw-5py_HMQRwD-Cq4N6g65GbCZswZ6keq5n0h4qtZKY=)** Your support helps us organize in every corner of Indiana, support candidates who reflect our communities, and turn out the vote in critical elections. Let’s honor the legacy of Juneteenth not just with reflection—but with action. Thanks,
Lindsay Brown
President, Indiana Democrat African American Caucus (IDAAC)

subject: as we celebrate Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democrats Win Seats PAC
body:
Our mission at DWS PAC is to strategically direct resources to help elect Democrats around the country, and our success depends on support from thousands of donors across the country. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here](https://go.dwspac.com/p/bg-w4eO8DEWuKhf2unAuQg2?unsubscribedata=83IJqUMKyoymX%2FFn0pM58T9aaGNW4EqWTTfhuttEBLrapcQzoEADvj63Ef6PjSjLy5%2FzcCmMpaYOgD0lAioUWBDrlJLns7d9HGGGfuCX2XCu9iYva4lyOK%2BG%2Bn0na18A7sXdB%2Fgh01EHtQKE6TnycomPjq%2BLqibCF7GfwbgUfd4oRbahcKAASXWpXY0XDrTgPc5yPr9xfNyVLjNUlQmzRm1eUJXl6mDKvFFiDLPryOXz%2BF2T7L3Rag2KvG9Ob1PKOvXVQspv2Yzpc6iM3gkqH3UOm2a0OPjyVM1ctgZdVwvTQ7cw5X1CjEK7229aXIBcDtMZo1oMrgIbZ%2BrMj94ZPosTyAnLZ3bSkxRWxm6YpN%2FQXBUQS9s4zmvZJS2ZfYGlUekoFLhSs0FxSim88iMAlSCJZ%2FBTzyZfeuMSLPwdmm4%3D).
---
| Peter,

Today, on Juneteenth, we commemorate the emancipation of Black Americans and honor the strength, resilience, and enduring contributions of the generations who have fought to move our nation closer to its founding promise: equality for all.

Juneteenth is not only a celebration of freedom — it’s also a time of reflection. Together, we remember the courage and perseverance of the generations of activists, community leaders, and everyday citizens who stood up and demanded a more just America.

Yet, we know the work is far from over. The legacy of injustice persists, and the struggle for civil rights, equal opportunity, and democratic representation is ongoing.

We’re committed to honoring the legacy of those who came before us by continuing the fight. That means organizing our communities, advocating for systemic change, and working to elect leaders who will uphold the values of justice, equality, and liberty for all people.

Today, as we celebrate Juneteenth, let us reaffirm our responsibility to build a future where freedom and equality are fully realized for everyone.

— Democrats Win Seats
---

name: Squad Victory Fund
subject: Happy Juneteenth
body:
[ https://act.squadvictoryfund.com/go/18910?refcode=em20250619-1385-foote&t=1001&akid=1385%2E342411%2EpXCetM ]Squad Victory Fund

Peter,

Juneteenth signaled the effective end of slavery in the United States. On
June 19, 1865 Union troops finally arrived in Galveston, Texas to share
the news of emancipation. Though the true end of slavery didn’t come until
the 13th amendment was ratified, newly freed people in Texas began
celebrating Jubilee Day or Juneteenth the following year to commemorate
the day.

Juneteenth is a day of remembrance. A day of reflection. A day of joy. But
most of all, it’s a day to recommit ourselves to the fight for racial
justice.

With Donald Trump back in the White House, banning DEI and removing Black
History content from federal websites, it is more important than ever that
we commemorate and celebrate Juneteenth today.

We know that real liberation means affordable housing, environmental
justice, health care for every person, and investment for our communities
— as budgets are reflections of values. The Squad is fighting for that
future — with policy, organizing, and bold leadership.

Happy Juneteenth. Black lives matter. Black joy matters. Black futures
matter.

In solidarity,

Squad Victory Fund

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

clean_body: [ ]Squad Victory Fund Peter, Juneteenth signaled the effective end of slavery in the United States. On June 19, 1865 Union troops finally arrived in Galveston, Texas to share the news of emancipation. Though the true end of slavery didn’t come until the 13th amendment was ratified, newly freed people in Texas began celebrating Jubilee Day or Juneteenth the following year to commemorate the day. Juneteenth is a day of remembrance. A day of reflection. A day of joy. But most of all, it’s a day to recommit ourselves to the fight for racial justice. With Donald Trump back in the White House, banning DEI and removing Black History content from federal websites, it is more important than ever that we commemorate and celebrate Juneteenth today. We know that real liberation means affordable housing, environmental justice, health care for every person, and investment for our communities — as budgets are reflections of values. The Squad is fighting for that future — with policy, organizing, and bold leadership. Happy Juneteenth. Black lives matter. Black joy matters. Black futures matter. In solidarity, Squad Victory Fund

name: Team OLCV
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth!
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/61925%20Juneteenth%20-%20email%20header.png]
Hi Peter,
Today is Juneteenth, serving not only as a celebration of Black freedom and resilience, but also a reminder of all the structural inequalities, racism, and discrimination still present in our country today.
It’s important for us to acknowledge Oregon’s longstanding history of racism, particularly against Black communities, as we go about today. This work to dismantle racial injustice is ongoing, and one that OLCV is committed to fighting everyday.
If you’d like to celebrate Juneteenth, be sure to catch one of these events around Oregon:
Portland
* Freedom Celebration [https://www.mesopdx.org/events] * When: Thursday, June 19th at 11:00am - 5:00pm
* Where: Meso Makers Market (376 NE Sumner St, Portland, OR 97211)

* 10th Annual Black Liberation Ride [https://www.shift2bikes.org/calendar/event-20913] * When: Thursday, June 19th at 6:00pm
* Where: Irving Park (707 NE Fremont St, Portland, OR 97212)

* Power of Place Roadshow [https://tomorrowtheater.org/movies/power-of-place-juneteenth-event/]

* When: Thursday, June 19th at 7:00pm
* Where: Tomorrow Theater (3530 SE Division St, Portland, OR 97202)

* The Clara Peoples Freedom Trail Parade [https://juneteenthor.com/] * When: Saturday, June 21st at 11:00am
* Where: King School (4906 NE 6th Ave, Portland, OR 97211)

Lake Oswego
* Lake Oswego Juneteenth Celebration [https://www.ci.oswego.or.us/parksrec/juneteenth-2025] * When: Sunday, June 22nd at 1:00pm - 4:00pm
* Where: Millennium Plaza Park (200 1st Street, Lake Oswego, 97034)

Salem
* In Freedom’s Footsteps: Juneteenth Celebration Walk [https://www.willametteheritage.org/calendar/juneteenth-community-history-walk/] * When: Thursday, June 19th at 9:00am - 4:00pm
* Where: Willamette Heritage Center (1313 Mill St SE, Salem, OR 97301)

* Juneteenth Celebration: Black Wallstreet [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/juneteenth-celebration-black-wallstreet-tickets-1330443134229] * When: Thursday, June 19th at 12:00pm - 6:00pm
* Where: 680 State Street Salem, OR 97301

Eugene
* 2nd Annual Juneteenth Gathering at Lane County Farmer’s Market Pavilion [https://www.facebook.com/events/649419691131178] * When: Thursday, June 19th at 3:00pm - 8:00pm
* Where: Lane County Farmer’s Market (85 E 8th Ave, Eugene, OR 97401)

Medford
* Southern Oregon’s BIGGEST Juneteenth Celebration [https://baseoregon.org/event/2025-juneteenth-celebration/] * When: Saturday, June 21st at 11:00am - 8:00pm
* Where: Pear Blossom Park (312 E. 4th Street, Medford, OR)

Oregon City
* Oregon City Juneteenth Celebration [https://www.orcity.org/3174/Juneteenth] * When: Saturday, June 21st at 2:00pm
* Where: End of the Oregon Trail Interpretive Center (1726 Washington St, Oregon City, OR 97045)

To honor Black freedom, reflect, and recenter our efforts on environmental justice, OLCV staff will be out of office today.
Happy Juneteenth!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/AV/AVLCV/1/70877/images/Email%20Logo.png]

1110 SE Alder Suite 220
Portland, OR 97214

| | Peter, Happy Juneteenth! As the first Black Lieutenant Governor of Pennsylvania, I'm reflecting a lot today on the generations of Black Americans who came before me and fought for me to have the opportunities I have today. I know that even in my grandparents' wildest dreams, they would not have imagined that their grandson would one day occupy the second-highest office in Pennsylvania. _But I wouldn't be here without them._ **It's this understanding that motivates me to make life better for everyone who will come after me. That's why I care so much about investing in our shared future. ** We have come a long way as a country in our fight for racial equality, but we still have a long way to go. As we celebrate the wins we've achieved and practice gratitude today, we must also remember how we can support one another to create a better tomorrow. **I'm ready to fight for that in Pennsylvania. I promise to always stand up to injustice, wherever and whenever I see it. ** Thank you, Peter. Lt. Gov. Austin Davis _P.S. – If you can chip in to my re-election campaign today, my team and I would be incredibly grateful. name: Shontel Brown
subject: Today, we observe 160 years of Juneteenth ✊🏾
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shontel Brown for Congress
body:
I hope we all have the opportunity today to immerse ourselves in the strength of our diversity while reflecting on the needs of our Black communities.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109877921/552546186/1806897946?refcode=EM_C_250619Juneteenth_N_SB_90DO_5_NG_N_logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGRjMzQxMy0xZTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JXf8lEyx1m9GUtBhjqjAg8JF8if6n4myHC_fiPSqPkw=) Peter,

Today, we observe 160 years of Juneteenth, celebrating the liberation and resilience of Black Americans. On June 19, 1865, the news of freedom finally reached the last emancipated people in Texas, two and a half years after President Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation, and 160 years later, we are still fighting for equality and justice.

**Juneteenth has always connected past and present – but it is especially important this year. Trump has been working overtime to roll back diversity, equity, and inclusion in our federal government and working to make our nation less fair, less just, and less inclusive.**

We’re not going to stand for it. In Congress, I am working to further federal support for Black history education, bolster civil rights protections, and expand economic opportunity for all. Black culture and history are etched into our country’s history and culture – and I am celebrating that powerful sentiment today and every day.

**I hope we all have the opportunity today to immerse ourselves in the strength of our diversity while reflecting on the needs of our Black communities.**

There’s no greater honor than fighting for the rights of my people and every disenfranchised American during this pivotal time in our history.

Gratefully,

Shontel Brown | Shontel Brown is proud to be elected as the Congresswoman for Ohio’s 11th District. She has spent the last decade serving the people of Ohio and building coalitions. As she enters her second full term in Congress, she will use that experience to continue the fight for affordable quality health care, jobs that pay fair wages, a strong public education system, and to preserve and protect our democracy for generations to come. Will you join her and be the change we need?
---
**[DONATE TODAY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109877922/552546187/1806897946?refcode=EM_C_250619Juneteenth_N_SB_90DO_5_NG_N_biobox&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzg0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4OGRjMzQxMy0xZTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JXf8lEyx1m9GUtBhjqjAg8JF8if6n4myHC_fiPSqPkw=)**
** **
** **
** **

Columbus, OH 43215
United States

name: Bryan
subject: Just 2 weeks away
body:
I’m being targeted by National Democrats. There are only two weeks left to HIT OUR GOAL

| | | | | [|
---](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=32d3a638fe&e=e907534017)
| | | | | **We are less than 2 weeks out from one of our most important deadlines of the year and Democrats are watching carefully!**
---
| | | | | Our 2nd-quarter deadline is coming up quickly. National Democrats are eyeing this seat. We need to put up a strong fundraising number to keep their dark money donors at bay!
Here are some facts about what I’m up against:
* Hakeem Jeffries and the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee (DCCC) **announced that I am one of their**[**top targets**](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=58818d6993&e=e907534017)**for** **2026.**
* Dark money groups **have singled me out for fighting for conservative values** and will be spending millions in our district.
* Last cycle, **the Left spent $4.4 million attacking me** , and with President Trump in the White House, you can bet they’re ready to do so again!
* My Democrat opponent is now standing with Iran over Israel and Los Angeles radicals over ICE officials.

---
[|
---](https://bryansteil.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5855432ef30dec79be4122095&id=bd31f5caf2&e=e907534017)
| **We have less than 2 weeks left until our end-of-quarter deadline.**
It’s important that we put up a strong showing to keep the Republican majority and fend off the Liberals from controlling the House. The **Left** knows that control of the U.S. House runs through Southeast Wisconsin, and they’ll stop at nothing to take this seat.
I’m ready to fight back and deliver on our conservative vision, but I can’t do it alone.
There are only 2 weeks left to make an impact and fend off additional left-wing dark money, but with your help, we can **REACH OUR END-OF-QUARTER GOAL** and keep control of the U.S. House!
---

| **On Wisconsin,** Bryan Steil

| | Team, events in Mississippi should give us all pause and show us why we need to fight in every state nationwide. **Mississippi has an egregious voting rights record** and has the highest level of felony disenfranchisement in the country. A Jim Crow-era constitutional provision enables Mississippi to disenfranchise more than 10% of its total voting-age population and _its disenfranchisement of Black voters exceeds 15%_. Mississippi has no early voting opportunities and a narrow set of qualifications for those seeking to vote absentee, meaning that the vast majority of ballots are cast on Election Day. **This inaccessibility contributes to the state’s notoriously dismal voter turnout:** In 2024’s primary election, only 30% of those registered to vote turned out, the lowest rate since 2007. This is nothing new. On Election Day 2023, **at least nine polling locations in the state’s most populous county** — _Hinds County, home of the city of Jackson_ — **ran out of ballots multiple times throughout the day**. The fight to extend polling hours became a confusing jumble of legal filings and contradicting orders all as voters waited in line -- _and many left entirely due to the lack of ballots_. Also in Hinds County, opponents of voting rights in the state legislature have previously taken aim at Jackson and its over 80% Black population by **creating a new court system with an unelected judge** in a portion of Jackson and expanding the state-run Capitol Police force’s jurisdiction to cover the entire city rather than just a handful of government buildings. Mississippi's playbook is clear: Disenfranchise, limit ballot access, obscure legal pathways for justice, rely on Jim Crow laws still on the books, and codify control for decades. **Their endgame is power and control** , not fairness or freedom. [**Team: Mississippi's voters deserve a real democracy. Stand up to make every vote count and every voice heard in Mississippi and beyond with $25 or whatever you can today >>**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109879409/552550647/1723810624?am=25.00&contactdata=7nRalHsT+daHMDQaSl2Z5wOz6JunIYpQ/GV3B/iuC7uwZ8tl+W7NBbpNfZqemBBhA+01UwCu1XsHwvOERqK0LygApVF1r1LF588t5Y10np8=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjkzOThhOTBhLTFmNGQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=dkSDE-UV1wM0pkZziQYcel6BN-Ltu7yJKOEq-3tPyIc=&emci=068e1aea-1a4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=9398a90a-1f4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)

Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.

Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities. [**Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109879444/552550684/-1704790427?am=20.00&contactdata=7nRalHsT+daHMDQaSl2Z5wOz6JunIYpQ/GV3B/iuC7uwZ8tl+W7NBbpNfZqemBBhA+01UwCu1XsHwvOERqK0LygApVF1r1LF588t5Y10np8=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg5MjE1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjkzOThhOTBhLTFmNGQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=dkSDE-UV1wM0pkZziQYcel6BN-Ltu7yJKOEq-3tPyIc=&emci=068e1aea-1a4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=9398a90a-1f4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=33603084)

name: Becca Balint
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/81342/images/BalintYellowCircle.png]
Peter, today on Juneteenth, we commemorate the day in 1865 on which the final enslaved Americans in the confederate South learned they were free.

Today is only our country's fifth year recognizing Juneteenth as a federal holiday. It took more than 150 years for America to formally celebrate something as massive as the abolition of slavery — which speaks volumes about the work we have ahead of us.

American history is often told as a simple story of continuous progress. We learn that the Emancipation Proclamation and the 13th Amendment freed slaves and set the country on a path towards equality. But now more than ever, we know it’s not nearly as simple as that.

The legacy of slavery in America has been horrific, enduring, and damaging. Following the abolition of slavery came Jim Crow, segregation, and discrimination. Today, the inequities that Black Americans experience in housing, health care, and education are a direct consequence of the racism that was written into America’s laws and policies in the century following the abolition of slavery.

There is so much work ahead of us to take responsibility for our racist history, repair the harm caused by it, and dismantle racist systems that continue to negatively impact Black Americans.

Today, I will be celebrating Juneteenth by reflecting on what I can do to leverage my own privilege and power to challenge racist systems, uplift the needs and voices of Black Vermonters, and continue making progress – real progress – towards our vision of liberty and justice for all.

Have a joyous Juneteenth, Peter.

Becca Balint

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rab-em-june2025]
beccabalintvt@beccabalint.com [beccabalintvt@beccabalint.com]
Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont
P.O. Box 291
Burlington, VT 05402

name: Tom Fitton
subject: We Don’t Believe in “Just Moving On”!
body:
At Judicial Watch, we don’t play the Washington game of “just move on.” Instead, we hold government officials accountable to the American people under the law for their actions, no matter how long it takes!

|
| |
---
|
---
|
---
| Dear Fellow American,

At Judicial Watch, we don't play the Washington game of "just move on."
Instead, we hold government officials accountable to the American people under the law for their actions, _no matter how long it takes_!
**[And we cannot let up now...and that’s why I am asking you to join with us and make a contribution to help us today.](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=74753&L=35348&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249TWF5JnV0bV90ZXJtPU5vbi1Eb25vcnM=&hash=6b7d730242d55e52186759e44e828acf66566736e1bae6f943796970e61717b5)**
| [Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=74753&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)
---

_We dig in for the long haul_ , and use the courts and the legal system to get to the facts that the American people have a right to know, even when the government stonewalls us and the media looks the other way...

* Thats why we _just sued_ the Defense Department for all records regarding U.S. military personnel possibly contracting Covid-19 during the World Military Games in Wuhan, China, in October 2019. _We have done more to inform Americans about the source of the pandemic and our government 's failures in responding to it than any group in America._

And we are fighting on many other fronts for the rule of law, and government transparency and accountability!

[Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=74753&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)
---

* _We recently sued_ the **Department of Homeland Security** for records of the **Federal Emergency Management Agency** (FEMA) payments to New York City for migrant housing... _apparently $59 MILLION for luxury hotels_! We hold _every administration_ accountable for its actions, and we urge the Trump administration to prioritize transparency about this potentially illegal misuse of government funds!
* _We are in court against three states_ challenging them to follow federal law and clean their voter rolls to help ensure honest elections, and we are preparing to appear before the U.S. Supreme Court in our historic challenge to **Illinois '** law "extending" Election Day by up to 14 days.

* And we _keep fighting_ in the courts against reparations and other ugly and unconstitutional woke programs.

The financial pressures on us are enormous and federal and state bureaucrats continue to stonewall our lawful open records requests...and that is [why I ask you to join with us today.](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=74753&L=35348&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTY/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PUFNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRCZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249TWF5JnV0bV90ZXJtPU5vbi1Eb25vcnM=&hash=6b7d730242d55e52186759e44e828acf66566736e1bae6f943796970e61717b5)

[Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=74753&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPU1heSZ1dG1fdGVybT1Ob24tRG9ub3Jz&hash=aafc90fdace25a5a1871d30fa9a66413e29290f69056683c8a490d5fbaf2a4d2)
---

I look forward to welcoming you to the Judicial Watch family of supporters! Thank you!

Sincerely,

Thomas Fitton
President

|

clean_body: Peter, this is Steve Scalise. I need you to read this immediately. Bernie Sanders is holding a SOCIALIST MEGA RALLY in Monica De La Cruz's district this Friday! is a true patriot, born and raised in South Texas. She's a proud America First Hispanic conservative who's not afraid to take on the liberal elites. And she's got a proven track record of winning: In 2022, she flipped Texas-15, a Democrat stronghold, and has held onto it since. But the Democrats have already unleashed up to $50 million against our vulnerable conservatives, including Monica. And now, Bernie's coming for her. That's why I'm asking you to help support Monica's race today. We need to send a message to Bernie and the Democrats that we won't let them take Texas-15 OR our House Majority. dollar counts, and every donation will go directly to supporting Monica's campaign. I truly appreciate everything you do, Peter. For America,- Steve STEVE SCALISEHouse Majority Leader * Democrats already pledged up to FIFTY MILLION dollars to wipe me out for being a MAGA Latina in a purple district... And that was all BEFORE I was added to this new target list! There's still time to right the ship as long as we move right now. - Monica De La Cruz| | | | | Paid for by Monica for Congress PO Box 4605 McAllen, Texas 78502 |

body:
You probably haven't gotten a message this honest in months. This is Majority Leader Steve Scalise. I took a lot of time out of my day to write this email - and all I'm asking for is two minutes out of yours to read it. I'm not going to guilt you into helping me hit my fundraising goal. I'm not going to shame you if you can't afford to give. I'm not going to cast doubt on your party loyalty. I'm going to give you a hard dose of reality: I know you were a MASSIVE part in helping us elect President Trump, saving the House, and winning the Senate. There are no words to describe how grateful I am for that. But our work didn't stop and end there. Right now, our House Majority is public enemy #1... Because WE are the front line of the Trump agenda. WE are the firewall preventing House Democrats from impeaching, investigating, and sabotaging President Trump. But WE are also the most vulnerable part of the Republican Trifecta. The Democrats know that. And they're FULLY COMMITTED to destroying each and every one of us. They're rolling out the red carpet to Liberal activists who infiltrate our town halls, spread Fake News lies about our most vulnerable Members, and can whip out their credit cards to donate at the drop of a hat. DON'T FORGET: We have one of the slimmest Majorities in a CENTURY and just a handful of seats is all that stands in the way of House Democrats taking control and holding the MAGA agenda hostage in the House. DON'T FORGET: Last year, the Democrat Machine spent over a BILLION dollars to defeat us and already has the Liberal Media AND the woke big businesses in their corner. They've raised $45,000,000.00 already - and they're just getting started. DON'T FORGET: We know what it will look like if the House is back in Democrats' hands - more sham impeachments, a complete blockade of the Trump agenda, forcing through votes on Liberal pet projects, and endless investigations of President Trump and his allies. I don't tell you any of this lightly. [But that’s our reality if we don’t do a COMPLETE 180 on the fundraising front. So as your House Majority Leader, I’m personally asking you to step up and chip in just a few bucks by midnight tonight to save our Pro-Trump House Majority.](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/FoeEIFiFkfcSe_F_q5oBzgwkAiU0NWsX-qibFAEgNRsUuBqh0TD2PUPxlmfEprX19R1IwEg226WsQOxKsKpjmUJQvcfo_m4A7glL3niG57cjFk4u3hj5dOCrf3CzU6k3EipIflwzDAoCGWnjt_4Tp7FwTTKxu4fjqjD1vuYi4rLiJuvhOdFGzkAcsn0OUwPt2yfxGtLDQ1CgVagEze3lCOqHFfKG40LkszBzddmfm36aMl5GApVSRfZMKdaAMcuOQQlT11ZdsilqcGrfn4B_BJjqkh_ezONy5c8MwEqt4wzvuZeGwfOMLwz1oEbRKoD-WaTRZ7N0mSvT-BAHfmDI6OY30NokW_x4WJ-QuCE82o7Qqg8cZiAmdoBQ79AS5OMyEADi53TqSIJjtzl0sdOeQbo6iTmvn7jstR2QSqwLUqLBeb4UBskB0MftHnS28I0YAKX-UTd_-rlFn0ixFNWBfQW75RDPhGNAXPUp2FUYEM6fnrGVPfibXOn-e7PXTBSfRVPUzBG_JxAQu52pvOr5VJwRqKcTOBZA1Jd3I7ur25CeLcoZ2l9jpOlqPrlSEt5oNfysfhFNsIEXiuvosdxKZZ-PfNgsmBdy5k4zWkVsCiKDl1uJCSmCdqv6wsJBMDSs16ov7lVFx4NG9Ur1-L_7lO-Z9DvUEawQk45a6vUoXEYEBz9wW80wpIImA-SdlCS8pzu1RUd-ITmur5q2-6oj0_0jxH1oCbCnOcqCiuq_uhZMBLh2Bz1YyX_q9ax5D2PwXo1WeS-VIDFUeQ3FixHQyfbAYJDIgl4xGReUi-bktHUPmSvspGHMG3eggxAEui6DeE-Eroi7Tw/RKujApGSGZusL1c9pX-gRyawZAVLZNJM/10) Look, I know your inbox is probably flooded right now. I'm not going to shame you if you can't afford to give. I'm not going to cast doubt on your party loyalty. I'm going to give you a hard dose of reality: I know you were a MASSIVE part in helping us elect President Trump, saving the House, and winning the Senate. There are no words to describe how grateful I am for that. But our work didn't stop and end there. Right now, our House Majority is public enemy #1... Because WE are the front line of the Trump agenda. WE are the firewall preventing House Democrats from impeaching, investigating, and sabotaging President Trump. But WE are also the most vulnerable part of the Republican Trifecta. The Democrats know that. And they're FULLY COMMITTED to destroying each and every one of us. They're rolling out the red carpet to Liberal activists who infiltrate our town halls, spread Fake News lies about our most vulnerable Members, and can whip out their credit cards to donate at the drop of a hat. DON'T FORGET: We have one of the slimmest Majorities in a CENTURY and just a handful of seats is all that stands in the way of House Democrats taking control and holding the MAGA agenda hostage in the House. DON'T FORGET: Last year, the Democrat Machine spent over a BILLION dollars to defeat us and already has the Liberal Media AND the woke big businesses in their corner. They've raised $45,000,000.00 already - and they're just getting started. DON'T FORGET: We know what it will look like if the House is back in Democrats' hands - more sham impeachments, a complete blockade of the Trump agenda, forcing through votes on Liberal pet projects, and endless investigations of President Trump and his allies. I don't tell you any of this lightly. Look, I know your inbox is probably flooded right now. But I'm 100% confident when I say that no one else is sending you something like this. So please (and ONLY if you can): We only have one shot at saving the House. And if we don't get started now, we may never get that shot again. We can't keep kicking the can down the road. name: Tim
subject: Honoring Melissa and Mark Hortman
disclaimer_text: paid for by Tim Walz for Governor
body:
Peter, our state suffered an unspeakable loss on Saturday. Speaker Emerita Melissa Hortman and her husband, Mark, were murdered in a politically motivated assassination. Senator John Hoffman and his wife, Yvette, were also shot and are now recovering from life-threatening injuries.
|
---
Peter,
Our state suffered an unspeakable loss on Saturday. Speaker Emerita Melissa Hortman and her husband, Mark, were murdered in a politically motivated assassination. Senator John Hoffman and his wife, Yvette, were also shot and are now recovering from life-threatening injuries.
Minnesota has lost an irreplaceable leader, and I lost a friend.
Melissa served the people of Minnesota with grace, compassion, humor, and a fierce sense of duty. She shaped the core of who we are as a state and will be remembered as the most consequential Speaker in Minnesota’s history.
Under her leadership, Minnesota secured free universal school meals, codified reproductive freedoms, implemented gun safety measures, enacted public safety reforms, and set ambitious clean energy goals. Her impact will be felt for generations to come.
But Melissa was more than a brilliant public servant. She was a dear family member and friend to many, a colleague cherished by those on both sides of the aisle, and a dedicated volunteer who trained service dogs for veterans.
Her deep care for others was evident in everything she did—from devoting her life to service to her habit of showing up with enough snacks to share. She will be missed by so many.
As a country, we cannot become numb to this violence. The way we move forward and solve the problems facing our nation is not through hate or violence, but through humility, grace, and compassion.
As we heal, we will not let fear win. We must move forward in Melissa’s honor with understanding, service, and above all, a sense of one another’s humanity.
I urge you to take to heart the powerful words of Melissa and Mark’s children, Sophie and Colin Hortman: “The best way to honor our parents’ memory is to do something, whether big or small, to make our community just a little better for someone else.”
May each of us take up that call so together, we can carry Melissa and Mark’s legacy forward.
Tim Walz
** **
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/8kDyG_KYfEGJozZtWQy_Ow2?unsubscribedata=LAyzlTlkcDAgFo4le8nIxqcoc%2FADyJAhYnhFP%2BWT3uQeGDlc9muvf%2FMpYU%2BReV0%2FServ5T43V%2BlF22wGmES5xrtVX483xjjYksaF1cVdHDKk7Ii5auRD%2BBVkdM9AK6jH9OSUZMH%2FprQWvG8NrMM%2FaIJYRlB1CRISV2bpDRhWu0k3JdIYRe8A430cWgMEoX6%2BWSgW1olNLxVv1M4nRF3n9Ak1Ey83MLjcFqzwKfknXTKgo4aP5x4sF8dTTeZiGSsCRyxCnttrbHb42Cz8w03%2FjlhcRjn7fjM0kER1L3RGm5wLe3pkBm60kJLLlXMTIFr8LSaPFMWE4L9YQ8CObyBq0TY17UzOZXGqwk9CkOudEdChWKdyDQJnpA5RJpg5N8P3j5lBmuKRhH4%2FlFRpQYtgutnj8%2FX5qXDv07fYmgH2Zq16nf9CNK7xfvMrndlaNM3L). | Prepared and paid for by Tim Walz for Governor

name: Letitia James
subject: Reflecting on this Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by James for NY 2026
body:
Why I'm still fighting
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109880407/552553606/-2022258813?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk2000-ot-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTg0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwMjBhOWJkZi0yMDRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_R-1Focm_Qp_VMNitK-7u7VCa-7015KEIbftoBYhc2U=)
---
Peter, on this day in 1865, news of emancipation finally reached the enslaved people of Galveston, Texas – more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation took effect.
Juneteenth marks that moment: a delayed but powerful announcement of freedom, and the beginning of a long and unfinished journey toward equality in America.
What followed was a period of Reconstruction, when newly freed Black Americans made incredible strides – holding office, building communities, and laying the foundation for future generations to fight for justice under law.
As New York's first Black woman attorney general, I carry that legacy with me every day. It's what inspires me to keep going.
As a child, I saw the Civil Rights movement take root in New York – in protests, in neighborhoods, in the voices of my family and community demanding better. Better wages, better schools, safer housing, and the freedom to live and thrive without fear.
And today, we're still fighting many of those same battles.
**We've made hard-fought progress – but the work is far from over. Across the country, attacks on our rights are intensifying: from attempts to consolidate unchecked power, to efforts to upend our voting rights and blatant attacks on our Constitution.**
That's why I'm committed to continuing this work. Because true freedom means more than just legal equality – it means justice in our courts, dignity in our workplaces, safety in our communities, and a safeguarded democracy.
This Juneteenth, I'm reflecting on the strength of those who came before us and recommitting myself to the fight for justice in New York and for this country we all call home. And I know that with fearless fighters like you with me, we'll keep on pushing forward until we achieve true freedom and equality for all.
Thank you for standing with me, and happy Juneteenth.
Letitia James
| With so many tough and crucial fights ahead, I am counting on people like you to fuel my fight for justice, progress, and true equality. Will you make a donation now or contribute monthly to make sure I can continue using the full power of the Attorney General's office to make a difference for everyday people and families?

subject: In honor of Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS
body:
Today, we celebrate the resilience and strength of Black Americans.

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109880899/552555141/-1882479441?refcode=LOC_EM_NOFR_2025.06.19_Juneteenth_B1_F1_S1_C1_AC_N-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYwODgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiM2Y3NTc1NDMtMjI0ZC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Vvll7-3sz7uivuK8qlZ4IzNb7ALIalSyUfmdEj5OKKs=)
---
Happy Juneteenth. Today, we celebrate the resilience and strength of Black Americans. On this day in 1865, the 250,000 enslaved people living in Texas were finally free, officially marking the end of slavery in the United States. All these years later, we continue to fight for civil rights, equality, and human dignity. Frankly, under this current administration, it sometimes seems insurmountable. But we cannot give up. I will not give up. Together, we have to stand up against the extremism plaguing our politics today, and every day. — Marlene

clean_body: Today, we celebrate the resilience and strength of Black Americans. | | = Happy Juneteenth. Today, we celebrate the resilience and strength of Black Americans. On this day in 1865, the 250,000 enslaved people living in Texas were finally free, officially marking the end of slavery in the United States. All these years later, we continue to fight for civil rights, equality, and human dignity. Frankly, under this current administration, it sometimes seems insurmountable. But we cannot give up. I will not give up. Together, we have to stand up against the extremism plaguing our politics today, and every day. — Marlene | To learn more about Marlene, visit: We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to PAID FOR BY MARLENE FOR CONGRESS Marlene for Congress 7904 E. Chaparral Road, Suite A110-472 Scottsdale, AZ 85250 United States

body:
| | | Hi, Adam Schiff here. It's my honor to serve as California's newest Senator and one of your DSCC Vice Chairs, fighting to flip the Senate blue.

**[In a moment, I'm going to tell you why we _must_ take back the Senate majority to stop Trump's agenda. But first, I need to ask: Will you donate $5 to the DSCC to make it happen?](https://go.dscc.org/go/21633?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20250619-AdamSchiff-VCSchiffTrumpFRRF-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20250619-AdamSchiff-VCSchiffTrumpFRRF-Pros-sl1&akid=20556%2E9716520%2EAS8dzT)**

Let me make this as plain as possible: **Donald Trump isn't fit to be president.**

We see it in his actions every day. Pardoning January 6th criminals on his first day in office, trying to cut Medicaid to pay for massive tax cuts for the wealthiest Americans, attempting to end birthright citizenship, turning his back on our allies -- **if you invented a fictional politician hellbent on harming our country, they would look exactly like Donald Trump.**

But how have Senate Republicans responded to this shocking display? With shrugs, and -- more importantly -- _votes_ to let Donald Trump have his way with our government.

**And that is exactly why we _must_ take back the Senate: Republicans will _never_ stand up to Trump, so if _anyone_ is going to stop this madness and hold him accountable, the responsibility falls to Democrats like us.**

**[DSCC leadership, myself included, is counting on this team to raise $50,000 by midnight to keep our plan to flip the Senate on track._Nothing_ could be more important in these early days. name: Wes Moore
subject: Juneteenth
body:
Juneteenth is a day to honor our communities and celebrate how far we’ve come, but there is always more work to be done.
# [](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/8?t=1&akid=196%2E1086043%2E9yiUhV)
Today is Juneteenth and – more so than on any Juneteenth that came before it – it’s imperative to reflect on our nation’s flawed history.
Juneteenth is in many ways the second Independence Day of America. Though the Emancipation Proclamation brought freedom to enslaved Black people on January 1st, 1863, it took **more than two years** for the news to reach the west.
It was on this day in 1865 that Union troops arrived in Galveston, Texas to share that the more than 250,000 enslaved Black people in the state were free.
Peter, Juneteenth serves as a reminder that our work continues.
It’s not just about one day. It’s not even about one month. It’s about the ongoing movement for racial equality.
I ran for governor to close the racial wealth gap that has plagued our state for far too long. By fighting child poverty, getting more people access to work, wages, and wealth, and fighting for environmental justice, I know that we can solve some of Maryland's greatest issues.
I’m also proud to have made history by making the largest single mass pardon by a state in this country for cannabis possession. Removing barriers for thousands of Marylanders to everything from employment to education to the ability to buy a home.
But my work is not done. And ours isn’t either.
Loving your country doesn’t mean lying about its history. True patriotism is knowing that we can always be better.
The Bible says, “Faith without work is dead.” And our experiment endures when we put in the work.
**So, this Juneteenth, let’s honor and celebrate those that have come before us and recommit to our fight for a better future.**
Elevate,
Wes

* * *
