clean_body: Juneteenth is a day to honor our communities and celebrate how far we’ve come, but there is always more work to be done. Today is Juneteenth and – more so than on any Juneteenth that came before it – it’s imperative to reflect on our nation’s flawed history. Juneteenth is in many ways the second Independence Day of America. Though the Emancipation Proclamation brought freedom to enslaved Black people on January 1st, 1863, it took more than two years for the news to reach the west. It was on this day in 1865 that Union troops arrived in Galveston, Texas to share that the more than 250,000 enslaved Black people in the state were free. Peter, Juneteenth serves as a reminder that our work continues. It’s not just about one day. It’s not even about one month. It’s about the ongoing movement for racial equality. I ran for governor to close the racial wealth gap that has plagued our state for far too long. By fighting child poverty, getting more people access to work, wages, and wealth, and fighting for environmental justice, I know that we can solve some of Maryland's greatest issues. I’m also proud to have made history by making the largest single mass pardon by a state in this country for cannabis possession. Removing barriers for thousands of Marylanders to everything from employment to education to the ability to buy a home. But my work is not done. And ours isn’t either. Loving your country doesn’t mean lying about its history. True patriotism is knowing that we can always be better. The Bible says, “Faith without work is dead.” And our experiment endures when we put in the work. So, this Juneteenth, let’s honor and celebrate those that have come before us and recommit to our fight for a better future. Elevate, Wes

name: Jena Griswold
subject: The work isn’t finished
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://jenaforcolorado.com/ [https://jenaforcolorado.com/]
Friends -
Today, on Juneteenth, we honor a defining moment in our nation’s history — the day in 1865 when the last enslaved Black Americans in Galveston, Texas finally learned they were free. More than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation, that message of freedom finally arrived.
Juneteenth is a day of celebration, but also a reminder: justice delayed is justice denied. And while we commemorate freedom, we must also recommit ourselves to the fight for racial equity and justice that continues to this day.
As Colorado’s Secretary of State, and now as a candidate for Attorney General, I’ve always believed that government must be a force for fairness, dignity, and equal rights. That means protecting voting access, defending civil rights, holding systems accountable, and standing up to those who would roll back progress.
On this day of remembrance and reflection, I’m holding close the stories, the sacrifices, and the strength of generations who fought for freedom — and I’m renewing my commitment to keep fighting for justice, every single day.
Thank you,
Jena Griswold

clean_body: [] Friends - Today, on Juneteenth, we honor a defining moment in our nation’s history — the day in 1865 when the last enslaved Black Americans in Galveston, Texas finally learned they were free. More than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation, that message of freedom finally arrived. Juneteenth is a day of celebration, but also a reminder: justice delayed is justice denied. And while we commemorate freedom, we must also recommit ourselves to the fight for racial equity and justice that continues to this day. As Colorado’s Secretary of State, and now as a candidate for Attorney General, I’ve always believed that government must be a force for fairness, dignity, and equal rights. That means protecting voting access, defending civil rights, holding systems accountable, and standing up to those who would roll back progress. On this day of remembrance and reflection, I’m holding close the stories, the sacrifices, and the strength of generations who fought for freedom — and I’m renewing my commitment to keep fighting for justice, every single day. Thank you, Jena Griswold Follow Jena Online: [] [] [] [] [] Paid for by Jena for Colorado. Rachel Gordon, Registered Agent. Jena for Colorado P.O. name: PA Dems
subject: Juneteenth isn’t just a history lesson
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party
body:
| |
---
Peter,

Juneteenth marks the day — June 19, 1865 — when enslaved Black Americans in Galveston, Texas were finally informed that they were free. More than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation.

That’s the story of Juneteenth. Freedom announced, but not delivered. Promised, but delayed. And that delay was no accident. It was the system doing exactly what it was built to do: protect power, protect capital, and keep Black people waiting.

160 years later, we’re still being told to wait.

Wait for voting rights.
Wait for Justice.
Wait for schools to not be made to erase history.
Wait for air we can breathe, water we can drink, lives we can live freely.

But we’re not waiting anymore. Because Juneteenth doesn’t just mark the end of slavery — it reminds us that the struggle didn’t end in 1865. It continued through Jim Crow, through redlining, through voter suppression, through mass incarceration, through every policy and power structure that tries to silence us today.

So, we’re not here to whitewash this history with platitudes. Juneteenth is a celebration, yes. But it’s also a call to action. Because freedom here has never been handed over. It’s been fought for, taken, over and over again.

And every time they tell us to quiet down, to be patient, to wait our turn — we remember the generations who never had the luxury of waiting.

So yes. Today is a celebration. One of resilience, of survival, of community. Today is also a moment to recommit. To organizing. To telling the truth. To building power that serves all of us, not just the privileged few.

Not just on Juneteenth. But for every day forward.

In remembrance, resistance, and resolve,

— PA Dems
Paid for by The Pennsylvania Democratic Party. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate committee. www.padems.com
---
PA Democratic Party
510 N. Third Street
Harrisburg, PA 17101
United States

name: Indiana GOP
subject: Indiana GOP Call to Convention
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE INDIANA REPUBLICAN STATE COMMITTEE
body:
<>

2026 Convention Official Call

June 19, 2025

TO ALL INDIANA REPUBLICANS:

In compliance with the provisions of the Indiana Election Code and pursuant to
the Rules of the Indiana Republican Party, adopted by the Indiana Republican
State Central Committee, and pursuant to the resolutions of said Committee, a
State Convention will be held on Saturday, June 20, 2026, for the purpose of
nominating a candidate for each of the following

offices:

Secretary of State

Treasurer of State

State Comptroller (Auditor of State)

All matters pertaining to the holding of a State Convention not provided for
by law shall be controlled by the Rules of the Indiana Republican State Central
Committee and by such rules as may be adopted by the convention.

THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF THE STATE OF INDIANA STATE CONVENTION WILL MEET ON
SATURDAY, JUNE 20, 2026, AT THE GRAND WAYNE CONVENTION CENTER 120 W. JEFFERSON
BLVD., FORT WAYNE, IN 46802. THE MEETING WILL CONVENE AT 10:00 A.M. E.T. ON THE
CONVENTION FLOOR.

name: Janelle Bynum
subject: This Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Janelle Bynum for Congress
body:
Today, I’m reflecting on how far our fight for Black freedom and equality has come, and how much work we still have to do.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882373/552560658/-629675555?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmZjNmZiOTUtMjY0ZC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=WAeyAle9VIkl-csHYfiWvLqTGxGeeG3brfpbQ5S80m0=) Friend, This Juneteenth marks 160 years since the news of the Emancipation Proclamation — which ended slavery in the United States — finally reached the last group of enslaved African Americans in Galveston, Texas. Today, I’m reflecting on how far our fight for Black freedom and equality has come, and how much work we still have to do if we want to build a more just, more equitable world for the next generation. When my mom encouraged me to run for Congress, she said, “If your generation doesn't stand up and fight, we stand to lose the progress my generation worked for.” And she was right. Now, I’m proud to be serving as the first Black U.S. Representative from Oregon, and fighting everyday in Congress against the current attacks on our freedoms. So today, I hope you’ll join me in celebrating how far we’ve come in our fight for justice and equality. And tomorrow, we will continue the fight. Thank you, Janelle | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882378/552560668/-629675555?refcode=email_061925_Juneteenth&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY3MjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNmZjNmZiOTUtMjY0ZC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=WAeyAle9VIkl-csHYfiWvLqTGxGeeG3brfpbQ5S80m0=)

body:

# ACROSS THE COMMONWEALTH
---
| | |
---
| | **OUR VIRGINIA DEMOCRATIC NOMINEES!** Congratulations to our Democratic nominees, Abigail Spanberger, Ghazala Hashmi, Jay Jones, and HD-40’s Donna Littlepage. We have a team that is ready to meet this moment - on to November! Election Day is November 4th, early voting begins September 19th.
---
|
---
[ **Election Results** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882351/552560639/1806916365?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)

| |

**Read More**
---
| **NATION-WIDE NO KINGS PROTEST** Last weekend, Americans protested the devastating acts of the Trump Administration in Roanoke, as well as all over the country. We are a nation built on People Power. We reject tyranny and stand with those fighting for a better tomorrow. #NoKings
---
| | |
---
# 2025 ELECTION
---
|
---
---
Don't get complacent, come out and canvass! There will be an informative discussion on how you can get involved in the campaign, and an opportunity to knock doors. Whether you're new to volunteering or an experienced activist, there are plenty of ways to help Sam win this election! East Gate Park is located at 1620 North Ave NE, Roanoke, VA 24012.
---
[ **Read More** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882352/552560640/-62267326?acontext=%7B%22event_action_history%22%3A\[%7B%22surface%22%3A%22home%22%7D%2C%7B%22mechanism%22%3A%22attachment%22%2C%22surface%22%3A%22newsfeed%22%7D\]%2C%22ref_notif_type%22%3Anull%7D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)
---
---
Please join the campaigns of Lily Franklin and Abigail Spanberger to knock on doors in Roanoke County! They're starting early to prove that they mean business and that they're in it to win it! First time canvassing? Don't worry, they'll provide all the training and cheerleading you need. Thank you so much for all of your help and support. Let's win this thing!
---
[ **Read More** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882353/552560641/-1639676393?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)

| | |
---
# IN OUR COMMUNITY
---
|
---
| |
---
**Lucy Addison Receives Virginia Landmark**
---
Roanoke’s Lucy Addison Middle School, which during the days of segregation was the city’s Black high school, has been named as one of nine new additions to the Virginia Landmarks Register. The school was built in 1928 and named for the pioneering Black educator Lucy Addison. The school was a major investment in Black education and became a model in Virginia for its high school programs for African American students.
---
[ **Read More** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882354/552560642/-252534982?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)
---
|
---
**Tim Kaine Office Hours Roanoke - June 23**
---
Join U.S. Senator Tim Kaine's team on Monday, June 23, at the South County Library in Roanoke from 10:30 am to 2 pm for “Kaine Connects”! From helping iron out an issue with a Social Security disability claim, to directing you toward more information about affordable housing resources, his office will be there to help.
---
[ **Read More** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882356/552560644/1857095221?fbclid=IwY2xjawK_9gxleHRuA2FlbQIxMQBicmlkETFwY3BMdGFkbHBJRzdPd0tsAR5sSz5_nnjwmqILlMh6Mnm2eWwrzuNwrsEyseEHxL2a1imlJAJnvCa9te4HCQ_aem_zwEMCdkYqOPV3LJ1R5VRPQ&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)
---
| |
---
**Hello Summer Festival****-**********June 20******
---
Get ready to celebrate the official start of summer at the Hello Summer Festival at Green Hill Park! Kick off your weekend with live music, delicious food and drinks, and fun activities for the whole family. Don't miss the highlight of the evening: our region's very first drone show, taking off at 9:30pm!
---
[ **Read More
**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882358/552560646/-1600181974?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)

|

**Spring Into Action 5k For Refugees - June****21****
---
In honor of refugees in our communities and World Refugee Day, run or walk a 5K in person or virtually to support refugees in the Spring Into Action 5K Run/Walk. Location: Rivers Edge North
Start time: 8 am
On site Registration start: 7 am
Award ceremony: 8:45 am
---
[ **Read More** ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882360/552560648/375947768?fbclid=IwY2xjawK9hrNleHRuA2FlbQIxMQBicmlkETFyNERRWHY5Z0xQWVdPUFprAR5ncRnb6iJeivE1hhwX9XXuho5urny1zqccmMT8LuhZzrCZQoGsOl6YzMUxQA_aem_XpxStxi5FI1lL-HG6NRZGA&ref=roanokerambler.com&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)

| |

**DOPE URBAN Walk - Juneteenth Edition****\- June 21**
---
Freedom is a walk worth taking. Join community members for an inspiring and reflective 4 mile guided stroll through Roanoke’s Black history, art, and legacy. Where: Downtown Roanoke & Historic Gainsboro
When: Saturday, June 21st at 9:00 am
Cost: $8.65 per person | Kids 12 & under walk free!
---
[ **Read More
**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882362/552560650/1441530523?acontext=%257B%2522event_action_history%2522%253A\[%257B%2522mechanism%2522%253A%2522discovery_local_tab%2522%252C%2522surface%2522%253A%2522bookmark%2522%257D\]%252C%2522ref_notif_type%2522%253Anull%257D&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzE1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMjI2NzM3My0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=JTHxtn_erktxwMirymeDaVna9uVQwwLcO7dabW93VAY=&emci=32dc1298-e94b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=22267373-264d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=18273010)

**Roanoke Pride March****-**** **June 21****
---
Join the Roanoke Diversity Center for the 2025 Roanoke Pride March this Saturday at 4 pm at 706 S. Jefferson St! This is an annual community-led demonstration of visibility, resilience, and joy. Whether you’ve marched before or this is your first time, your presence matters. Pride is protest. Pride is celebration. Pride is solidarity.

# RCPS SUMMER MEAL SERVICE
---
Roanoke City Public Schools will offer free summer meals to children ages 18 years or younger through the USDA's Summer Food Service Program (SFSP). Meals are provided on-site on a first-come, first-served basis at school locations. # RCPS UPDATED CELL PHONE POLICY
---
RCPS has updated its guidelines for high school students’ cell phones and wearable electronic devices. Starting next week with their summer programs and continuing into the 2025-2026 school year, all students in grades PK–12 will be expected to keep devices off and away for the entire school day. Learn more: www.rcps.info/cellphones.

Last year, elementary and middle schools did a great job following these expectations. Now, the guidelines will expand to include all grades.

name: Wiley Nickel
subject: Peter, we are not truly free until everyone is.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina**
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882451/552560960/-1960556440?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_NOA_AC_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDg5NzdhOS0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VAZT8xuSNot2sTyi6y3ysoIhz2-xvqsr6q1ygKaxl9I=)
---
Peter, As we gather to honor Juneteenth, I’m reminded of the powerful moment on June 19, 1865, when the last enslaved people in Galveston, Texas, finally heard the news of their freedom. Although the Emancipation Proclamation had been issued two years earlier, it was on this day that the promise of liberty reached every corner of our nation. This fight for freedom is a call to action that resonates profoundly today. In just the first five months of this Trump term, we've faced dramatically increasing efforts to roll back voting rights, erode civil liberties, and deepen systemic inequities. **Juneteenth is a powerful reminder that even after 160 years, our liberties must be defended every single day.** Black communities and vulnerable people nationwide continue to encounter barriers that contradict our nation's founding ideals. We face modern forms of poll taxes, voter suppression laws and tactics, and economic challenges that restrict opportunity. We can’t sit and watch these injustices unfold. Every decision made and action taken this year will echo into our children’s futures. With a pivotal election on the horizon, I urge you to join me in channeling our shared commitment into action. Volunteer your time and talent. Raise your voice at your local town hall. Cast your vote for leaders committed to expanding — not eroding — freedom. Only by working together can we dismantle the obstacles that hold us back. **, wPetere are not truly free until everyone is.** This Juneteenth, let us honor those who came before us by fighting for a more just tomorrow. Let's ensure North Carolina continues moving toward true equality for all. _Here's to our progress, persistence, and the day when every American can unequivocally declare their freedom._ Sincerely, Wiley | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882452/552560961/474294398?refcode=AU_GWN_CL_GEN_NOA_AC_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODMxNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMDg5NzdhOS0yNjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VAZT8xuSNot2sTyi6y3ysoIhz2-xvqsr6q1ygKaxl9I=)

clean_body: | = Peter, As we gather to honor Juneteenth, I’m reminded of the powerful moment on June 19, 1865, when the last enslaved people in Galveston, Texas, finally heard the news of their freedom. Although the Emancipation Proclamation had been issued two years earlier, it was on this day that the promise of liberty reached every corner of our nation. This fight for freedom is a call to action that resonates profoundly today. In just the first five months of this Trump term, we've faced dramatically increasing efforts to roll back voting rights, erode civil liberties, and deepen systemic inequities. Juneteenth is a powerful reminder that even after 160 years, our liberties must be defended every single day. Black communities and vulnerable people nationwide continue to encounter barriers that contradict our nation's founding ideals. We face modern forms of poll taxes, voter suppression laws and tactics, and economic challenges that restrict opportunity. We can’t sit and watch these injustices unfold. Every decision made and action taken this year will echo into our children’s futures. With a pivotal election on the horizon, I urge you to join me in channeling our shared commitment into action. Volunteer your time and talent. Raise your voice at your local town hall. Cast your vote for leaders committed to expanding — not eroding — freedom. Only by working together can we dismantle the obstacles that hold us back. , wPetere are not truly free until everyone is. This Juneteenth, let us honor those who came before us by fighting for a more just tomorrow. Let's ensure North Carolina continues moving toward true equality for all. Here's to our progress, persistence, and the day when every American can unequivocally declare their freedom. Sincerely, Wiley | = Wiley Nickel for North Carolina PO Box 563 Raleigh, NC 27602 United States | mailto:info@wileynickel.com | Paid for by Wiley Nickel for North Carolina

name: Gretchen Whitmer
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fight Like Hell PAC
body:
|
#
---
Team, 159 years ago today, General Gordon Granger issued General Order No. 3 in Galveston, Texas — freeing the last remaining slaves in the United States. Today on Juneteenth, we are reminded that freedom is not given, it's won — and that fight continues today. Voting rights face constant attacks from Republican lawmakers. They are actively wiping Black leaders' stories from government websites and school textbooks. The cost of essentials and childcare for families across the country is only rising. So today, we come together in celebration of the fundamental American values of freedom and equality, both of which are embodied by the legacies and stories of the Black community, and to also **recommit ourselves to action.** In Michigan, we’re committed to ensuring that everyone in our state has the opportunity to succeed and reach their full potential. I’ve made equality a key part of my agenda, and with Michigan Democrats, I’m proud of the steps we’ve taken to fulfill that commitment. I’ve signed multiple budgets to fund the Healthy Moms, Healthy Babies initiative that I proposed in 2020 to reduce the racial impacts of maternal and infant mortality and ensure Black moms have access to affordable and quality healthcare. I’ve also taken action to close racial gaps in healthcare and education. And I signed the bipartisan _CROWN Act_ , which ends race-based discrimination based on hair and ensures that Black Michiganders can be themselves in the workplace and in school. **But there are people in power actively trying to take us in the opposite direction.** To build a state where every Michigander can thrive and a country where everyone has equality, it’s up to us to pursue unity and continue getting things done that make a difference in people’s lives. This fight is far from over. Together, we must continue uplifting the Black community and fighting like hell alongside them to achieve true equality and freedom. Thank you, and happy Juneteenth! — Gretchen | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109882803/552562118/-1809995021?amount=&refcode=AU_FLHP_CL_GEN_NOA_AC90_20250619_PM1_V1_S2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDY0NjIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjcyMDNiYzItMjY0ZC1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=Gwwt2XsIONpXbragtwn2D5UHCeCzx27Di9-XWkUqDho=)

DID YOU SEE THIS? **Trump just blew up to $45 million on a military parade for his birthday.** Yes, you read that right, Peter. Donald Trump just used your tax dollars to throw a vanity military parade in Washington, D.C., with tanks and troops on full display — **all so he could play tough guy for the cameras.** And while he put on a show, active-duty Marines and National Guard troops were stationed in an **_American city_** aimed at **_American citizens._** He used the military as a prop and a weapon. This is an attack on our American values. The military serves the American people, not the authoritarian ambitions of Donald Trump. This country deserves better. And the people of Georgia deserve better. [But we want to hear from you. clean_body: $45 million to cosplay as a tough guy. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | = DID YOU SEE THIS? Trump just blew up to $45 million on a military parade for his birthday. Yes, you read that right, Peter. Donald Trump just used your tax dollars to throw a vanity military parade in Washington, D.C., with tanks and troops on full display — all so he could play tough guy for the cameras. And while he put on a show, active-duty Marines and National Guard troops were stationed in an American city aimed at American citizens. He used the military as a prop and a weapon. But I know I can't do this all on my own; it's going to take all of us, and I really meanall of us , to win. If you can't afford to send in even just $17, I'll understand. That's right - all I 'm asking is that you send in $17 or more to help me catch up with my Democrat opponent's LUDICROUS fundraising numbers! Look, this race is TIED. There's ONLY one thing holding me and my team back from winning right now; my liberal opponent's crazy cash advantage. Thank you for being a crucial part of my team, Friend. I can't tell you how much I appreciate your support! -Winsome Earle-Sears Mother Marine Lt. | [View in Web Browser](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8kNFuwiAUhp8G7mzgAK1ecDE1ddkyY3TZ9BKhKLNIhdrGt190rrfnfOf8X37lm9rZm0TFFAEcnUkIALEXBHDZ9T5ef_z7YnR4W4vRJV96cd_C7Il2MMBnrXwz3aiKbL7TYjud7brlR0cHvL011QA_Q7PKK1ffp8UcG8msIRxXkhaCMEFyRvFR6gmxBiZcWA6FVXvDtABtRW4s03ossJNAQJCcTmjOCCMZ2wtKOafWjDlheow4eeRkylfRaUVT26sYQ_CZDh7X8ti2TXqIlQjKPobsfNV1dU1_Zzp4BOVTGUHZIShPp1Xa7A9r-4pY-SiNzeer7ddn3AaPozRN7-rapbxAnBz-3-BOwm8AAAD__2hQb08)

The RINOs, Neocons, and Never Trumpers in Washington, D.C. hate me because I have the courage to call for peace and refuse to take their dirty money. _They are fake people_ ,_they are liars_ ,_and they hate you and me_.
**_[Which is exactly why they are actively funding a primary opponent against me](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8z0-P2yAQBfBPg29YMPhfDj40G2WlVt1D99DuySIwdlgZ44yxrXz7iijKFX6j95728-j6e8vqIwO4OrswAKZ-MIDb1-5p_fa_3vnw80_Jb9WHL9MvvD3pBi88Ge3n46dG8fl3ef93fPvaPn5v8sXjfcYXfobm6LUb02t9ymyreiuKDFtZl0KVolIyu7ZwMBWCaaDvG1vXjalsY5W4aHuQur7YzLUgoBSVPMhKCSVydSmlLArZ26YQyjSsEI-cXHskZ7Rc4q6JQvC5CT4b22uM8_IodmZwXtCshPnuJkKbBIOz1_QdyCGP-j4G4gMhTsgXPmOYR-RhQ-JzGF10xulp4SYdxaGzYeqC75A6P_CZnO9o6Z59m5Snzmv0nUfrVs_U6UIMKkoNyE0DU6dIKzKoEopIiYCoy6bbSWbU2nl34-iWqmaFGB4j06Sthf8BAAD__5sFk-U)_.**
They are trying to take over our America First MAGA movement and usher in a new era of warmongers in Congress. In their minds, President Trump is a speed bump in history, and the Republican Party will return to the party of Neocons and forever wars.
[DONATE NOW](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8z0-P2yAQBfBPg29YMPhfDj40G2WlVt1D99DuySIwdlgZ44yxrXz7iijKFX6j95728-j6e8vqIwO4OrswAKZ-MIDb1-5p_fa_3vnw80_Jb9WHL9MvvD3pBi88Ge3n46dG8fl3ef93fPvaPn5v8sXjfcYXfobm6LUb02t9ymyreiuKDFtZl0KVolIyu7ZwMBWCaaDvG1vXjalsY5W4aHuQur7YzLUgoBSVPMhKCSVydSmlLArZ26YQyjSsEI-cXHskZ7Rc4q6JQvC5CT4b22uM8_IodmZwXtCshPnuJkKbBIOz1_QdyCGP-j4G4gMhTsgXPmOYR-RhQ-JzGF10xulp4SYdxaGzYeqC75A6P_CZnO9o6Z59m5Snzmv0nUfrVs_U6UIMKkoNyE0DU6dIKzKoEopIiYCoy6bbSWbU2nl34-iWqmaFGB4j06Sthf8BAAD__5sFk-U)
I’ve been fighting against these evil people since the day I first announced for Congress, and I’m still fighting them today.
They are slandering me as an anti-Semite because I don’t want to go to war with Iran, and nothing could be farther from the truth.
**I support Israel.**
**_I don’t support the secular Israeli Government trying to drag America into another endless foreign war._**
Being America First isn’t anti-semitic, and I refuse to be branded as a hateful person for standing up for my country and my Christian faith which teaches me “thou shall not murder”.
**I have the courage to stand on my convictions, and the RINOs and Neocons are coming after me because I have the courage to stand up to them.**
Patriot, I’m asking you to stand with me at this moment as I face the biggest political fight of my life.
**_[If you’re able, please consider making a donation of any amount to my primary campaign](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8z0-P2yAQBfBPg29YMPhfDj40G2WlVt1D99DuySIwdlgZ44yxrXz7iijKFX6j95728-j6e8vqIwO4OrswAKZ-MIDb1-5p_fa_3vnw80_Jb9WHL9MvvD3pBi88Ge3n46dG8fl3ef93fPvaPn5v8sXjfcYXfobm6LUb02t9ymyreiuKDFtZl0KVolIyu7ZwMBWCaaDvG1vXjalsY5W4aHuQur7YzLUgoBSVPMhKCSVydSmlLArZ26YQyjSsEI-cXHskZ7Rc4q6JQvC5CT4b22uM8_IodmZwXtCshPnuJkKbBIOz1_QdyCGP-j4G4gMhTsgXPmOYR-RhQ-JzGF10xulp4SYdxaGzYeqC75A6P_CZnO9o6Z59m5Snzmv0nUfrVs_U6UIMKkoNyE0DU6dIKzKoEopIiYCoy6bbSWbU2nl34-iWqmaFGB4j06Sthf8BAAD__5sFk-U)_.**
[DONATE NOW](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8z0-P2yAQBfBPg29YMPhfDj40G2WlVt1D99DuySIwdlgZ44yxrXz7iijKFX6j95728-j6e8vqIwO4OrswAKZ-MIDb1-5p_fa_3vnw80_Jb9WHL9MvvD3pBi88Ge3n46dG8fl3ef93fPvaPn5v8sXjfcYXfobm6LUb02t9ymyreiuKDFtZl0KVolIyu7ZwMBWCaaDvG1vXjalsY5W4aHuQur7YzLUgoBSVPMhKCSVydSmlLArZ26YQyjSsEI-cXHskZ7Rc4q6JQvC5CT4b22uM8_IodmZwXtCshPnuJkKbBIOz1_QdyCGP-j4G4gMhTsgXPmOYR-RhQ-JzGF10xulp4SYdxaGzYeqC75A6P_CZnO9o6Z59m5Snzmv0nUfrVs_U6UIMKkoNyE0DU6dIKzKoEopIiYCoy6bbSWbU2nl34-iWqmaFGB4j06Sthf8BAAD__5sFk-U)

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: We are at a crossroads.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
This movement is not about partisan labels or purity tests. It is about class solidarity.
It's about the thousands of _you_ who stand together and say **our lives deserve dignity, and our work deserves respect**.
It will never be _just_ institutions and officials alone that uphold our democracy. It will always be the people — the masses who refuse to comply with authoritarian regimes — who are the last and strongest defense of our country and our freedoms.
We are at a crossroads: oligarchy or democracy. But we cannot have both.
I've made my choice. **We must fight the oligarchy that has created this nightmare**.
And that is why I have never taken money from lobbyists or corporations. And it's why I never will.
But beyond elections, **our task is to build community**. Block associations, volunteer groups, church organizations, PTA's, activist organizations — because **community is the most powerful building block we have to defeat authoritarianism and root out corruption**.
This is the path to guaranteeing healthcare to every American, to establish a living minimum wage, to take on skyrocketing rents and mortgages, to tackle the climate crisis, and establish a country where the American Dream is actually possible for us.
Because we know that a better world is possible. **And we are willing to do something about it**.
[Will you contribute right now to help us power our Fighting Oligarchy tour on the road?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250619.U5lPxO&refcode=em20250619-9117-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD)
Your support allows us to rally people together at record scale to organize their communities for the better world that we know is possible.
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250619.U5lPxO&refcode=em20250619-9117-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD)**
Pa’lante,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250619?refcode=em20250619-9117-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=9117_2526796_-DoogD&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250619-9117-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD)**
---|---
[](https://www.facebook.com/OcasioCortez/?t=23&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD) | [](https://twitter.com/AOC?t=24&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD) | [](https://www.instagram.com/aoc/?t=25&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD)
---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=26&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=27&akid=9117%2E2526796%2E-DoogD)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32191?t=1011&refcode=email-20250619_v&akid=8892%2E5885482%2EZp-6of ]Our Revolution logo

may do it. I may not do it. Nobody knows what I’m going to do.”

We CANNOT allow Trump to lead us into yet another catastrophic war in the
Middle East. And Bernie Sanders is leading the way out of this nightmare,
introducing legislation to block Trump from dragging the U.S. into
Netanyahu’s Iran war. But he can't win without our urgent support.

Trump escalating Netanyahu’s conflict into a full-blown American military
action against Iran would ignite a devastating regional war, threaten
thousands of American troops, and put millions of innocent civilians at
grave risk.

And that’s why earlier this week Bernie Sanders introduced the No War
Against Iran Act in the Senate, and Ro Khanna introduced a bipartisan War
Powers Resolution in the House: to stop Trump in his tracks.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32191?source=email-20250619&t=1060&akid=8892%2E5885482%2EZp-6of ]Bernie tweet declaring the American people must stand firm and not be
dragged into Netanyahu's war with Iran.

These critical bills would block any unauthorized military action by using
Congress’s full constitutional authority to cut off funding for war with
Iran – and we need huge, immediate pressure on Congress to ensure they
pass.

After Bernie released his bill on Monday night, Our Revolution sprang into
action. Within HOURS, thousands of us flooded congressional offices with
emails demanding they join Bernie's effort to halt another disastrous
Middle East conflict.

But we didn’t stop there. We’re organizing emergency advocacy meetings on
the hill, bird-dogging key representatives to put them on the spot and
force them to say where they stand, and delivering thousands of petitions
directly into lawmakers' hands.

Together we can ensure everyone in Congress knows that the people are
watching. But with hawkish interests pouring millions into lobbying
Congress pushing for destructive conflict, we have to URGENTLY scale up
this work.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/32191?t=1054&amount=22&refcode=email-20250619nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=8892%2E5885482%2EZp-6of ]We’re mobilizing as fast as we can because we know that time is short, and
the threat of a catastrophic new war is very real. But all this
rapid-response organizing requires urgent resources. Can you donate right
now to power Our Revolution's rapid response work and stop Trump's
reckless march toward war?

When we organize, we win.

name: Reagan Dunn
subject: Let’s unite behind the one true conservative for King County Executive
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Reagan Dunn
body:
King County deserves a choice. | | https://us5.forward-to-friend.com/forward?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id;=1eccd0c20f&e;=77eef70c61 | Is this email not displaying correctly?
[View it in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/082ad14617c2/lets-unite-behind-the-one-true-conservative-for-king-county-executive?e=77eef70c61).
---|---
| [](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=e0f1575269&e=77eef70c61)
---
| | Friends,

As the only budget hawk on the King County Council, I've seen firsthand our county's need for at least a moderate, if not conservative, pragmatic voice in the King County Executive's office. King County residents deserve an Executive who will push back against the extreme downtown Seattle policies of raising taxes, defunding law enforcement, and refusing to address our homelessness crisis.

While some of the declared candidates have pretended to be opposed to higher taxes and attempted to claim a moderate mantle, the reality is they have a long history of supporting extreme tax hikes. This, along with the unfortunate array of personal issues that have taken center stage in the race for Executive, has convinced me that we deserve a better choice.
**That is why, today, I am endorsing conservative Derek Chartrand for King County Executive**.
_[Learn more about Derek here.](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=74b4c40ce9&e=77eef70c61)_ **__** Derek is the only candidate officially endorsed by the King County Republican Party. As a successful businessman who has fought for our community for years, Derek is running a campaign focused on lessening the burden of high taxes and costs of living, while fully funding law enforcement to combat out-of-control crime.

Unfortunately, John Wilson is just another tax-and-spend politician who will be a **_do-nothing_** County Executive just like he's been a **_do-nothing_** County Assessor for years. Don't waste your vote on Wilson. Instead support the only conservative in this race. Vote Derek Chartrand!

In order to push back against the extreme politics of downtown Seattle, we, as King County voters, should unite behind a candidate who will fully fund our law enforcement agencies, establish reasonable standards for removing homeless encampments, and rein in ever-growing taxes and wasteful spending.
King County voters deserve a choice between the extreme activist-driven policies of downtown Seattle and responsible leadership. Derek is the only right-of-center candidate in this race who can make it to the general election and give voters that choice.
I'm proud to endorse Derek for King County Executive and I hope you will join me.
Reagan Dunn
Vice Chairman, King County Council
---
| | [Follow on Twitter](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=258629eb19&e=77eef70c61) | [ on Facebook](https://reagandunn.us5.list-manage.com/track/click?u=0220f14f6bfeefc23687abe64&id=ea8ca0aa38&e=77eef70c61) |

name: Rick Larsen
subject: June 19, 1865
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen
body:
Juneteenth commemorates the moment enslaved people in Texas received word of the Emancipation Proclamation - two years after it was signed.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109885467/552572112/-201694504?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDgwOTE1YS0yYjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8gp8DlICZ4RJhDRBij45ALDtyvWNd6VNkWC8VDHriZw=&emci=9d1578bf-f046-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=b080915a-2b4d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=9494239)
---

| | **Juneteenth commemorates the moment enslaved people in Texas received word of the Emancipation Proclamation — two years after it was signed.** This holiday is an important opportunity to reflect on the legacy of slavery and to recommit to supporting equality and justice for all. Although President Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation in 1863, word traveled slowly, especially where it was not welcomed. **June 19, 1865 — or Juneteenth — marks the day when emancipation finally reached enslaved people in the deepest parts of the former Confederacy.** Americans celebrate Juneteenth for the promise it holds and for the powerful message it symbolizes. From its beginning, Juneteenth offered hope that America was moving closer to the ideals of the Declaration of Independence, including the idea that all people are created equal and "endowed by their Creator with certain unalienable Rights, that among these are Life, Liberty, and the pursuit of Happiness." **Juneteenth is a day to celebrate the promise of freedom for Black Americans and to focus on the work we still must do to realize that dream.** We have made progress, but we must continue to do more now to address injustice, racism, and violence in our country. More soon, _Rick_ |
---|---|---

| | Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen.
---

clean_body: Juneteenth commemorates the moment enslaved people in Texas received word of the Emancipation Proclamation - two years after it was signed. | | | | Juneteenth commemorates the moment enslaved people in Texas received word of the Emancipation Proclamation — two years after it was signed. This holiday is an important opportunity to reflect on the legacy of slavery and to recommit to supporting equality and justice for all. Although President Abraham Lincoln signed the Emancipation Proclamation in 1863, word traveled slowly, especially where it was not welcomed. June 19, 1865 — or Juneteenth — marks the day when emancipation finally reached enslaved people in the deepest parts of the former Confederacy. Americans celebrate Juneteenth for the promise it holds and for the powerful message it symbolizes. From its beginning, Juneteenth offered hope that America was moving closer to the ideals of the Declaration of Independence, including the idea that all people are created equal and "endowed by their Creator with certain unalienable Rights, that among these are Life, Liberty, and the pursuit of Happiness." Juneteenth is a day to celebrate the promise of freedom for Black Americans and to focus on the work we still must do to realize that dream. We have made progress, but we must continue to do more now to address injustice, racism, and violence in our country. More soon, Rick | ---|---|--- | | Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen. | This email was sent by . Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen PO Box 326 Everett, WA 98206 United States Email is an important way for Rick Larsen to keep supporters like you informed about critical issues and to build a winning grassroots campaign. name: Lydia Edwards, State Senator
subject: Celebrating Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Massachusetts Democratic State Committee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/Logo.png]
Peter,
For me, Juneteenth is the most patriotic celebration of our Nation. While the 4th of July celebrates the birth of the nation, Juneteenth celebrates the Nation living up to the aspirations of the Declaration of Independence and the promises of our Constitution. Juneteenth is a reminder that laws, without affirmative enforcement and without an eye towards liberation, are simply pieces of paper.
Juneteenth, like Memorial Day, are reminders that the Black American struggle for freedom is the American struggle for freedom. Juneteenth holds up a mirror to the Nation and asks simply, “Are we free?”
Are we free from economic exploitation? Are we free to love? Are we free to breathe? Are we free from violence?
I can only imagine the sense of shock, surprise, joy, anger, and fear those former slaves felt in Galveston TX, when they learned they were lied to and kept in bondage. I often wonder about the impact on the Union soldiers who relayed the message.
As a Black woman, policy maker, and military service member, I am forever aware of the freedoms I have that my own mother didn’t have, let alone my slave ancestors. It is in their honor that I celebrate Juneteenth with a recommitment to be a freedom fighter for not just my own community but for all communities that make up this diverse tapestry of America.
This Juneteenth, let us all commit to that work. Whether it’s pushing for policies that remove barriers to quality education, working towards fair, resilient housing, expanding transportation access, or investing in underserved communities, we must ensure that the promise of freedom is available for all.
In closing, I would also like to address the current political climate. Let me be clear, what we are facing as a nation is atrocious, cruel, and racist. However, I draw inspiration from the Black Americans who have struggled for freedom since the founding of this Nation under much worse conditions. I hope that you will commit to drawing inspiration, studying the history, and saying their names. There is a reason why the federal administration is attacking education and history lessons. They know if we study how powerful we have been, the shoulders we stand on, and the multi-racial coalitions that have existed since the beginning of this Nation, we will never be slaves again.
Sincerely,
Lydia Edwards
State Senator
Third Suffolk District
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP31/1/93809/images/17d4b84d-0534-43af-b853-930a4ff9fd9a.jpg]
https://www.facebook.com/massdems [https://www.facebook.com/massdems] https://twitter.com/massdems [https://twitter.com/massdems] https://instagram.com/massdems [https://instagram.com/massdems] https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems [https://www.tiktok.com/@massdems]

Massachusetts Democratic Party
11 Beacon St
Suite 410
Boston, MA 02108
United States

name: =?utf-8?B?QeKAmXNoYW50aSBGLiBHaG9sYXI=?=
subject: What Juneteenth means for our democracy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emerge America
body:
Today, on Juneteenth, we commemorate the emancipation of the last enslaved people in the United States, a full two and a half years after Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109886328/552575965/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-06-19_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjUxMTVjM2Q0LTJlNGQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=9DRggHPJhkNY9f43oDC6IEGAsLk-pMDI6ETDcpLgVjU=)
---
| Peter,

Today, on Juneteenth, we commemorate the emancipation of the last enslaved people in the United States, a full two and a half years after Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation.

Juneteenth is a celebration of freedom, but also a sobering reminder: justice in this country has often been delayed, and democracy has always required a fight.

That’s especially true now. With Donald Trump back in the White House, the stakes for our communities have never been higher.

We must remain clear-eyed about where we are and steadfast in our work to ensure that Black voices are represented and empowered in every level of government.

As the first Black woman to lead Emerge, I’m proud to guide an organization that’s not only committed to representation but to building real power that reflects the lived experience of our communities.

That means supporting Black women leaders in every state, on every ballot. And it’s why we launched our Seated Together program, the nation’s first advanced candidate leadership program for Black women elected officials seeking higher office.

**Right now, Black Emerge alums are serving in office from school board to Congress. And every day, we’re training more bold women of the New American Majority to rise, run, and win.**

Juneteenth reminds us that progress takes work, and that the fight for freedom isn’t over.

Thank you for being part of this movement.

In solidarity,
A’shanti F. Gholar
President, Emerge [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109886330/552575967/1143593133?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-06-19_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjUxMTVjM2Q0LTJlNGQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=9DRggHPJhkNY9f43oDC6IEGAsLk-pMDI6ETDcpLgVjU=)

name: AG Jason Miyares
subject: November is everything.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Miyares for Virginia |
body:
Take a moment to read this, friend.| | | Friend, I never expected this job to be easy.

I ran because **my mother fled Communist Cuba** to give her family freedom. I ran because **victims deserve justice,** and **law enforcement needs a partner,** not an opponent. And I ran because I believe **Virginia is worth fighting for.**

But now everything we’ve built is on the line.

Jay Jones is backed by the same far-left activists who want to defund the police, erase parental rights, and let criminals off easy. _**His policies aren’t just wrong, they are dangerous.**_

This race isn’t about politics, it’s about the people. It's about your family and mine. _Your kids. Your neighbors. Our future._

clean_body: Take a moment to read this, friend.| | | Friend, I never expected this job to be easy. I ran because my mother fled Communist Cuba to give her family freedom. I ran because victims deserve justice, and law enforcement needs a partner, not an opponent. And I ran because I believe Virginia is worth fighting for. But now everything we’ve built is on the line. Jay Jones is backed by the same far-left activists who want to defund the police, erase parental rights, and let criminals off easy. His policies aren’t just wrong, they are dangerous. This race isn’t about politics, it’s about the people. It's about your family and mine. Your kids. Your neighbors. Our future. We have until November to win this fight—and I am all in. I cannot do this without YOU by my side. Let's go. – AG Jason Miyares | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | | Paid for by Miyares for Virginia | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

We only have **[48] open signature spots** left for grassroots Democrats, Peter. [Sign the letter before our midnight deadline »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109886616/552576466/-649712124?amount=7&refcode=DOTL_20250619_EMLTR_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNjMTVjM2Q0LTJlNGQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=TiN2XdnNUuzoVb4lq8SMMJS-v04Jzdby9aP3RVZp_LA=) | [SIGN THE “THANK YOU GAVIN NEWSOM” LETTER NOW »](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109886617/552576468/-649712124?amount=7&refcode=DOTL_20250619_EMLTR_E2_T0_AC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNS8xLzk0MjI1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjNjMTVjM2Q0LTJlNGQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=TiN2XdnNUuzoVb4lq8SMMJS-v04Jzdby9aP3RVZp_LA=)

**TO: Gov. Gavin Newsom
FROM: A loyal Democrat
RE: Democrats stand with you ** Dear Governor Newsom,

**Thank you SO much for standing up for our democracy by suing the Trump Administration!**

Trump is clearly threatened by how you exposed him to his unconstitutional and authoritarian actions.

**His threat to jail you is not only baseless, it’s a power-grab and another attempt to launch us into a MAGA dictatorship.**

This marks a terrifying point in our nation’s history. That’s why I’m so grateful to have leaders like you who won’t back down in the face of these anti-democratic threats.

Look ↙↙ = So tomorrow at 9am sharp we’re going to send a letter to Governor Newsom to let him know: Democrats across the country have his back. We only have [48] open signature spots left for grassroots Democrats, Peter. = | = TO: Gov. Gavin Newsom FROM: A loyal Democrat RE: Democrats stand with you Dear Governor Newsom, Thank you SO much for standing up for our democracy by suing the Trump Administration! Trump is clearly threatened by how you exposed him to his unconstitutional and authoritarian actions. His threat to jail you is not only baseless, it’s a power-grab and another attempt to launch us into a MAGA dictatorship. This marks a terrifying point in our nation’s history. name: Ilhan Omar
subject: What happened to Melissa and Mark is unconscionable
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/35?t=1&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)
Friend,
I had the immense honor of serving with Melissa Hortman during my time in the state legislature. She was a mentor to me as a freshman legislator, like she was to so many new legislators. She was kind and thoughtful, but she was also a tenacious leader. Her heart had room to care for so many of us and about so many issues, and while she never shied away from the fights to get the work done, her heart was how she led. She was a great organizer, and her guidance helped secure the democratic trifecta that earned us some of the most impactful legislation in Minnesota’s history. We will be living in the world that Melissa helped build for decades, and it will never cease to inspire me.
What happened to Melissa and Mark is unconscionable. These two people were good and kind, and while Melissa was an elected leader, she was above all else a human, and human life is so precious. My heart aches for their children, Sophie and Colin, who should have seen their parents grow old.
I am so grateful to know that John and Yvette Hoffman are expected to recover. But I cannot imagine the terror of having to protect your daughter in that moment by putting your own life on the line. As a mother I am heartbroken just considering it. John, Yvette, and Hope are on my mind and in my heart every day.
This man carried out an assassination and an assassination attempt because our current political climate encouraged his violence. It is the result of years of misinformation, manipulation, fear-mongering, and hatred, which has divided our country to the extent that we largely live in separate realities. Even in the aftermath of this horrific tragedy, we saw a sitting US Senator spread lies for no reason other than to incite the mobs online. It is disgusting, and there should be no place for such rhetoric anywhere in our politics.
When I received the call detailing the devastating events of that night, authorities also warned me that I was named in the documents found in the assailant’s vehicle. Throughout my time in Congress, I have received many phone calls and messages warning me that someone had made legitimate threats on my life, and multiple people have been sentenced to prison for the threats they have made against me. I know too well how hatred has poisoned the minds of too many of my fellow Americans. It is a sad reality of this moment that the comments on this heartfelt statement will be filled with more lies, personal attacks, and misinformation. But this statement is not for them – it is for the millions of Americans who are disgusted by the normalization of violence, and whom I stand alongside in calling for every single one of us to refuse to allow these terrorists to win. The promise of America is that we, the people, control our fate.
If you haven't already read the touching statement written by Melissa and Mark’s kids, I hope you take a few minutes to do that [**here.**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4450?t=2&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)
I will honor their memory when I pet our dog, when I go for a walk in one of Minnesota’s world class parks, and when I enjoy a homemade baked good. I will embody Melissa's legacy every day in working towards a country where every single one of us is safe, healthy, and prosperous.
Please consider donating to support these families in their time of immense need:
[**For Sophie and Colin Hortman**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4451?t=3&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)
[**For John and Yvette Hoffman**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/go/4452?t=4&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)
In solidarity,
Ilhan Omar

PAID FOR BY ILHAN FOR CONGRESS
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can **[unsubscribe here](https://act.ilhanomar.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?source=em-footer&t=5&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)**. You can also **[sign up to receive fewer emails](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=6&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)**.
Submit this form to [**update your contact information**](https://act.ilhanomar.com/signup/update-contact-info/source=em-footer?t=7&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)
Ilhan is counting on our people-powered grassroots movement to fund this campaign. If you can, stand with us by **[making a contribution today](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/io-em-footer/?refcode=em-footer&t=8&refcode2=1675_791427_wIgQaJ&akid=1675%2E791427%2EwIgQaJ)**.
Ilhan for Congress
PO Box 33079
Washington D.C., 20033

name: Warning
subject: Trump’s scheme for a third term
body:

Peter, Earlier this month, the Supreme Court rejected a Republican attempt to throw out Democrat leaning ballots in Pennsylvania, reaffirming that states have jurisdiction over how elections are administered:
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT--LMaNOB4yciFE_Av6m0aGGP3iADKCWJ0NkLyHcZQVtgfZNN6yK-j3eHvnAd5NIWBFP6-KXhvGSx_5gS31JgRUwD-xGeJLun99Evn5P14HpINmFSz7c0bhiH8wzbHJdl9--e2vZSZSqIe4BWddLYLy5UJh8NsDe7D43LfCWgsyhDQfvirwxeRXQO-7yANi8A62DUlgOXiWQmJAx2NQ2JubHZbEx-rae6oyJFhlS1kDbKXGsxIb_n7yXkwU-J2OmRRoj6ySIWPHFVGFikAqnM9wr7GIs-M7kB-yYO1Kd7FCdU/4hh/k2WNucsgSaO-Xq8K4sE2Kw/h1/h001.6wcCSrnvBrcF4BK_zNQNOCrc-uuJDE7bnm7WxmKV9MA)

_If a MAGA majority was in control of the PA State Supreme Court,_ the GOP would have been able to throw out ballots that overwhelmingly lean Democrat - giving Republicans an advantage in the most important swing state in America. **The stakes for the upcoming 2025 PA Supreme Court justice retention election couldn 't be higher. ** The PA Supreme Court is now under assault by Trump and his billionaire cronies. They are attempting to remove Democratic justices from the PA Supreme Court and replace them with MAGA loyalists who will issue decisions on key election lawsuits that would suppress Dem votes and turn PA from a swing state to solid red - _securing the White House for Trump when he attempts to run for a third term in 2028._
---
| [ ](https://url1005.email.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv1STuy_axapI11rAiEMjCBP8MqJU5OU8RUGh4w-siT--LMaNOB4yciFE_Av6m0aGG2PPTuP1hsR0EF8sOQvGAuEXh6eY2noP8u1NM1Qc5y3Trr7FcmJqJz4RVR0FyEktqlEGuFvf7ePEbKpfmhLevbW5-037UIyaDo9T6kx1PA1r_EZni6mqrn1Ud1Zth2HvqL3nn3rVSp4T2syi70c3IKLyU5oOuSJD3f0I43syTw5ps6PM_yt5vI3RRz3QDO8ecCLzirfELF_qXxtqg_M1n9IjTXunVXA2edfblkb1R2Glir8XdKicnE-g1vJRBlY1JI8oAvHnn7Hv4nfM4lslSx/4hh/k2WNucsgSaO-Xq8K4sE2Kw/h2/h001.1qenHk7plii9X6XPf1ipdU3ukDpoL9K5iBu5GdOqiHU)

**Defend Our Courts is sounding the alarm about the 2025 PA Supreme Court battle.** The rare special election is garnering little media attention and Republicans are assembling a massive $30 million+ war chest. We can't wait until the last minute to mobilize. **[PA law limits the ability of the democratic state Supreme Court justices to campaign - so we are stepping up in their stead. name: Donavan McKinney
subject: Bernie Sanders endorsed our campaign
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Hey Peter, I’m Donavan McKinney –
running for Congress in Michigan’s Mighty 13th district – and I’ve got
some big news: Senator Bernie Sanders just endorsed our campaign this
week.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250619-dm?refcode=em20250619-36774&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36774_1203867_is35Z2&akid=36774%2E1203867%2Eis35Z2 ]A screenshot of a post on X from Bernie Sanders (@BernieSanders)
reading '.@DonavanMI13 is exactly the type of leader we need in Washington
right now. He understands the struggles working class communities are
facing in Detroit because he's lived those struggles himself. I'm urging
all Michiganders to join me in supporting his campaign.'

I am honored to have Bernie’s support in this race. For decades, he’s been
a champion for the issues that matter most to our district: environmental
justice, health care for all, labor rights, and so much more.

His commitment to standing with the people to build a multi-racial,
multi-generational working class movement to take on the corporate
oligarchy is exactly what our campaign’s about. I’m ready to join him and
take that fight to D.C.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250619-dm?refcode=em20250619-36774&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36774_1203867_is35Z2&akid=36774%2E1203867%2Eis35Z2 ]Bernie is with us, and now I’m asking you to join him. Split a donation
between our campaign and Justice Democrats today to help us build this
grassroots movement to take on the corporate interests and win!

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250619-dm?refcode=em20250619-36774&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36774_1203867_is35Z2&akid=36774%2E1203867%2Eis35Z2 ]Contribute $5

For far too long, communities like ours have been left behind while
corporate interests rake in record profits and politicians turn a blind
eye. As a State Representative, I serve the poorest district in Michigan.
I’ve seen firsthand how the ultra-wealthy put their interests ahead of
working families. The time for waiting is over. We need bold change now.

I’m in this fight because I’ve lived the struggle. I grew up in the shadow
of Detroit’s factories and smokestacks and developed a cough – like so
many others in my family – that I still have today. Our kids deserve
better. Our neighbors deserve better. That’s why I’m fighting for a Green
New Deal to get the lead out of our water, the pollution out of our air,
and transition to 100% clean and renewable energy.

As a former leader with SEIU Healthcare Michigan, I know our families
cannot afford anything less than Medicare for All. I’ll work with Bernie
to ensure everyone in this country has universal, guaranteed healthcare —
no matter their employment status, zip code, race, gender, sexuality, or
disability.

And we’re also keeping the working class at the heart of this campaign.
Every worker deserves a living wage and the ability to form a union. But
for decades, we’ve watched corporations and their paid-for politicians in
both parties gut our unions and their power, while billionaires only get
richer and pay even less in taxes. I’m running to flip the script and make
sure the 99% stops getting left behind the 1%.

I’ve spent my life fighting for the people of Michigan, and I’m proud to
have Senator Bernie Sanders on our side. But the truth is that winning
this race won’t be easy.

My opponent, incumbent Rep. Shri Thanedar, is an out-of-touch, self-funded
millionaire who has more in common with Donald Trump and Elon Musk than
working families like ours. As long as there are multimillionaires like
Shri in Congress, the needs of working-class people in districts like
Michigan’s 13th will go ignored and unmet.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250619-dm?refcode=em20250619-36774&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36774_1203867_is35Z2&akid=36774%2E1203867%2Eis35Z2 ]We are in this Democratic primary to win it and fight for working-class
Detroiters — but we can’t do it without you. Help me and Bernie take on
the corporate interests in Congress with a grassroots donation to my
campaign and Justice Democrats now.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250619-dm?refcode=em20250619-36774&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36774_1203867_is35Z2&akid=36774%2E1203867%2Eis35Z2 ]Contribute $5

Thank you,

Donavan McKinney

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250619-36774-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36774_1203867_is35Z2&akid=36774%2E1203867%2Eis35Z2

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36774%2E1203867%2Eis35Z2

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
JusticeDemocrats.com
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster, PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

# [Juneteenth Is Not Just About Ending Slavery](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=166333203&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY2MzMzMjAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTAzNTMwNTgsImV4cCI6MTc1Mjk0NTA1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.tbEzjQIBGDwqyCbh649o0_3Z5LPHD-XR071z0u13CMo)
| | [Qasim Rashid, Esq.](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---
| Jun 19
---
| [](https://substack.com/@qasimrashid)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=166333203&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY2MzMzMjAzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MDM1MzA1OCwiZXhwIjoxNzUyOTQ1MDU4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjU1MDIyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.jkdJzOWU9Lj6One2XsRynApphEGlln9SbuXdJsL6ITw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=166333203&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY2MzMzMjAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTAzNTMwNTgsImV4cCI6MTc1Mjk0NTA1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.tbEzjQIBGDwqyCbh649o0_3Z5LPHD-XR071z0u13CMo&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2550223&post_id=166333203&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY2MzMzMjAzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTAzNTMwNTgsImV4cCI6MTc1Mjk0NTA1OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTI1NTAyMjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.tbEzjQIBGDwqyCbh649o0_3Z5LPHD-XR071z0u13CMo)

| | [](https://substack..ZAXQeY4ONlZmDxUlmaGLCw9cnJVTGXIRPBfXNsnD8hM?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/qasimrashid/p/juneteenth-is-not-just-about-ending?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Today marks Juneteenth, a day to commemorate that moment in 1865--more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation--when Union troops arrived in Galveston, Texas to inform enslaved Black Americans that they were finally free. It is a celebration of liberation long delayed, a day that reminds us of both how far we've come--and how far we have yet to go.
But let us be honest about what Juneteenth truly represents: a partial liberation. Because the truth is, slavery may have been outlawed on paper, but it never fully ended in practice. So what steps can we take today to advance true liberation in the United States? **Let 's Address This.**
**Let's Address This is made possible by you, the thoughtful reader. Consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack..uMKear8WE4U2H3STh0cQzWUjIZSbNYafPH6xXULkteQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=166333203)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/7cf01813-f256-4849-b1b5-56552b4aef5a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Illustrated print by Thomas Nast depicting life before and after emancipation. [Credit: Keith Lance/Getty Images]
## The Truth about Legality vs Morality and Justice
This Juneteenth, let us remember that human trafficking was legal. Slavery was legal. Jim Crow segregation was legal. The U.S. government paying reparations to white enslavers--compensating them for the "loss property," (i.e. enslaved people)--was legal. And yet not one dollar of reparations has ever been paid to those who were enslaved, brutalized, and dehumanized. So let this be our collective reminder: legality is not a measure of morality nor justice. It never has been. And it never should be.
If you need further proof, consider that slavery _still_ exists in America today--within the prison system. The 13th Amendment abolished slavery _except_ as punishment for a crime. And that exception created a pipeline from plantation slavery to prison slavery, where today, incarcerated people--disproportionately Black and brown--are forced to work for pennies on the dollar. Meanwhile, the private prison industry has become a multi-billion dollar empire, profiting from modern-day enslavement. That's not justice. That's not freedom. That's human trafficking and white supremacy by another name.
Also consider that Juneteenth only became a federal holiday in 2021--four years ago. For centuries, Black Americans carried this history on their own, commemorate it in the margins, and pass it down from generation to generation because the federal government refused to acknowledge its own crimes. Recognizing Juneteenth on the national calendar is progress, yes--but it is not justice. It is not accountability.
## Substance Over Symbolism
Indeed, we cannot confuse symbolism for substance.
This Juneteenth, let us not simply celebrate freedom with BBQs and hashtags--let us (particularly non-Black people) recommit ourselves to the work of building a more perfect union. That means fighting for reparations. That means abolishing prison slavery. That means ending voter suppression. That means investing in Black communities instead of over-policing them. That means funding racial justice instead of white supremacy. That means demanding HR 40 (the bill to study reparations) is passed, and the move to pay reparations is completed. This brilliant piece by [Professor Ta-Nehisi Coates on The Case for Reparations](https://substack.com/redirect/ff9cdf9e-18e9-4e28-b8b1-a4f9d5eb123a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is a must read for anyone unsure on the subject.
Justice isn't just a date on the calendar--it requires action, courage, and the willingness to name hard truths. It requires taking action. It requires investing resources.
So as one small but meaningful step, join me by investing in amazing Black voices this Juneteenth. These are just 10 voices I listen to, read, learn from, and subscribe to as much as I can as a paid subscriber, and I invite you to do so as well:
* [The Breakdown with Dara Starr Tucker](https://substack.com/redirect/776c8cd2-f7e4-4cee-9a3c-f9944d9ddad9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [Bet on it with Booker](https://substack.com/redirect/9582959b-15dc-4618-a5f5-812b92106c4d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [We Need to Talk by Malynda Hale](https://substack.com/redirect/6c661558-8c77-4eb4-8278-ef245c4e295c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [Change Cadet Action Network](https://substack.com/redirect/66fa2b30-ed05-4d63-a2d9-cc6eda8b92fa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [Leslye Joy Allen, Historian](https://substack.com/@leslyejoyallen)
* [Onward with Ava DuVernay](https://substack.com/redirect/e4ad9981-97ea-4043-8a0e-26cd43f661ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [History Can't Hide from Kahlil Greene](https://substack.com/redirect/6a998723-05b4-443b-855b-23cd3969f9d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [10 Minute History by Anthony M Public Historian](https://substack.com/redirect/2e22a493-4c56-4b54-b664-2fcf9086dbf4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [Keeping it Conscious by Conscious Lee](https://substack.com/redirect/651ad04e-5985-4cb7-8850-860bd241c152?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* [Mr. Ernest Crim, Your Favorite Black History Teacher](https://substack.com/redirect/3d3227cb-18ce-4831-8b2f-de50d15a23ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

There is no justice without economic justice. And investing in the economic growth of Black voices as they elevate calls for racial justice and human rights is a critical step forward for our country. So, skip coffee once or twice a month and invest in at least a few, or as many as you can, of the aforementioned voices. And tag in the comments the Black voices you follow and learn from, so others can also follow and support them.
## In Closing
This is a journey, and it is far from over. Today we remember the enslaved who fought for freedom. We remember the children lost to mass incarceration. We remember the redlining, the lynchings, the Black codes, the broken promises of Reconstruction, the war on drugs, the school-to-prison pipeline. And we remember those who never stopped resisting--like Harriet, like Frederick, like Coretta, like Malcolm, like Martin, like Rosa, and like Ruby, and so many more. We walk in their footsteps and carry their torch.
We affirm that justice delayed is justice denied--and that we will not wait another century for what is rightfully owed. We stand with our Black neighbors, not just in memory, but in movement.
A solemn and powerful Juneteenth to you and yours.
**Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.**

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/b4ac9d76-98b4-4378-a3f1-81b1b477c9c5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
