Thank you for being an awesome subscriber! When possible, I hope you'll consider upgrading to a $6/mo or $50/year subscription! | | | Jun 19 | | | | | | | | | | | | Today marks Juneteenth, a day to commemorate that moment in 1865--more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation--when Union troops arrived in Galveston, Texas to inform enslaved Black Americans that they were finally free. It is a celebration of liberation long delayed, a day that reminds us of both how far we've come--and how far we have yet to go. But let us be honest about what Juneteenth truly represents: a partial liberation. Because the truth is, slavery may have been outlawed on paper, but it never fully ended in practice. So what steps can we take today to advance true liberation in the United States? Let 's Address This. Let's Address This is made possible by you, the thoughtful reader. Consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- Illustrated print by Thomas Nast depicting life before and after emancipation. [Credit: Keith Lance/Getty Images] The Truth about Legality vs Morality and Justice This Juneteenth, let us remember that human trafficking was legal. Slavery was legal. Jim Crow segregation was legal. The U.S. government paying reparations to white enslavers--compensating them for the "loss property," (i.e. enslaved people)--was legal. And yet not one dollar of reparations has ever been paid to those who were enslaved, brutalized, and dehumanized. So let this be our collective reminder: legality is not a measure of morality nor justice. It never has been. And it never should be. If you need further proof, consider that slavery still exists in America today--within the prison system. The 13th Amendment abolished slavery except as punishment for a crime. And that exception created a pipeline from plantation slavery to prison slavery, where today, incarcerated people--disproportionately Black and brown--are forced to work for pennies on the dollar. Meanwhile, the private prison industry has become a multi-billion dollar empire, profiting from modern-day enslavement. That's not justice. That's not freedom. That's human trafficking and white supremacy by another name. Also consider that Juneteenth only became a federal holiday in 2021--four years ago. For centuries, Black Americans carried this history on their own, commemorate it in the margins, and pass it down from generation to generation because the federal government refused to acknowledge its own crimes. Recognizing Juneteenth on the national calendar is progress, yes--but it is not justice. It is not accountability. Substance Over Symbolism Indeed, we cannot confuse symbolism for substance. This Juneteenth, let us not simply celebrate freedom with BBQs and hashtags--let us (particularly non-Black people) recommit ourselves to the work of building a more perfect union. That means fighting for reparations. That means abolishing prison slavery. That means ending voter suppression. That means investing in Black communities instead of over-policing them. That means funding racial justice instead of white supremacy. That means demanding HR 40 (the bill to study reparations) is passed, and the move to pay reparations is completed. This brilliant piece by is a must read for anyone unsure on the subject. Justice isn't just a date on the calendar--it requires action, courage, and the willingness to name hard truths. It requires taking action. It requires investing resources. So as one small but meaningful step, join me by investing in amazing Black voices this Juneteenth. These are just 10 voices I listen to, read, learn from, and subscribe to as much as I can as a paid subscriber, and I invite you to do so as well: There is no justice without economic justice. And investing in the economic growth of Black voices as they elevate calls for racial justice and human rights is a critical step forward for our country. So, skip coffee once or twice a month and invest in at least a few, or as many as you can, of the aforementioned voices. And tag in the comments the Black voices you follow and learn from, so others can also follow and support them. In Closing This is a journey, and it is far from over. Today we remember the enslaved who fought for freedom. We remember the children lost to mass incarceration. We remember the redlining, the lynchings, the Black codes, the broken promises of Reconstruction, the war on drugs, the school-to-prison pipeline. And we remember those who never stopped resisting--like Harriet, like Frederick, like Coretta, like Malcolm, like Martin, like Rosa, and like Ruby, and so many more. We walk in their footsteps and carry their torch. We affirm that justice delayed is justice denied--and that we will not wait another century for what is rightfully owed. We stand with our Black neighbors, not just in memory, but in movement. A solemn and powerful Juneteenth to you and yours. Let's Address This with Qasim Rashid is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. | | ---|---|--- Quick Ask! I am doing everything in my power over the next four years to stave off fascism in the United States, and I need your support.Become a paid subscriber and join the movement.** | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Qasim Rashid, Esq. 1552 South Route 59 #1333 Naperville, IL 60564 ? ?

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: An inspiration
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR
body:
I will not stop fighting until we can achieve real equity in Michigan and beyond.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=428%2E181023%2EOLDrIT)
Hi team —
I believe the power of the people will always be greater than people in power. And few holidays emulate that truth more than Juneteenth, the anniversary of the end of slavery in our country. I am always moved by the fact that Juneteenth is about honoring and reflecting on the work it took to eradicate one of the most inhumane chapters in American history, but also how long it took for the promise of the Emancipation Proclamation to be made real for Black Americans throughout our country.
In so many ways, Juneteenth is also a reminder of the unfinished business of the historic fight for racial justice and equity in America, and an opportunity for all of us to recommit ourselves to the work of ensuring that every American can thrive in our country.
When I think of those who came before us and worked to create a better, more just, more fair world, like Opal Lee, the grandmother of Juneteenth, I know their eyes are on us now and asking: what will we do to continue their work, and make real the promises of our Constitution, and our democracy?
Making that promise real is what our campaign for governor is all about. From my time investigating hate groups and hate crimes with the Southern Poverty Law Center, to my service as a clerk for Judge Damon Keith in Detroit advocating for civil rights for _all_ Michiganders, to my time as Secretary of State working to expand ballot access, **I have and will always prioritize protecting and expanding the rights and freedoms of every American.**
Juneteenth is an important reminder of the persistent fights so many Black Americans have faced and of the work that we must continue every single day. Like so many who have come before us, I will not stop fighting until we can achieve real equity in Michigan and beyond.
We’ve got a lot to do in the months and years ahead to carry on the banner for justice and equity in our state and beyond. And as governor, I’ll be marching with you every step of the way. So let’s go.
Jocelyn

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with supporters like you.
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe-jbmi/?t=2&akid=428%2E181023%2EOLDrIT)

PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR GOVERNOR

23133 Woodward Ave, Number 116,
Ferndale, MI 48220

clean_body: The same billionaires who elected Trump are working to covertly buy this open Congressional seat for their trojan horse candidate and are hoping we won't notice | | Friend \- if we haven't met yet my name is Bushra Amiwala. I just announced my campaign for Congress in Illinois' 9th Congressional district. But why should you care about IL-09, a deep blue district in Chicago, when MAGA is destroying the country and the GOP controls congress? | The same billionaires who elected Trump are working to covertly buy this open Congressional seat for their trojan horse candidate and are hoping we won't notice. MAGA can't buy me. In 2019, I made history when I won a highly contested school board election - becoming the first woman in my generation to take office. Now after seven years of service, I'm ready to fight for working families, reproductive freedom, climate justice, and peace, unlike my MAGA funded opponent. Thank you, Bushra Amiwala Democrat for IL-09 | | Mail checks to: No Dem Left Behind P.O. Box 15320 Washington, D.C. 2003 | Paid for by No Dem Left Behind PAC Not Authorized by Any Federal Candidate or Candidate's Committee. | | Sent via . name: Adam Schiff
subject: Honoring Juneteenth and fighting for equality
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=8700%2E5257348%2EBF0HfR)
Derek,
Today, we commemorate Juneteenth, a day of profound significance in our country’s history.
On June 19th, 1865, the last enslaved African Americans in Galveston, Texas, were informed of their freedom, more than two years after President Lincoln issued the Emancipation Proclamation. It was a turning point in the long and unfinished fight for freedom and equality for Black Americans.
**_That fight continues today._**
Juneteenth reminds us that the path to justice is not paved by proclamations or legislation alone — it’s a daily struggle, driven by action, courage, and perseverance.
In Congress, I’ve supported legislation to address systemic injustice and expand opportunity, from protecting voting rights and advancing meaningful criminal justice reform, to ensuring equitable access to health care and education.
But I also know that we can’t achieve equality without a functioning democracy. That’s why I’ve worked to strengthen our institutions, defend our elections, and speak out against white nationalism regardless of where I see it — whether it’s in our communities, our criminal justice system, or even the halls of Congress.
So today, let us remember. Let us reflect. And let us resolve to continue the work — together.
**[Will you join me in honoring Juneteenth and committing to the fight for justice and equality?](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1023?c0=engagement-20250619-juneteenth.hjrTja&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=8700%2E5257348%2EBF0HfR)**
[SIGN ON NOW](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1023?c0=engagement-20250619-juneteenth.hjrTja&refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=5&akid=8700%2E5257348%2EBF0HfR)
---
Thank you.
— Adam
[](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/5245?refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=6&akid=8700%2E5257348%2EBF0HfR)
| | [DONATE](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/5243?refcode=AU_ABS_EG_GEN_PET_AC90_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&email=&t=7&akid=8700%2E5257348%2EBF0HfR)

**[www.adamschiff.com](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/60?t=2&akid=8700%2E5257348%2EBF0HfR) | [contact@adamschiff.com](mailto:contact@adamschiff.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/64?t=3&akid=8700%2E5257348%2EBF0HfR)**

This land connects some of the last remaining ancient redwood forests, forming a corridor of biodiversity and natural beauty that people from all over the world cherish.
By joining us, you’re not just preserving redwoods—you’re preserving a living legacy. Once we do so, this land will be entrusted to Sonoma County Regional Parks, ensuring it remains open and accessible to all. And who knows? You might even find yourself walking through these woods one day taking in the breathtaking views knowing you played a role in keeping them healthy and resilient.
**But we can’t do it without you.[Act now to be part of this historic moment!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/109887350/552579457/2018652235?contactdata=80t1fVBtorT89ad+vodu1MstPiKp2nbdngU0t4wnnqghHpo1tS8gBOTI6FP7/CdA9/RbiAUTXmDXxHE5KBfcc5LasPvCEceKpdxJem+rIB6nUGSvBtvCXU3qzbI4ljV3jqqnnGsXrbXS1o5mU3ZMJHpd0/czof1xmPTnUrtQyE8=&ms=MonteRio9_Link2&sourceid=1166936&amtOpts=&recurringAmtOpts=&am=&recurringAm=&is_optimized_ask=true&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9TVFJXTC9TVFJXTC8xLzg5NTc0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImQ3ZWZlYjM0LTMxNGQtZjAxMS04ZjdjLTYwNDViZGZlOGU5YyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=9Ss_MCRAfvOfyUToY8KXpFNw8GUbmnGBEu-2O5CYuX0=&emci=f3b6e4fa-404c-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=d7efeb34-314d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=1504551)**
Your support today means this land will remain wild, open, and free for everyone to enjoy—including you.

clean_body: Keep this forest wild, open, and free Peter, Time for our matching gift opportunity is running out. By the end of the day tomorrow, our opportunity to conserve this breathtaking piece of forest will come to a close and with it, our chance to ensure its preservation for generations to come. Monte Rio Redwoods is more than a beautiful landscape; it’s a vital link in the heart of Sonoma County. Thanks to our generous match donors, The Visser Family, you can now double your impact. Photo by: Max Whittaker This land connects some of the last remaining ancient redwood forests, forming a corridor of biodiversity and natural beauty that people from all over the world cherish. By joining us, you’re not just preserving redwoods—you’re preserving a living legacy. Once we do so, this land will be entrusted to Sonoma County Regional Parks, ensuring it remains open and accessible to all. And who knows? You might even find yourself walking through these woods one day taking in the breathtaking views knowing you played a role in keeping them healthy and resilient. But we can’t do it without you. Your support today means this land will remain wild, open, and free for everyone to enjoy—including you. Thank you for all you do to help the redwoods. With gratitude, | Kate Berry Director of Annual Giving ---|--- | | | | ---|---|---|--- Save the Redwoods League 111 Sutter Street, 11th Floor San Francisco, CA 94104 United States |

subject: Honoring Juneteenth
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sanchez
body:
Today, we honor Juneteenth — a powerful reminder of how far we’ve come, and how far we still have to go.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109887337/552579414/2116327045?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkYWVmZWIzNC0zMTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=dr-OBItRqrsmjF6hho1BweWclOQ_OGJ40Dn8QvtIKns=&emci=fc439f55-5e4c-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=daefeb34-314d-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=6681226)
---
| Peter,

Today, we honor Juneteenth — a powerful reminder of how far we’ve come, and how far we still have to go.

On June 19, 1865, more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation, the last enslaved people in Galveston, Texas finally learned they were free. Juneteenth is a celebration of that long-overdue freedom and the enduring strength, resilience, and leadership of the Black community.

It’s also a day to reflect. While we commemorate progress, we must also acknowledge the deep, persistent injustices that persist in our institutions, economy, criminal justice system, and beyond.

In Congress, I remain committed to advancing racial justice, protecting voting rights, and ensuring that equity is not just an aspiration, but a reality.

Today and every day, let’s honor the legacy of Juneteenth by standing up for freedom, justice, and equality for all.

Happy Juneteenth!

Linda
---

* * *

name: Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders
subject: Did you see my dad's text to President Trump?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Sarah for Governor |
body:
When I called him on Father’s Day, he was in a bunker – like thousands of Israelis.| | | Peter, **Did you see the text my dad sent to President Trump this week?**
---
---
My dad is amazing and I love him!

When I called him this past weekend on Father’s Day, he was in a bunker – like thousands of Israelis.

_**It is time for moral clarity and for us all to stand with Israel against the forces of evil.**_

I am so proud my dad is helping to provide that moral clarity while serving as the U.S. Ambassador to Israel, and I am praying for him and all of those in harm’s way every day.
---
God Bless,Governor Sarah Huckabee Sanders
---

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: This week for Juneteenth, I spoke on the floor of the House of Representatives demanding reparations, Black maternal health equity, and more
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress
body:
I will keep fighting for Black folks in Michigan’s 12th congressional district and across the country. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWWGZeQa3aWPwL7fBo4EnyY_TXs_pz7BdEs3EneXQxvbdcU7bELSKdcZo8EKWiT4rl2ANFh413-5utzIJ0-zoSfGAJRrbrmnPNXDvVcUlfA8qh5XjWYtQkGCwxX9VP3_4awzOIg7YboUby6FQH8Z-F6ViykwgYKcycgdznfaBVjRX2jWjIZFBUkrrQoFNZ4INRYDMOkolCGB_E3aSnFmYNVtfPDRdiGldIReg5T1HcY74aq8qcQDlwp7I4hcMAS-BDqVQ3hgA7r6vXvLcRgDEGwx48SxpvCjUtm5L5bU5TStGmPj6hBTjl2xFptzr0iNZN_8VXO8-6sQdv66Zw6Eguk9I59s40LOnhCuXRTbwUDR3weGNMOBwM2rPbokKe1sMXUT3DaKudRWI9vmWiCkhetCmJ3UvIYNvhNuf74IUmIWuqeHyoRDojhH7igxk4nrA0/4hh/pbKPEMUXQo2qL1MTpiXwMw/h0/h001.hL_GncrUyG5wMOpBmHW0XSpxF-prp_qZq56hHXQP50g)
---
Friend, Today is Juneteenth’s 160th anniversary, marking 160 years since African Americans won freedom from slavery. The struggle for Black liberation continues, and I promise I will keep fighting for Black folks in Michigan’s 12th congressional district and across the country. This week for Juneteenth, I spoke on the floor of the House of Representatives to urge my colleagues in Congress to honor our Black neighbors by fighting for what’s long-overdue, including reparations, Black maternal health equity, investments for HBCUs, and real protections for voting rights. Detroiters know that reparations are not just about beginning to repair the past, they’re also about addressing the present-day impacts of institutional racism. We see this in the growing Black-white wealth gap, health gap, and homeownership gap. I have the honor and responsibility of representing a district where African Americans make up the largest racial group, and I’m serious about showing up for my Black neighbors. That includes raising money for local racial justice organizations and working in partnership with my constituents to develop policies for racial justice, economic and housing justice, environmental justice, and civil rights protections. Recently, I joined local groups and racial justice scholar Dr. Ibram X. Kendi for a press conference to challenge Detroit’s discriminatory property tax system—which taxes the _least_ expensive homes _six times_ _more_ than the _most_ expensive homes. With unaffordable property tax bills, lower-income homeowners in our majority-Black city are threatened with unjust foreclosures and displacement. This injustice continues nationwide, with Black homeowners paying 10 to 13 percent higher property tax bills on average. I’ll keep demanding housing justice and advocating for the human right to housing. In partnership with Black-led Michigan group Mothering Justice, **I co-founded the Congressional Mamas Caucus to advocate for mothers of color** and working-class families. We’ve pushed for affordable childcare, paid leave, reproductive justice, and the **_Black Maternal Health Momnibus Act_**. Last week we heard from dads who shared their powerful stories about the urgent need to address the Black maternal health crisis. I promise to keep fighting for an equitable health system with culturally affirming care that truly supports Black women and birthing people. **In Congress, I’ve passed legislation to address racial inequities, including expanding Black homeownership and ending banks’ denial of small-dollar mortgage loans** , banning medical debt from appearing on credit reports, funding home repairs to keep people in their homes, and addressing our country’s crisis of lead poisoning**.** I also created the bipartisan **Get the Lead Out Caucus to push for urgent action to remove dangerous lead pipes** across the country**.** We brought members of Congress to Michigan communities with lead contamination to hear directly from community leaders. Thousands of Detroiters have had their access to water shut off because of lead contamination or an inability to pay skyrocketing water bills. Early in the pandemic, I led successful efforts to secure a **$1.1 billion water assistance fund to prevent water shutoffs and help people with water costs.** I’m still leading policies to ensure that utilities—like water and power—are treated like the human rights they are. Here are some relevant policies I’ve also introduced:

* The **_Justice for All Act,_****which would restore and strengthen the protections in our country’s crucial civil rights laws.** It would combat intentional _and_ unintentional discrimination**** against people based on race, color, religion, sex, disability, age, or national origin—clarifying the definition of “race” to include race-related traits like hair texture. **It would also prohibit racial profiling in police activity and end qualified immunity for government employees such as police officers.**

* The **_Prohibit Auto Insurance Discrimination Act,_****which would stop auto insurance companies from using anti-Black, predatory practices to set auto insurance rates** based on income, credit score, education levels, zip code, and other racial proxies unrelated to driving history. Despite being one of the most impoverished areas in the country, metro Detroit’s car insurance rates are the most expensive in the country. Auto insurance discrimination keeps people in the cycle of poverty.

* The **_Cumulative Impacts Act_****, which would address environmental racism** and block corporate polluters from continuing to treat Black and brown communities as dumping grounds.

* The******_BOOST Act_****, which would uplift 45% of people living in poverty** instantly by providing refundable tax credits to individuals and families.

* The **_End Childhood Poverty Act,_****which would create a universal, monthly child allowance of over $400 per child** —including the lowest-wealth families who are traditionally left out of the tax credit system.

* The **_Dismantle Mass Incarceration for Public Health Act_****, which would release eligible people from jails, prisons, and detention centers** during the pandemic.

* The **_Public Banking Act,_****which would expand access to banking, credit, and loans** for marginalized people who have been systematically shut out of our nation’s private banks.

* The __**_Tenant Empowerment Act_****, which would strengthen HUD tenant protections** and provide tenants with the tools necessary to improve the quality of their homes.

* The **_Medical Debt Cancellation Act_** , which would eliminate all medical debt**** held by millions of Americans.

* The **_Public Housing for the 21st Century Act_** , which would increase urgently-needed affordable housing production.

I will keep pushing for racial and economic justice. And I’ll continue pushing back against the White-Supremacist-in-Chief’s racist attacks on our communities—such as his travel ban on Black and brown people from Africa, the Caribbean, and the Middle East. I’m co-leading the **_NO BAN Act_****to block travel bans** , and I’m speaking out about how our country’s anti-Black racism and policing leads to disproportionate detention and deportation rates for Black immigrants. I’ve held Customs and Border Patrol accountable for its abuses against Haitian immigrants, and I’m demanding the Trump administration reinstate Temporary Protected Status for Haitian immigrants living in the U.S. I will never back down in the fight for immigrants’ rights, including Black immigrants’ rights. I also know that my job as a public servant includes helping my constituents through everyday challenges. In my district, my team and I created**three Neighborhood Service Centers that have resolved more than 11,000 constituent cases**. We’ve returned over $6 million back to district residents by helping them with back pay, Social Security and disability benefits, veterans’ benefits, debt reduction, and more. I’ve secured over $55 million in federal funding for 25 local community projects—including replacing dangerous lead pipes, providing affordable housing for predominantly Black youth, cleaning up industrial pollution, and expanding clinics in the effort toward health equity. I’m committed to dismantling systemic racism, including by investing in Black communities that have experienced years of disinvestment. I promise I will always push back against discriminatory policies and dehumanizing rhetoric. I will keep fighting to end poverty and defending vital programs nationwide like Job Corps, Head Start, Medicaid, and SNAP. And remember: Change doesn’t start from the Oval Office or the halls of Congress, it starts on the streets when the people demand it. I’m grateful for generations of Black activists who have expanded people’s rights and imaginations—and built transformative practices for community-led justice and care. We all deserve to live with dignity and safety. Together, we’ll keep demanding a government that invests in people’s needs rather than mass death, deportations, policing, and prisons. We will keep fighting for Black liberation and equity for all people. In solidarity, Rashida | [DONATE NOW](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvguEk3RN6gApuGFpihZET6vEMYafo3j3ZT7CL5BzPC-H1guD8tAEOvIGBce28XL3MVBg61dKlGEO7a-Qa7eEC26tA-DRcR1ZhjcZOJ1pl2Q1szoTvoFh_xe9MLQ31MGafagbW7rHFUZrI1fL4tJEJIlfBF6OIezMCYOqak1R_7ZHf9tnD4vOIoLCPw1tHurXyJXfEgo7yfaQ0qKUpcRcyeU9jqyYqFQHyeH3AoPW7MT_XDmrxodAtiEz8ISxrExu1V0ExuMlQc8Z0dtA8rC276KahIblUmHUHWcX-Yn6kD5XVccNp5Aeqml5k7yhDg5WXohcEr1StH3URIDcpEU-5QnWxmO7dfl_nyPrpm91knF8Ub8WHtUhRrZI89xvsiUn28nuge6mUVKLLqVWuLh_B-0YZCQ8tp2w6wrN5v6Pb8u99IMV3QdSPZhYoIvSUtwyNxy69Mdk4ibZ1yo1jMOVZ-5qd5Q7vM1Ch5syPYpEFRk7csq1GmVvqH1bG-87ZdlsKHKByNvzATWtTlXirKZqCdtOKX4H3-z5WbE1cj0R9IIaSj7Ez4d1aJjAXKnPQrHb7N-skOCWriZ-qRXkcE-LVOyr9xu9lNFcqHnRxJZVgEzw/4hh/pbKPEMUXQo2qL1MTpiXwMw/h1/h001.oVB7pZMazdrwKkbWOjZmqbLzH6DkO1_4kplf7K0Ogds)
---

I also created the bipartisan Get the Lead Out Caucus to push for urgent action to remove dangerous lead pipes across the country. We brought members of Congress to Michigan communities with lead contamination to hear directly from community leaders. Thousands of Detroiters have had their access to water shut off because of lead contamination or an inability to pay skyrocketing water bills. Early in the pandemic, I led successful efforts to secure a $1.1 billion water assistance fund to prevent water shutoffs and help people with water costs. I’m still leading policies to ensure that utilities—like water and power—are treated like the human rights they are. Here are some relevant policies I’ve also introduced: The Justice for All Act,**which would restore and strengthen the protections in our country’s crucial civil rights laws. It would combat intentional and unintentional discrimination** against people based on race, color, religion, sex, disability, age, or national origin—clarifying the definition of “race” to include race-related traits like hair texture. It would also prohibit racial profiling in police activity and end qualified immunity for government employees such as police officers. The Prohibit Auto Insurance Discrimination Act,which would stop auto insurance companies from using anti-Black, predatory practices to set auto insurance rates based on income, credit score, education levels, zip code, and other racial proxies unrelated to driving history. Despite being one of the most impoverished areas in the country, metro Detroit’s car insurance rates are the most expensive in the country. Auto insurance discrimination keeps people in the cycle of poverty. The Cumulative Impacts Act**, which would address environmental racism and block corporate polluters from continuing to treat Black and brown communities as dumping grounds. The**BOOST Act, which would uplift 45% of people living in poverty instantly by providing refundable tax credits to individuals and families. The End Childhood Poverty Act,**which would create a universal, monthly child allowance of over $400 per child —including the lowest-wealth families who are traditionally left out of the tax credit system. The Dismantle Mass Incarceration for Public Health Act**, which would release eligible people from jails, prisons, and detention centers during the pandemic. The Public Banking Act,**which would expand access to banking, credit, and loans for marginalized people who have been systematically shut out of our nation’s private banks. The _Tenant Empowerment Act**, which would strengthen HUD tenant protections and provide tenants with the tools necessary to improve the quality of their homes. The Medical Debt Cancellation Act , which would eliminate all medical debt held by millions of Americans. The Public Housing for the 21st Century Act , which would increase urgently-needed affordable housing production. I will keep pushing for racial and economic justice. And I’ll continue pushing back against the White-Supremacist-in-Chief’s racist attacks on our communities—such as his travel ban on Black and brown people from Africa, the Caribbean, and the Middle East. I’m co-leading the NO BAN Actto block travel bans , and I’m speaking out about how our country’s anti-Black racism and policing leads to disproportionate detention and deportation rates for Black immigrants. I’ve held Customs and Border Patrol accountable for its abuses against Haitian immigrants, and I’m demanding the Trump administration reinstate Temporary Protected Status for Haitian immigrants living in the U.S. I will never back down in the fight for immigrants’ rights, including Black immigrants’ rights. I also know that my job as a public servant includes helping my constituents through everyday challenges. In my district, my team and I createdthree Neighborhood Service Centers that have resolved more than 11,000 constituent cases. We’ve returned over $6 million back to district residents by helping them with back pay, Social Security and disability benefits, veterans’ benefits, debt reduction, and more. I’ve secured over $55 million in federal funding for 25 local community projects—including replacing dangerous lead pipes, providing affordable housing for predominantly Black youth, cleaning up industrial pollution, and expanding clinics in the effort toward health equity. I’m committed to dismantling systemic racism, including by investing in Black communities that have experienced years of disinvestment. I promise I will always push back against discriminatory policies and dehumanizing rhetoric. I will keep fighting to end poverty and defending vital programs nationwide like Job Corps, Head Start, Medicaid, and SNAP. And remember: Change doesn’t start from the Oval Office or the halls of Congress, it starts on the streets when the people demand it. I’m grateful for generations of Black activists who have expanded people’s rights and imaginations—and built transformative practices for community-led justice and care. We all deserve to live with dignity and safety. Together, we’ll keep demanding a government that invests in people’s needs rather than mass death, deportations, policing, and prisons. We will keep fighting for Black liberation and equity for all people. In solidarity, Rashida | Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . subject: What Juneteenth means to me this year
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Foushee for Congress

Today, we celebrate our progress. Tomorrow, we continue our fight.

If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/9_07fM1dnEmqdVR1wv8kcQ2?unsubscribedata=hdrmaySk0V%2B2F%2BR5iC02L03qFqCE8iBQJ%2FXRBZwuZNZOD7d75ShQpI2cDSLwJa0G8KDj9eCVT%2Boxg7qO85E56%2FK9k%2FK7GhUdcLN%2Fhi%2FTCQ9AhL8DGki8ZDWnrGpZcxl1l%2BKaJlzxQZK0J872jwPjEISmqLEPiZXubL5wT1TEqnXimA3D3%2FyWVGgMY9O0fUT8F2AmAICxJ44GBFbyJzZ4GvrFTP1FSfQ08ReTW8AKX0ArXBzcLY49BMan%2BANTlfpWKrQkG3XFxrQOepBqxePLIiy%2BsTCPpXOcLCn%2BEz7RmA3FtiLKVSOnoiaXp5Yi1NY6OduTCkRACtX5Xhf75cdE92taqk2rUmuVeW%2B5H7Oakfq8fJIRdeXGcJ%2FCcmGDAuThHHympEyuYqSkVhwYHVm9%2FR4jw5HSaAsa0Hafw30E3ZI%3D)

---
Peter, I wanted to take a moment and reflect with you on what Juneteenth means to me today - the progress we have made and the progress we will continue to make.

**A little bit about my story:** I grew up during segregation in North Carolina. During much of my childhood, we were often not allowed to coexist in the same spaces as white people - whether it be at school, in a restaurant, at church, and more. We were taught not even to look white folks directly in the eye. **We’ve all heard the phrase “separate but equal” - but I can attest that _nothing_ was equal.**

I only started attending school with white classmates for the first time when I was 13 years old. Despite our new books and friends, the transition from segregation to integration was far from equitable. We conducted sit-ins to advocate for the allowance of Black students into the cheer squad, reliable buses to and from school, and for at least one Black marshall at graduation. Progress was slow, but we kept advocating for ourselves.

**Mine is just one of many stories which demonstrate progress and the power of many. As we commemorate the emancipation of enslaved Black people in America today, it is critical that we celebrate the progress we have made and will continue to make.**

It is important, today more than ever before, that we reflect on our history so as to not repeat the errors of our past. We are living in a moment where the rights and freedoms of marginalized people in this country are being threatened by the Trump administration. Rather than cowering, we can use our history to rise up and continue to fight for progress not only for Black people, but for all of us. **We have the power of many on our side - we can show them that we are bigger than our differences and fight for what is right.**

I taught my children to look people in the eyes, to smile at them and offer a handshake, instead of conditioning them to look away. I want them to teach their children the same, and their children’s children after them. I want them to know that we have much more in common than we have differences, no matter the color of their skin.

We know we still have plenty of work to do, and I’m honored to be doing that work with you and for you in Congress. Today, we will celebrate the progress we have made and how far we have come in our fight for racial justice. Tomorrow, we will continue our work.

Thank you for taking the time out of your day to reflect on Juneteenth with me.

– Valerie Foushee

A little bit about my story: I grew up during segregation in North Carolina. During much of my childhood, we were often not allowed to coexist in the same spaces as white people - whether it be at school, in a restaurant, at church, and more. We were taught not even to look white folks directly in the eye. We’ve all heard the phrase “separate but equal” - but I can attest that nothing was equal. I only started attending school with white classmates for the first time when I was 13 years old. Despite our new books and friends, the transition from segregation to integration was far from equitable. We conducted sit-ins to advocate for the allowance of Black students into the cheer squad, reliable buses to and from school, and for at least one Black marshall at graduation. Progress was slow, but we kept advocating for ourselves. Mine is just one of many stories which demonstrate progress and the power of many. As we commemorate the emancipation of enslaved Black people in America today, it is critical that we celebrate the progress we have made and will continue to make. It is important, today more than ever before, that we reflect on our history so as to not repeat the errors of our past. We are living in a moment where the rights and freedoms of marginalized people in this country are being threatened by the Trump administration. Rather than cowering, we can use our history to rise up and continue to fight for progress not only for Black people, but for all of us. We have the power of many on our side - we can show them that we are bigger than our differences and fight for what is right. I taught my children to look people in the eyes, to smile at them and offer a handshake, instead of conditioning them to look away. I want them to teach their children the same, and their children’s children after them. I want them to know that we have much more in common than we have differences, no matter the color of their skin. We know we still have plenty of work to do, and I’m honored to be doing that work with you and for you in Congress. Today, we will celebrate the progress we have made and how far we have come in our fight for racial justice. Tomorrow, we will continue our work. Thank you for taking the time out of your day to reflect on Juneteenth with me. – Valerie Foushee * | | | ---|---|--- = Valerie is fighting to protect access to health care, working families, and to reform our criminal justice system. But she needs your help to continue to do this great work in Congress. Your donation right now will help protect Democrat Valerie Foushee by giving us a jumpstart on the next cycle. | = = Paid for by Foushee for Congress Valerie Foushee for Congress PO Box 16446 Chapel Hill, NC 27516 United States If you need to you can unsubscribe, You can also =.

name: Sarah McBride
subject: On this day in 1865
disclaimer_text: Paid for by McBride for Delaware
body:
I believe it is our duty to meet this moment with the same courage and conviction as the generations before us.
#
Happy Juneteenth!
On this day in 1865, Union soldiers arrived at the last stronghold of the Confederacy with the news that President Lincoln had decreed all enslaved African Americans to be free. While the Emancipation Proclamation technically went into effect in 1863, it took more than two years for the news to reach all parts of the country.
Today, we celebrate emancipation and freedom, and honor the countless courageous civil rights activists and leaders who have fought for freedom in the face of prejudice and oppression. **We are reminded that our freedoms must be actively defended if they're to remain a reality — an important reminder in today’s world**.
I believe it is our duty to meet this moment with the same courage, conviction, and moral clarity as the generations before us. To listen, to learn, and most importantly, to act.
Let’s celebrate today with joy and recommit ourselves to the struggle for justice that endures.
In solidarity,
Sarah McBride
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/mcb-em-footer?refcode=em20250619-1&t=1&refcode2=2112_21716_D5rDVv&akid=2112%2E21716%2ED5rDVv)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Jay Jones HQ here.
We're incredibly grateful for the support Virginians showed throughout the primary – and we're more ready than ever to elect Jay as the next Attorney General of Virginia!!
But there is NO time to rest: We set a $50,000 fundraising goal for the first 3 days of the general election and that deadline is coming up tomorrow night. It’s never been more critical that we meet this goal.
MAGA-aligned Jason Miyares is failing to represent Virginians’ best interests. He’s a Trump loyalist who has let extremist policies negatively impact Virginia’s workers and economy.
But Jay has spent his career fighting for voting rights and other freedoms – and he’ll keep that fight going as Attorney General.
The Commonwealth needs a leader who will defend it from Trump and his far-right agenda. But Miyares is being bankrolled by ultra-wealthy conservatives, so we’re counting on you to help power Jay to victory.
Please chip in $10 or more today to help us hit our General Election Launch Fund goal before tomorrow’s deadline and prove the strength of our grassroots campaign. Every dollar makes a big difference! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_jones_gen-election-launch_em_q22025?amount=10]

**Gender ideology is on the ropes.** Now we have to keep the momentum going and finish the job. Several cases and many clients are still waiting for justice from the lies of gender ideology, including:
* **Selina Soule** --she and several other female athletes have been standing up for fairness on and off the field for years. We've beaten back efforts to rewrite Title IX at the federal level, but many states, like Connecticut, continue to push policies that force girls to compete against male athletes.

clean_body: Hey - this is Monica De La Cruz. I wanted you to hear this from me: Bernie Sanders is flying all the way to my district in South Texas to rally socialists to defeat me! Bless his heart... Democrats have been seething since I flipped a liberal district for the first time in 100 years. Now they're pulling out all the stops to claw it back - including sending in the socialists to destroy my campaign. Make no mistake: This move changes everything. Bernie's visit is a rallying cry for the far-left radicals who want to turn our state blue and change America as a whole. We can't let that happen. I know personally what it takes to defeat the Democrats in South Texas. For the last five years, I've been on the front lines, fighting for our values and way of life in a purple district. And I can tell you that this is a battle we can't afford to lose. won't sit back and let the Democrats waltz into my backyard without a fight. But I need every single Patriot reading this email to help me mobilize our grassroots army, shut down their agenda before it takes root, and send a clear message that they're not welcome here. For Texas and America,Monica De La Cruz * Democrats already pledged up to FIFTY MILLION dollars to wipe me out for being a MAGA Latina in a purple district... And that was all BEFORE I was added to this new target list! There's still time to right the ship as long as we move right now. - Monica De La Cruz| | | | | Paid for by Monica for Congress PO Box 4605 McAllen, Texas 78502 To unsubscribe from Monica for Congress |

name: Senator Steve Daines
subject: I humiliated Chuck Schumer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana
body:

NEXT, National Democrats dropped a WHOPPING $5 MILLION into the race. NOW, Winsome's opponent, Abigail Spanberger, OUTRAISED her 2:1! Peter - This can't wait another second! Do you see how serious this is? Abigail Spanberger has billionaires on her side. Not to mention the liberal media lives in her pocket. But the Swamp is fighting back.**
**The radical Left, the Deep State, and their media allies know they can 't win on ideas. So they're trying to cut us off at the knees - starting in key battleground states like Ohio.**

## **THE****WHITE HOUSE!**

Friend,
It is my Great Honor to announce that I just installed two beautiful Flag Poles on both sides of the White House, North and South Lawns.
[](https://email.win.donaldjtrump.com/c/eJw8kUGP2ywQhn8NvhGZwYHk4EO-z3Gltlq12qq76sUiMMREgKmNE21_fWU3yfXlmeF9QIXknf2oifyPAPTOTASA8AMBOHyHt9cfeERJzVkcqDri55flFP6_o1d4wlGr5F_6r2LUt-n3Gzvqy68_w5U9-fyR8Enfb91gUM4vqWwKU4PegdYF1kxuS76VfM-KvmZSaFEKrLAUttKnShpm98h3e8aZkKfC1VDCthRsz3bAy_1mW1WIwtrqxMDshCBVeXNxY4aovLnkcQ5po4dQ-LrPOU1rpZZAO6GeR9zcXBzRLASBdqVpVNkt01QPIbicEenFagKtT7S3dNWg1qszTYPHiV4Z4e2cQxfQuDkQ3txNxRJOwzxqJLzxqfOp6-091yok5c6R8OZh1OWou2XxY2_X286spTqfHnNDzBgz4c2imJGA6KJ3hvCmfVev8_uXc_8vXP-XNz-_jYOTn7aiGGuTbs57NwlJqvK8tFzf5lrD3wAAAP__LferMA)
It is a GIFT from me of something which was always missing from this magnificent place.

These are the most magnificent poles made – they are tall, tapered, rust proof, rope inside the pole, and of the highest quality.
Hopefully, they will proudly stand at both sides of the White House for many years to come!
**Here’s why I did it:**
For far too long, Americans have abandoned their love for this country - **ABANDONED PATRIOTISM!**
It is high time that we started loving and honoring our flag again - more importantly, OUR NATION.
With that in mind, I have directed my team to create the GREAT AMERICA PATRIOT SURVEY, and I want everyone reading this message to answer it!

Here’s why I did it: For far too long, Americans have abandoned their love for this country - ABANDONED PATRIOTISM! It is high time that we started loving and honoring our flag again - more importantly, OUR NATION. With that in mind, I have directed my team to create the GREAT AMERICA PATRIOT SURVEY, and I want everyone reading this message to answer it! With the Golden Age of America just beginning - because of ME and YOU - I need True Trump Patriots advising me. I’m making many VERY IMPORTANT decisions right now, and I don’t want to take action without hearing from the people that I care about the most - YOU! Thank you, President Donald J. Friend,

_Have you seen what 's happening in Los Angeles?!_

The city is on fire, ICE agents and local law enforcement are under attack, and Democrats are siding with the rioters.

What's happening in Los Angeles is NOT a protest. It is an entire city declaring open rebellion against American sovereignty and authority.

Frankly, I've had it.

If you burn or spit on our flag, I want you _out_ of our country.
If you're an illegal alien, I want ICE to _remove and deport_ you.
If you're an American _citizen_ who thinks Mexican culture is preferable, _please_ GET OUT.

This is a preview of our future if we DO NOT defeat Democrats.

Friend, _THIS_ is why it is CRITICAL that we Build the Red Wall now to ensure America First victories in 2026, 2028, and beyond. This craziness cannot be allowed to fester any longer.

[Please, stand with me — stand FOR America First — today by donating $50, $250, $500, or even just $10 to help Turning Point PAC fund new field operations in key battleground states like Arizona, Nevada, and New Hampshire! >>](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=413b6bdcf6084e2eb1eea5adb8487ac9&_e=86_Bx6q2DfQ0CZeF9mCoctk4waIuqYMSOgfqjAXz8mSmUQktnyGAMbCNa8yT8A9zi7fQ8wRiEIgj5zjtWaLN8a4MPQkowNUQt3dJYlEfzqfnHa3DLe4_mrbD9rGgQrPpHLGuvK-TRo6Fka5VtcPJinWHcIgowLj8aJu5u8vSmOFgeKYoTdvtJIfoc7vV4-A8ZHa9JQKHSL9Zd2hAjCm9UAiiWxiQ7I0YnRV5EO8nSFsRwF_PPaWroAacxX2EiYHjIg9S-0gVPTerzVzMxedxxjYFK_MiqSLGmrieL34eYcdHLqbFKQYdIr-WUxPJzCodH9g8qL9pjzjvfogQIAdukdWMS69ld_cKporgSpsy9ZqTcjzmWSN8j3YbmbQhDr50HJZqNo2cU0nyp-qEtpGyGn6LDYm3bxClB6wo3brXgES6ZwrIRUKOG4AmlQhfQ0DwYY6oYIdbdex7jlJ_pCYbwaPDgRyWSZXPF_d5dVg2OEw_dFfjK51rRtZZpvLHyutdZMoT_MxKuCsUtdcxwlKtTSwr2QMwIp6BHEvnovqsRZO3oqIn75G0MRVeV-CBMiFyPDoYFL0d1Y5tG_6wjVWhdzQWg85BArNfJVcLJQRntr0%3D)

President Trump was elected to close the border and restore law and order. If we fail, our nation will be permanently populated by foreigners who hate us and owe their allegiance to another country.

As patriots, we CANNOT stand by and let this happen.

President Trump is doing his part, leading the way on mass deportations, calling for the National Guard to be deployed, and standing firm in the face of arrows being hurled toward him from every side.

Now, it's time for us to do our part...

And that starts by building a winning ground game to Build the Red Wall.

Together, we CAN take our country back, restore law and order, and rebuild our civilization around our shared _America First values_.

_**Are you with me, Friend?**_

Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.'s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees)

As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information.

Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law.

Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Contributions to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account are not tax deductible for federal income tax purposes.

clean_body: Friend, Have you seen what 's happening in Los Angeles?! The city is on fire, ICE agents and local law enforcement are under attack, and Democrats are siding with the rioters. What's happening in Los Angeles is NOT a protest. It is an entire city declaring open rebellion against American sovereignty and authority. Frankly, I've had it. If you burn or spit on our flag, I want you out of our country. If you're an illegal alien, I want ICE to remove and deport you. If you're an American citizen who thinks Mexican culture is preferable, please GET OUT. This is a preview of our future if we DO NOT defeat Democrats. Friend, THIS is why it is CRITICAL that we Build the Red Wall now to ensure America First victories in 2026, 2028, and beyond. This craziness cannot be allowed to fester any longer. President Trump was elected to close the border and restore law and order. If we fail, our nation will be permanently populated by foreigners who hate us and owe their allegiance to another country. As patriots, we CANNOT stand by and let this happen. President Trump is doing his part, leading the way on mass deportations, calling for the National Guard to be deployed, and standing firm in the face of arrows being hurled toward him from every side. Now, it's time for us to do our part... And that starts by building a winning ground game to Build the Red Wall. Together, we CAN take our country back, restore law and order, and rebuild our civilization around our shared America First values. Are you with me, Friend? Failure is NOT an option. Thank you for standing with TurningPoint PAC in support of President Trump's America First agenda. It's going to take each and every one of us, but together we CAN and WILL win the war for Western Civilization. For Freedom and America First, Charlie Kirk Turning Point PAC Non-Contribution Account (For TTPAC, Inc.'s Expenditures Other Than Direct Contributions to Federal Committees) As permitted by law, TTPAC, Inc. maintains a non-contribution account for the purpose of influencing federal, state, and local elections. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, address, occupation, and employer of individuals whose contribution exceeds $200 in an election cycle. For contributions from federal committees, corporations, and labor unions - regardless of contribution amount - federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the committee's name and address. State and local law may require us to collect and report additional information. Contributions to this account may be used to influence federal elections by making independent expenditures in support of or in opposition to federal candidates. Additionally, in state and local jurisdictions that permit non-individual type (e.g., corporate) contributions directly to candidates and/or committees, contributions to this account may be used for both independent expenditures and direct contributions to candidates and/or committees, as permitted by state and local law. Individuals, federal committees, corporations, and labor unions may contribute to TTPAC, Inc.'s non-contribution account without limit. Contributions by national banks, foreign nationals (without lawful permanent resident status), and other foreign entities (e.g., foreign corporations) are prohibited. Dear Peter,
---
It’s going to be a powerful summer as we kick off a coast-to-coast Canadian Revival Tour of the Great White North! Let Us Worship has a long history of holding revivals across the globe, but one of our favorite places on earth is right next door. **EAST COAST****July 23** 🇨🇦 Halifax**July 24** 🇨🇦 Charlottetown**July 24** 🇨🇦 Moncton**July 25** 🇨🇦 Quebec City **July 26** 🇨🇦 Ottawa**July 27** 🇨🇦 Toronto **WEST COAST****Aug 20** 🇨🇦 Winnipeg**Aug 21** 🇨🇦 Saskatoon**Aug 22** 🇨🇦 Edmonton**Aug 23** 🇨🇦 Kelowna **Aug 24** 🇨🇦 Abbotsford We keep coming back to Canada because we’ve seen our Canadian brothers and sisters—desperate for revival to sweep across the country—come out in massive numbers! Watch an amazing clip from our revival in Toronto!
---

name: George Whitesides
subject: Updated survey for Peter
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gw-q2-2025?refcode=em-header [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gw-q2-2025?refcode=em-header]
These last few months have been challenging, Peter. I know you feel it, too.

House Republicans are enabling Trump’s scorched-earth agenda – voting to gut billions from vital programs including Medicaid and SNAP. And the White House continues to sow division across the country, in recent days right here in Southern California.

I want you to know that no matter what is thrown at us, I have this district’s best interests at heart. I’m fighting for our community at every turn – through legislation, discussions with local leaders, and working against Trump’s corruption.

While the stakes continue to grow higher by the day, I’ve rarely seen more people engaged in our political process and ready to get to work than right now. It’s a great sign that together, we can work toward meaningful progress at home and in Washington.

That’s why today, instead of asking you to contribute toward my re-election campaign, I’m asking you to take a minute to complete our June priorities survey. Your input directly impacts our next steps – can I count on your response today?

Begin the survey >> https://secure.ngpvan.com/7BBRQwqiskm1MxCFBvpawg2?refcode=em-6.19.25-all-a

Thank you,

George
https://www.facebook.com/whitesidesforcongress/? [https://www.facebook.com/whitesidesforcongress/?] https://www.instagram.com/whitesidesforcongress/? [https://www.instagram.com/whitesidesforcongress/?] https://twitter.com/gtwhitesides? [https://twitter.com/gtwhitesides?]George has spent his career solving problems. Now, he’s ready to deliver for the people of California's 27th Congressional District. Chip in to George's campaign today. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gw-q2-2025?refcode=em-footer]
To learn more about George and his commitment to working families, please visit: GeorgeWhitesides.com [https://www.georgewhitesides.com/?]
We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/7Lng7fM74EmfvnJSCPSYrg2?unsubscribedata=kdnw5OSaXBfw4C3PHLayOknpsY0%2FVvD7m7z487tbPwmOpXKiUy6%2B4sx9El%2FR6Z0KKtTV8fILPDClbMNE3dhovN%2BchBerI7zkb8H8HaSQjKxvIRMbU%2B%2Fe3eHtyw9zK5fwwoN50aa%2B%2FKp9QMkHkKpX8WjMPTCOoRhtSRNBgcSfktdGNaoso%2FtajGjwtcMDUbh%2BS0r5LBcc%2B1i564sTYXJ2B1rYGKc6SWHdah68qv927bQaT8i1xTPrdXmNxlPA%2FVd6xF4WoDIcWoJcaq75k9dxr5%2BzTTfPkWKKlwQfoAPddVTnB3D0xPB8yV4OSdnC4F%2BD0OSKju4op5dvLslCAjFvmculJCPdjovpWwwk2U9zqgeDMSR7pZvjZZBwa68JWzgExA5uN1AuaL7PqthtxgddpHYE7KP0eZBAaGZOav8CalX3vgkiPZeI%2F4dMXL%2F6WZe5 .

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2364d0fd-0151-4e59-be30-7c1e09e0fc53?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
**[Click here to see the original post on X](https://substack.com/redirect/c1939a16-46e6-4e2e-a172-a42f8867a380?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

------------**
Well, it's NOT because...
Club for Growth said I'm one of the **highest-rated Republican senators driving economic growth** in Idaho...
Or because CPAC gave me the**"Award for Conservative Excellence"** in 2025 and 2024...
Or because Attorney General Raul Labrador endorsed me in 2024, saying,** "Idaho simply can't afford to lose him."**
Or because I'm endorsed and supported by all the pro-life groups.
Or because a group called the "Institute for Legislative Analysis" gave me the **"Champion of Limited Government Award"** for being one of the few pushing back against big government in Idaho.
Or because last year the Canyon County Republican Central Committee gave me the **" Canyon County Republican of the Year" **award.
I could go on, but you get the idea.
Sure, most conservative groups and individuals think I'm doing a good job.
_But that's not why you should keep me in the Idaho Senate (per se)._
> You should keep me here because I actually fight to CONSERVE things like borders, language, and culture (you know, the whole point of being a Conservative)?
No, I'm not here to "get loud," flip over tables, or burn the place down like some deranged anarchist or an open-borders pro drug Libertarian (we have to live here, after all).
I'm here to preserve American culture, law and order, and traditional family values.
And to prove it, here's just SOME of what I've achieved since taking office in 2023:
## Some ACHIEVEMENTS From the 2025 Legislative Session:
1. Sponsored a bill combating lawfare (i.e. SLAPP lawsuits) to protect free speech **(S1001) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
2. Sponsored a bill to permanently ban mask mandates**
(H32) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
3. Sponsored a bill to reduce power of Idaho's public health districts
**(S1031) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
4. Sponsored a bill to curb Health & Welfare bureaucrats' overreach after a $15 million child care budget overrun**
(H90) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
5. Sponsored a bill to make Dept. of Health & Welfare bureau chiefs "fireable" **(S1052) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
6. Sponsored several transparency and accountability bills to keep government efficient**
(H312, H215, H222, H113, H358) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
7. Sponsored an E-Verify bill to require employers to verify immigration status, but it didn't receive a hearing
**(H252) BLOCKED**
8. Sponsored a bill mandating Idaho law enforcement to pursue 287G status for ICE deputization. We had President Trump's clear directive to deport illegals, yet, the bill gained no ground in the House
**BLOCKED**
9. Co-sponsored the "Medical Ethics Defense Act"
**(H59a) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
10. Co-sponsored a bill preventing indecent exposure (e.g. "Nampa PRIDE")
**(H270) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
11. Co-sponsored a bill to make Ivermectin available over the counter
**(S1211) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
12. Passed a school choice tax credit, empowering parents over bureaucrats (H93) SIGNED INTO LAW!
13. Passed a bill requiring parents to opt in to sex education, not opt out
**(H239) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
14. Passed a bill to keep men out of women's locker rooms
**(H264) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
15. Mandated the death penalty for pedophiles
**(H380) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
16. Made firing squad the #1 method for death penalty cases
**(H37) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
17. Fixed vaccine exemptions to favor parents
**(S1179) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
18. Passed the "Medicaid Reform and Cost Containment Act"
**(H345) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
19. Raised the grocery tax credit to reduce taxes by over $400 million
**(H231) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
20. Passed the "Idaho Medical Freedom Act" to protect Idahoans in public and private sectors from forced medical mandates
**(S1210) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
21. Eliminated DEI practices in Idaho higher education
**(S1198) SIGNED INTO LAW!**

## Highlights From the 2024 Legislative Session:
1. I sponsored a bill to lead the charge against international threats by sponsoring a bill that stops foreign entities, specifically China, from stealing American genetic information (the first bill like this in the nation)
**(H670a) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
2. I sponsored a bill to take a proactive stance against the adoption of Socialist "Universal Basic Income" programs in Idaho's cities and counties, ensuring our state remains a beacon of individual responsibility and free-market capitalism **(H572a) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
3. We empowered our police and legal teams with greater authority in sentencing fentanyl traffickers, taking a firm stand against this deadly crisis
**(H406) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
4. We revolutionized how we handle our state's budget. For the first time in decades, we're breaking it down piece by piece, moving away from the all-or-nothing approach (this change ensures more thoughtful and effective use of our resources)
**INTRODUCED ACCOUNTABILITY!**
5. I was the deciding vote to kill a bill that would've made it nearly impossible for parents to remove obscene content from libraries
**(S1289) STOPPED A BAD BILL!**
6. We also killed a bill that would have granted Big Pharma legal immunity from the consequences of their products
**(S1245) STOPPED A BAD BILL!**
7. I supported a bill to shield children from obscene materials in libraries
**(HB710) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
8. I backed a bill to define terms like male, female, and related terms, asserting that gender and sex are synonymous
**(HB421) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
9. I voted to safeguard the right of students and government employees to opt out of using someone's "preferred pronouns"
**(HB 538) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
10. I defended property owners from being compelled to engage in any federal housing assistance programs (aka rent control) - a HUGE victory for Idaho conservatives
**(HB545) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
11. I fought tooth and nail against bills draining taxpayer dollars into social justice and DEI positions across institutional offices. This fight included pushing back on funding for gender equity centers, women's centers, LGBTQ offices, cultural centers, and offices of equity and inclusion, along with **House Bill 722** --the governor's free college scheme I see as a leap towards socialism
12. I stood firm with "Defend The Guard" because Idaho's National Guard should never be chess pieces in never-ending, pointless wars. Time to guard our own ground, not police the world. Enough's enough. **(S1252)**
13. All session long, I fought like hell for parental rights in education, health care, and every other area of life where the government is trying to encroach on parents.

## Highlights From My First Session in 2023:
1. Sponsored a bill to prevent discrimination based on vaccine status, ensuring CPS cannot investigate or determine parental rights based on a child's vaccine status **(S1029) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
2. Co-sponsored a bill to protect Idaho gun owners from ESG-driven "backdoor registry" efforts, upholding Second Amendment rights
**(H295) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
3. Co-sponsored a bill to deliver property tax relief to Idaho homeowners, reducing tax burdens
**(H292) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
4. Supported a bill to protect Idaho farmlands from foreign, particularly Chinese, land purchases, affirming national security and economic autonomy
**(H173a) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
5. Supported a bill to ensure that public schools maintain separate bathrooms, locker rooms, showers, dressing areas, and overnight accommodations for biological boys and biological girls.
**(SB1100) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
6. Supported a bill that reinforces the fundamental rights and responsibilities of parents or legal guardians as primary stakeholders to make decisions regarding the upbringing of their child
**(H163) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
7. Supported a bill to reject ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) agendas, defending Idaho's fiscal sovereignty and economic freedom
**(H191) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
8. Supported bills to create a revolving loan fund for public charter schools, promoting choice and competition in education
**(S1043, H0113) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
9. Supported a bill to promote sexual abstinence education in Idaho schools, fostering responsibility and family values
**(H228) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
10. Supported a bill to prohibit ranked choice voting, protecting Idaho elections from manipulation
**(H179) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
11. Supported a bill to prohibit transgender surgeries for minors, protecting children from irreversible procedures
**(H71a) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
12. Supported a bill to reject COVID-19 vaccine mandates, upholding medical freedom
**(S1130) SIGNED INTO LAW!**
13. Fought for fiscal restraint against Idaho's largest spending bills, challenging excessive government spending in committee and on the Senate floor
14. Supported a bill to ensure public libraries are free of pornography, protecting Idaho's youth from harmful material
**(H314a) VETOED BY GOVERNOR!**
15. Sponsored a bill to strike the Blaine Amendment from Idaho's state constitution, opposing religious discrimination to support educational freedom and parental choice for religious schools
**(RS30025) BLOCKED!**

Pretty cool, right?
My point?
I'm not here to make a mess of the place or climb a political ladder (gross). And I'm not here to serve bureaucrats, the Governor, or the lobbyist class either (equally revolting).
_**I'm here to make Idaho a conservative haven that values law and order, traditional family values, and America First principles. Full-stop.**_
And as Idaho's conservative heart beats stronger every year, I'm 100% committed to that.
But if you're still here and grumbling about the state's path without stepping up to make a difference, I hear Portland's always an option if you're looking for a change of scenery.

Sponsored a bill combating lawfare (i.e. SLAPP lawsuits) to protect free speech (S1001) SIGNED INTO LAW! 2. Sponsored a bill to permanently ban mask mandates (H32) SIGNED INTO LAW! 3. Sponsored a bill to reduce power of Idaho's public health districts (S1031) SIGNED INTO LAW! 4. Sponsored a bill to curb Health & Welfare bureaucrats' overreach after a $15 million child care budget overrun (H90) SIGNED INTO LAW! 5. Sponsored a bill to make Dept. of Health & Welfare bureau chiefs "fireable" (S1052) SIGNED INTO LAW! 6. Sponsored several transparency and accountability bills to keep government efficient (H312, H215, H222, H113, H358) SIGNED INTO LAW! 7. Sponsored an E-Verify bill to require employers to verify immigration status, but it didn't receive a hearing (H252) BLOCKED 8. Sponsored a bill mandating Idaho law enforcement to pursue 287G status for ICE deputization. We had President Trump's clear directive to deport illegals, yet, the bill gained no ground in the House BLOCKED 9. Co-sponsored the "Medical Ethics Defense Act" (H59a) SIGNED INTO LAW! 10. Co-sponsored a bill preventing indecent exposure (e.g. "Nampa PRIDE") (H270) SIGNED INTO LAW! 11. Co-sponsored a bill to make Ivermectin available over the counter (S1211) SIGNED INTO LAW! 12. Passed a school choice tax credit, empowering parents over bureaucrats (H93) SIGNED INTO LAW! 13. Passed a bill requiring parents to opt in to sex education, not opt out (H239) SIGNED INTO LAW! 14. Passed a bill to keep men out of women's locker rooms (H264) SIGNED INTO LAW! 15. Mandated the death penalty for pedophiles (H380) SIGNED INTO LAW! 16. Made firing squad the #1 method for death penalty cases (H37) SIGNED INTO LAW! 17. Fixed vaccine exemptions to favor parents (S1179) SIGNED INTO LAW! 18. Passed the "Medicaid Reform and Cost Containment Act" (H345) SIGNED INTO LAW! 19. Raised the grocery tax credit to reduce taxes by over $400 million (H231) SIGNED INTO LAW! 20. Passed the "Idaho Medical Freedom Act" to protect Idahoans in public and private sectors from forced medical mandates (S1210) SIGNED INTO LAW! 21. Eliminated DEI practices in Idaho higher education (S1198) SIGNED INTO LAW! Highlights From the 2024 Legislative Session: 1. I sponsored a bill to lead the charge against international threats by sponsoring a bill that stops foreign entities, specifically China, from stealing American genetic information (the first bill like this in the nation) (H670a) SIGNED INTO LAW! 2. I sponsored a bill to take a proactive stance against the adoption of Socialist "Universal Basic Income" programs in Idaho's cities and counties, ensuring our state remains a beacon of individual responsibility and free-market capitalism (H572a) SIGNED INTO LAW! 3. We empowered our police and legal teams with greater authority in sentencing fentanyl traffickers, taking a firm stand against this deadly crisis (H406) SIGNED INTO LAW! 4. We revolutionized how we handle our state's budget. For the first time in decades, we're breaking it down piece by piece, moving away from the all-or-nothing approach (this change ensures more thoughtful and effective use of our resources) INTRODUCED ACCOUNTABILITY! 5. I was the deciding vote to kill a bill that would've made it nearly impossible for parents to remove obscene content from libraries (S1289) STOPPED A BAD BILL! 6. We also killed a bill that would have granted Big Pharma legal immunity from the consequences of their products (S1245) STOPPED A BAD BILL! 7. I supported a bill to shield children from obscene materials in libraries (HB710) SIGNED INTO LAW! 8. I backed a bill to define terms like male, female, and related terms, asserting that gender and sex are synonymous (HB421) SIGNED INTO LAW! 9. I voted to safeguard the right of students and government employees to opt out of using someone's "preferred pronouns" (HB 538) SIGNED INTO LAW! 10. I defended property owners from being compelled to engage in any federal housing assistance programs (aka rent control) - a HUGE victory for Idaho conservatives (HB545) SIGNED INTO LAW! 11. I fought tooth and nail against bills draining taxpayer dollars into social justice and DEI positions across institutional offices. This fight included pushing back on funding for gender equity centers, women's centers, LGBTQ offices, cultural centers, and offices of equity and inclusion, along with House Bill 722 --the governor's free college scheme I see as a leap towards socialism 12. I stood firm with "Defend The Guard" because Idaho's National Guard should never be chess pieces in never-ending, pointless wars. Time to guard our own ground, not police the world. Enough's enough. (S1252) 13. All session long, I fought like hell for parental rights in education, health care, and every other area of life where the government is trying to encroach on parents. Highlights From My First Session in 2023: 1. Sponsored a bill to prevent discrimination based on vaccine status, ensuring CPS cannot investigate or determine parental rights based on a child's vaccine status (S1029) SIGNED INTO LAW! 2. Co-sponsored a bill to protect Idaho gun owners from ESG-driven "backdoor registry" efforts, upholding Second Amendment rights (H295) SIGNED INTO LAW! 3. Co-sponsored a bill to deliver property tax relief to Idaho homeowners, reducing tax burdens (H292) SIGNED INTO LAW! 4. Supported a bill to protect Idaho farmlands from foreign, particularly Chinese, land purchases, affirming national security and economic autonomy (H173a) SIGNED INTO LAW! 5. Supported a bill to ensure that public schools maintain separate bathrooms, locker rooms, showers, dressing areas, and overnight accommodations for biological boys and biological girls. (SB1100) SIGNED INTO LAW! 6. Supported a bill that reinforces the fundamental rights and responsibilities of parents or legal guardians as primary stakeholders to make decisions regarding the upbringing of their child (H163) SIGNED INTO LAW! 7. Supported a bill to reject ESG (Environmental, Social, Governance) agendas, defending Idaho's fiscal sovereignty and economic freedom (H191) SIGNED INTO LAW! 8. Supported bills to create a revolving loan fund for public charter schools, promoting choice and competition in education (S1043, H0113) SIGNED INTO LAW! 9. Supported a bill to promote sexual abstinence education in Idaho schools, fostering responsibility and family values (H228) SIGNED INTO LAW! 10. Supported a bill to prohibit ranked choice voting, protecting Idaho elections from manipulation (H179) SIGNED INTO LAW! 11. Supported a bill to prohibit transgender surgeries for minors, protecting children from irreversible procedures (H71a) SIGNED INTO LAW! 12. Supported a bill to reject COVID-19 vaccine mandates, upholding medical freedom (S1130) SIGNED INTO LAW! 13. Fought for fiscal restraint against Idaho's largest spending bills, challenging excessive government spending in committee and on the Senate floor 14. Supported a bill to ensure public libraries are free of pornography, protecting Idaho's youth from harmful material (H314a) VETOED BY GOVERNOR! 15. Sponsored a bill to strike the Blaine Amendment from Idaho's state constitution, opposing religious discrimination to support educational freedom and parental choice for religious schools (RS30025) BLOCKED! Pretty cool, right? My point? I'm not here to make a mess of the place or climb a political ladder (gross). And I'm not here to serve bureaucrats, the Governor, or the lobbyist class either (equally revolting). I'm here to make Idaho a conservative haven that values law and order, traditional family values, and America First principles. Full-stop. And as Idaho's conservative heart beats stronger every year, I'm 100% committed to that. But if you're still here and grumbling about the state's path without stepping up to make a difference, I hear Portland's always an option if you're looking for a change of scenery. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Brian Lenney ? ?

It means so much to me that my sister Thea made the document that granted my great-great-great-grandfather the right to vote into a scarf.
**The day I was sworn into the House of Representatives, I carried that scarf.** It was surreal knowing how far my family had come. From being enslaved, to _finally_ getting the right to vote, to having me be elected to represent the First State.
**I also carried that scarf on the day of the January 6th insurrection.** On such a dark day, my scarf served as a reminder that every effort to undermine our democracy has been met with voices of power and reason.
**And I carried that scarf when I was sworn in to the Senate.**
**[Just like my ancestors, I am committed to fighting for justice. If you’re with me, will you please chip in $25 so I have the resources I need to keep standing up for our rights against attacks from Trump and his MAGA cronies?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109890998/552593337/-499364019?amount=25&source=LOC_EM_FR_2025.06.19_Scarf_B1_F2_S3_C2_30DOx90DON_RS-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OWFkNmM1Zi0zZDRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=iPgitoQw0DkhUCEBgaSSjzf4uHlg7MthE6jte2rf2v0=)**
