name: Survey Team [Heritage Action]
subject: Should foreign citizens bankroll American elections?
body:
Please see a special message from our sponsor:

Unsubscribe
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xul=2qq8ny363wdaxf3eu1ztoj2053gioccb1dff4d2orwv3atqutew083kktkvpqla14x419l2yj0g58o0cde9co2oeutfjkvjpm4mmu8htw&__stmp=sy4a5z&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh

Please see a special message from our sponsor:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sy4a5z&__onlt=t

Dear Fellow American,

I am enclosing a survey on a very urgent matter.

It will only take a moment of your time, but the topic is vital to our nation's future.

A movement is growing, led by progressives—but supported by many well-meaning Americans—to change the way we elect our president. In effect, it seeks to do away with the Electoral College as devised by the Framers of our Constitution.

That's why Hillsdale College is conducting a National Survey on Presidential Selection.

By completing this survey and returning it to me as soon as possible, you will help Hillsdale College more clearly understand the views of mainstream Americans concerning this issue—views we will make available to policymakers and opinion leaders.

You can complete the National Survey on Presidential Selection by using the link below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jfn8gc6dy7m6r6qnjymsiz34os852dywg2x9lqaxfzc2wgdq1uf3awn5f5cd5ax6mhp1oob8uvc2rdm3fd0lsew1kbofyr18dflce2epzpz4lji6gdg63fwty2w4h5rji8p2vce24ufjvxi90yx6&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sy4a5z&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sy4a5z&__onlt=t

Do you think that most Americans understand how the Electoral College works and its role in preserving free and representative government?

YES
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sy4a5z&__onlt=t

NO
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sy4a5z&__onlt=t

For most of our nation's history, the Electoral College, as devised by the Framers of our Constitution, was not controversial. Indeed, it was considered essential to preserving free and representative government.

But today, more than a dozen states have joined together in an attempt to undermine this constitutional bulwark of liberty.

This movement to undermine the Constitution has grown largely because of the failure of America's schools to provide young people with a proper grounding in American civics—too many Americans simply don't understand the importance of the Constitution, including the Electoral College, to liberty.

Because of this, Hillsdale College has expanded its educational mission on behalf of liberty nationwide, directly teaching millions of Americans in all 50 states.

These efforts, which aim to counter the Left's domination over most of our schools, include:

Teaching millions of citizens nationwide through free online courses, such as "Constitution 101" and "The Great American Story: A Land of Hope;"

Reaching over six million households and businesses nationwide with Imprimis, Hillsdale's free digest of liberty;

Promoting constitutional education through social media outlets popular with young people;

Sending millions of pocket-size copies of America's founding documents to citizens and K-12 schools across America; and

Restoring excellence to K-12 education by helping establish and guiding classical K-12 schools nationwide and providing, free of charge, Hillsdale's American classical K-12 curriculum (including American civics) to thousands of American educators and homeschoolers.

Your answers to the National Survey on Presidential Selection will help Hillsdale form the most effective strategy to educate millions of Americans about the importance of the Constitution to liberty—including the importance of the Electoral College.

And this is especially important in this critical time for America's future.

So please share your views on this urgent topic by completing our National Survey on Presidential Selection and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few minutes of your time.

You can take the survey using this secure link I've provided below:

http://trk.victorymailings.com/?xtl=17x64w94u6jfn8gc6dy7m6r6qnjymsiz34y3mxmvwrxlebax2900njr5tndcs9h3dzlagsym086erpoi53x7wpdglkyi4h3qfezy5evpo8awgxywellfazmhxpiajecf6dmlg2nklu8u2ro7r1lwvz1mlvkn12&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sy4a5z&__onlt=t
http://trk.victorymailings.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=sy4a5z&__onlt=t

And after you take your survey, I hope you will also consider supporting Hillsdale College with a tax-deductible gift.

Since 1844, Hillsdale has been "pursuing truth and defending liberty." Today, it does so through its extensive educational outreach efforts while refusing to accept ONE PENNY of government funding—not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

The support of private citizens like you is essential to our work.

Please represent your state and take the National Survey on Presidential Selection today. Thank you for your time and attention—and for your friendship and support of liberty.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn

President, Hillsdale College

Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

body:
With your help, I’ll be Trump’s kryptonite
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892212/552597789/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMTFmZjE2OC00MTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mN6wJLNOyu2W0creTPRrPGxzJ4t1VUzl6xkVUqhv_w0=)
Hi there, it’s Robert Garcia — the Democrat representing California’s 42nd District in Congress.
My favorite superhero, Superman, has a slogan: **"Truth, Justice, and the American Way."**
It’s a phrase that represents the core values he embodies: honesty, fairness, and freedom — and it’s a phrase I try to live my life by.
**It’s that ethos that drove me to run for Congress. And it’s that same ethos that drives me every day in my efforts to hold the Trump Administration accountable.**
While Donald Trump and his lawless administration trample on our Constitution and the rule of law, it’s never been more critical to have strong Democratic leadership that’s not afraid to stand up for Truth, Justice, and the American way.
That’s the kind of leader I’ve always been — and I’ve vowed to be Donald Trump’s kryptonite so long as I hold my position.
[**But I need you with me. So today I’m asking you to pitch in $20 or more as a sign of support for my efforts to be Donald Trump’s kryptonite.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892213/552597790/-309737922?refcode=250619_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMTFmZjE2OC00MTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mN6wJLNOyu2W0creTPRrPGxzJ4t1VUzl6xkVUqhv_w0=)
[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892214/552597791/-309737922?refcode=250619_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMTFmZjE2OC00MTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mN6wJLNOyu2W0creTPRrPGxzJ4t1VUzl6xkVUqhv_w0=)
Republicans in Congress refuse to do their job. **Instead of honoring their oaths of office and performing the kind of oversight needed, they’re turning a blind eye as Donald Trump leads us into a constitutional crisis.**
Enough is enough. We need to fight back like the future of our democracy depends on it — because it does.
[**So, please, if you’re ready to join me in this fight, donate now and let’s work together to fight for the future.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892215/552597792/-309737922?refcode=250619_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMTFmZjE2OC00MTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mN6wJLNOyu2W0creTPRrPGxzJ4t1VUzl6xkVUqhv_w0=)
Thank you,
Robert
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892216/552597793/-309737922?refcode=250619_RG_Kryptonite&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMTFmZjE2OC00MTRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=mN6wJLNOyu2W0creTPRrPGxzJ4t1VUzl6xkVUqhv_w0=)

name: Chris Pappas
subject: A personal note this Pride Month
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii**
body:
|
#
---
_Hello, Mazie here! Below is a message from Rep. Chris Pappas, who is running for Senate in New Hampshire. Please read his message below, and consider[**splitting a donation between our campaigns.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892500/552598688/1765955805?amount=&refcode=AU_MKH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNjY3OTlhZS00MjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9l2lzECiQLRP1hq2EW9NI3vp3iqRdkpsdA9XLt4aJqU=)_
* * *
**I’m Chris Pappas, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in New Hampshire.** Mazie said I could reach out to you today with a special message about Pride. Growing up gay in New Hampshire, I never imagined this future was possible, Peter. While I was lucky to receive love and support from family and friends, it was still hard to picture a time when I would be able to live openly let alone be elected to Congress. I certainly never imagined the community I grew up in would embrace me, believe in me, and elect me to represent them — not just once, but four times in a row. Now, because of the progress we’ve made together, I’m running for U.S. Senate. And if elected, **I’d make history as the first openly gay man to ever serve in that chamber.** **[Will you help me make history? Pitch in $10 to support my grassroots campaign and Mazie Hirono now.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892501/552598689/1765955805?amount=10&refcode=AU_MKH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNjY3OTlhZS00MjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9l2lzECiQLRP1hq2EW9NI3vp3iqRdkpsdA9XLt4aJqU=)** When I came out, there were examples of LGBTQ+ trailblazers – like Harvey Milk – who had been elected to public office. But it wasn’t a very long list. I knew that if I was going to serve my neighbors the way I envisioned, I’d need to break some barriers of my own. With the support of my community, I became the first openly gay man to represent New Hampshire in Congress back in 2018. I defeated the GOP four times in one of the most competitive districts in the country. Now I’m fighting to keep our U.S. Senate seat blue. New Hampshire is one of the most critical swing states in this election cycle. Republicans are targeting this race, aiming to flip this competitive seat red and expand their majority. But we’re going to stop them together, just like we’ve done before. We’re going to keep this seat blue – _and_ we’re going to defy expectations when we take back the Senate. This campaign means so much, Peter. It’s something that my younger self would never have even imagined. And not only is this race our opportunity to make LGBTQ+ history, it’s a crucial election we can’t afford to lose to the GOP. The stakes are simply too high. [**If it makes sense for you today, please split a $10 contribution to my campaign and Mazie right now.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892502/552598690/1765955805?amount=10&refcode=AU_MKH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNjY3OTlhZS00MjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9l2lzECiQLRP1hq2EW9NI3vp3iqRdkpsdA9XLt4aJqU=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $5 NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109892503/552598691/-1566255520?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=AU_MKH_FR_TAN_GEN_AC_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MTU3NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNjY3OTlhZS00MjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9l2lzECiQLRP1hq2EW9NI3vp3iqRdkpsdA9XLt4aJqU=)

clean_body: | Hello, Mazie here! Below is a message from Rep. Chris Pappas, who is running for Senate in New Hampshire. Please read his message below, and consider= I’m Chris Pappas, Democratic candidate for U.S. Senate in New Hampshire. Mazie said I could reach out to you today with a special message about Pride. Growing up gay in New Hampshire, I never imagined this future was possible, Peter. While I was lucky to receive love and support from family and friends, it was still hard to picture a time when I would be able to live openly let alone be elected to Congress. I certainly never imagined the community I grew up in would embrace me, believe in me, and elect me to represent them — not just once, but four times in a row. Now, because of the progress we’ve made together, I’m running for U.S. Senate. And if elected, I’d make history as the first openly gay man to ever serve in that chamber. = When I came out, there were examples of LGBTQ+ trailblazers – like Harvey Milk – who had been elected to public office. But it wasn’t a very long list. I knew that if I was going to serve my neighbors the way I envisioned, I’d need to break some barriers of my own. With the support of my community, I became the first openly gay man to represent New Hampshire in Congress back in 2018. I defeated the GOP four times in one of the most competitive districts in the country. Now I’m fighting to keep our U.S. Senate seat blue. New Hampshire is one of the most critical swing states in this election cycle. Republicans are targeting this race, aiming to flip this competitive seat red and expand their majority. But we’re going to stop them together, just like we’ve done before. We’re going to keep this seat blue – and we’re going to defy expectations when we take back the Senate. This campaign means so much, Peter. It’s something that my younger self would never have even imagined. And not only is this race our opportunity to make LGBTQ+ history, it’s a crucial election we can’t afford to lose to the GOP. The stakes are simply too high. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = = | = = | = = Happy Pride Month, Peter. And thank you – as always – for your kindness and support. With gratitude, Chris Pappas Friends of Mazie Hirono 335 Merchant Street #677 Honolulu, HI 96809 United States mailto:info@mazieforhawaii.com | Paid for by Mazie Hirono For Hawaii

name: Justice via VPP
subject: I grew up celebrating Juneteenth. This year feels different.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
| | [On this day in 1865, news of freedom finally reached the last enslaved Black Americans in Galveston, Texas. But 160 years later, the fight for “absolute equality” is far from over—especially with Trump back in the White House and erasing progress for Black Americans in real time. Read more below, then chip in $7 or more to help us fight back »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91212?t=6&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0) | [CONTRIBUTE $7 NOW »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91212?t=7&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)
---
Hi friend! Justice here from the Voter Protection Project.

Growing up in Houston—so close to where Juneteenth started—I remember the celebrations: food, music, and community. For us, it was more than a holiday. It was about honoring where we come from and how far we’ve come as Black Americans.

_But this year, Juneteenth feels different._

Juneteenth honors Black liberation—but Trump and his MAGA allies are actively dismantling racial equity across government. Here’s how they’re doing so:
* **They’re _gutting_ federal DEI programs**—firing staff, eliminating equity offices across agencies, and scrubbing protections meant to combat systemic racism.

* **They’re _defunding_ education support**— freezing aid to HBCUs and threatening access to education for Black students.

* **They’re _slashing_ healthcare**—gutting Medicaid and the ACA, hitting Black families the hardest.

* **They’re _weakening_ civil rights enforcement**—dropping key DOJ cases and redirecting resources to attack diversity initiatives.

* **They’re _erasing_ Black history**—removing names like Medgar Evers from federal buildings.

* **They’re _abandoning_ environmental justice**—dropping pollution probes in Black communities and handing power to industry insiders.

These attacks aren’t just cruel— _they’re calculated._ Trump’s agenda is about maintaining power by silencing marginalized people, including Black Americans.

[That’s why the Voter Protection Project is on the frontlines, working to elect Democrats who will fight for true equality. Can you chip in $7 today to help us fight back against Trump’s dangerous agenda? »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91212?t=8&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
[CONTRIBUTE $7 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91213?t=9&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)
---
[CONTRIBUTE $25 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91214?t=10&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)
[CONTRIBUTE $50 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91215?t=11&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)
[CONTRIBUTE $100 »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91216?t=12&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)
[Or a custom donation »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/91212?t=13&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)
With resolve,

Justice M.
Digital Director
The Voter Protection Project
| | Paid for by VPP
(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0).**

[Click here](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0) to request fewer emails. Click here to [unsubscribe](https://act.protectvoting.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=14&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0). | | [Please: Pitch In Once A Month To Protect Voting Rights »](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/123?t=4&akid=20271%2E878179%2EFrbNk0)

name: Republican Party of Nebraska
subject: Your Weekly Update from the NEGOP 🚨
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY]()
body:
News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong.

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/f60e73e88f91/your-weekly-update-from-the-negop-12767856?e=25844e23a8)
---
| | | | [|
---](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=96166a8dcd&e=25844e23a8)
| | | | | Peter,
---
|
---
|
---
|
# Letter from the Chair
---
| _Dear Friends,__Candidate recruitment season is in full swing; the 2026 midterms are fast approaching. The NEGOP Political Director Joe Hagerty has been hard at work mapping out open seats and training county GOP leaders on how to do the same at the county level. Joe and I have been meeting with a steady stream of potential candidates and incumbents.__The NEGOP Government Relations Director Jeanne Reigle has been leading our Legislative Plan Committee. We have been working to sharpen our areas of focus and ultimately to go with a two-year legislative plan that would follow the unicameral biennial cycle if approved by the State Central Committee at the August 16th meeting in Lincoln.__Our goals this summer are:__• Determine which incumbents are not running for re-election_ _• Find candidates for every open race and for targeted seats held by Democrats_ _• Broaden our donor base so we better execute our political plan_ _• Continue to train our county chairs in fundraising, data, voter engagement and candidate recruitment_ _The time passes all too quickly and we have so much still to do, but I’m blessed by the many people who have stepped up to help. As I travel the state this summer, I look forward to meeting some of you on the journey.____Mary Jane Truemper_ _Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party_
---
|
---
|
# What’s Happening...
---
| The NEGOP is hosting a **Campaign & Candidate School** in Lincoln, Nebraska, on July 26th. This event, in partnership with the RNC, will provide candidates, campaign managers, staff, and volunteers with the skills and knowledge needed to run and manage successful political campaigns. Topics covered will include campaign organization, fundraising strategies, voter contact, and crafting effective messages.
* **Date** : July 26th
* **Time** : 9am - 4pm
* **Location** : Lincoln, Nebraska
* **Target Audience** : Candidates, campaign managers, staff, and volunteers
* **Topics** : Campaign organization, fundraising, voter contact, messaging, and more
* **Sign-up Link** : Stay tuned for registration details!

In addition, county Republican parties across Nebraska are participating in voter registration efforts during local summer events. These celebrations, including parades and voter registration booths, provide great opportunities to connect with current and potential voters. If you’re interested in helping out, contact your local county Republican Party.The **Latinos Coalition** continues to expand its outreach efforts and build lasting relationships within Nebraska’s Latino community. Recently, the coalition participated in the Cinco De Mayo Festival with a voter registration booth and parade involvement. The coalition has now launched its website and is actively recruiting volunteers to help with outreach.Lastly, with the 2026 primary filing deadline approaching, the NEGOP is working to recruit candidates for federal, statewide, legislative, and multi-county offices. The Leadership Institute recently hosted a training to help candidates build and run winning campaigns. If you know someone interested in running, please contact [**political@ne.gop**](mailto:political@ne.gop?subject=&body=).
---

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Celebrating freedom
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
I am proud to honor Juneteenth, as we face the reality of our past and commit ourselves to building a more just and equitable future for all Americans.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=2701%2E400544%2E6A9QA8)
Today is an important day.
On June 19, 1865, Union soldiers arrived in Galveston, Texas to finally enforce the Emancipation Proclamation that “all slaves are free.”
Each year, Juneteenth is a day to celebrate Black resilience and joy. **It is a reminder of just how long Black people across this country have been fighting for true equality.**
**It’s also a time to acknowledge that the fight for justice and equality is ongoing.** The history of enslaved people in our country created a legacy of stark injustices and systemic racism that communities still grapple with today. It is on all of us to continue addressing these lingering inequities each and every day.
This year especially, Juneteenth takes on a renewed meaning. We must stand up, join together, and create a brighter future where everyone can thrive in America no matter the color of their skin.
As a proud member of the Pueblo of Laguna, I know how important it is to face the reality of our past and commit ourselves to building a more just and equitable future for all.
In solidarity,
Deb

body:

Black fathers belong in the home.
I’ll say that on Juneteenth & any other day because it’s the TRUTH.
We celebrate the end of slavery, but now liberal Democrats are trying to stamp out any free thinker who escapes their _**woke plantation**_.
It’s President Trump’s _STRONGEST_ supporter in Florida, Byron Donalds. The Radical Left is trying to shut me up after I said the quiet part out loud.
**Look, I DON’T CARE about the color of your skin. If you love the USA, you’re a Patriot in my book.**
But the America-Last Woke Mob is blowing up over me - some of them even calling me a “race traitor” straight to my face over my beliefs.
Now they’re sending more vicious attacks my way, fundraising millions of dollars against me, and telling me to SURRENDER to their racist ideology.
_**[I fear this is only the beginning](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/aFzq/?__vbtrk=Mjk3NjQ5OjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=Mjk3NjQ5OjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct):**_

If we can’t hold Florida, everything that President Trump worked so hard to achieve will come CRASHING down.
I’m the FIRST line of defense against the Radical Left, and all I have is YOU. So it’s with a humble heart that I ask you to chip in any dollar amount TODAY.

We celebrate the end of slavery, but now liberal Democrats are trying to stamp out any free thinker who escapes their woke plantation. It’s President Trump’s STRONGEST supporter in Florida, Byron Donalds. The Radical Left is trying to shut me up after I said the quiet part out loud. Look, I DON’T CARE about the color of your skin. If you love the USA, you’re a Patriot in my book. But the America-Last Woke Mob is blowing up over me - some of them even calling me a “race traitor” straight to my face over my beliefs. Now they’re sending more vicious attacks my way, fundraising millions of dollars against me, and telling me to SURRENDER to their racist ideology. : | | | | I’m running for Governor in the most MAGA state in the nation. Democrats want nothing more than to CRUSH our movement and turn Florida into a WOKE WASTELAND. If we can’t hold Florida, everything that President Trump worked so hard to achieve will come CRASHING down. I’m the FIRST line of defense against the Radical Left, and all I have is YOU. name: Adam Schwarze, former Navy SEAL
subject: The definition of a woman isn’t up for debate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Schwarze for Senate
body:
Democrats want our girls competing against biological boys in sports, sharing bathrooms with trans individuals, and now the governor of Minnesota can’t even define what a woman is. | [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=47c6012b633a4de2a6976b819fce47c4&_e=iTHZ4zudvFdu0KSWPJR4lErwwiI8bm_Lu1QMeRN2Je4lx5ljFkzmrS_5HpEt0CL4g6w6LM6j5ncWJBqiWc36rlWyc_pthAAEjaqvZhW81ILBTzLvlk0wuuMisQh-rTaQ7OHQs64eV_a-DSwlm_QrSe1uLqR9vt94H4nYEPnChHSRHmsbio2Tdc-JDuj8a2OUFNqj7dQSP_IUznX4IfuAfTx_BND5mXL25TZvCrylIUgP6YnaP5IH8TnulK2Wm7FY27TtGFhEX6P7B9ROZuiN42BZ1NbdHaMlHdlq2c2O62ZnjdyQHUT0NTr7mqflGSNhY5GAHkj1NKlZ-KQv4C-o5i1y36xXSNAz1HRi3OF7g8HJTiCmYW47Y4zbZxtkkBIzPnyDreM0WY774_rnQy2vinvarDCAl8DGyVuvE9nt8Q3BquJr8n-F7QlQ1JAmWQguHFR39oenkN_1RUpFGBRvUVWN_EhpvGkCkZ9ijmafHtNJkxtwD65r7Mv4nU_UGf8Ts83J_jwiZ9ZIRXjksg05U0s5zK4DRICGbS2DvwbRQMng1g7lcz5fvzhrVLj15Ba07Fe6o89fe-VhF615e_ZYsEJUujPzJ5ljaoCOIrC3RcsizV1v4dZdxUTCw5zoCBTS1nVwIsO03GlrIl7KBfkiaGvi_vulpT5sBxAJ6EWHOZp-zGMtT2zcOvD1MtGYbxnQaGANTDBP6qxpBU3J9braCnaPMAIjaNULPHZusCzTdVtizaJFV4GigO2XPi7PZrEer8VUZLFmyGkduXsSHJSykw%3D%3D)

---

Peter,

Figuring out where to start writing this email has been tough because the content of the copy you’re about to read is, quite honestly, absurd.

I’ve spent most of my adult life in some of the world’s most dangerous places. **I’ve seen what happens when governments collapse, the truth is suppressed, and strong men choose silence instead of standing up. But I never thought I’d see it here, not in America.**

And yet last week, sitting in a congressional hearing, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz was asked a straightforward question:

_**“Can you define what a woman is?”**_

_**He couldn’t.**_
_**He wouldn’t.**_

He dodged. He deflected. And then he sat in silence.

Let me be clear — this wasn’t a trick. This wasn’t a “gotcha” question. This was the easiest test a leader can face: tell the truth, or bow to the mob.
**
Governor Walz chose the mob.**

That moment — that silence — wasn’t just embarrassing. It was dangerous.

Because when leaders abandon truth to protect their politics, it’s the people who suffer. And right now, our daughters, wives, sisters, and grandmothers are being erased in real time—not just from locker rooms and playing fields but from language itself.

My name is Adam Schwarze.

**I’m a proud Minnesotan and a retired Navy SEAL.** I joined the Marine Corps after 9/11 and served in special operations, deploying 13 times to over 70 countries.

_**I didn’t just fight for this country — I bled for it.**_

[](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=47c6012b633a4de2a6976b819fce47c4&_e=iTHZ4zudvFdu0KSWPJR4lErwwiI8bm_Lu1QMeRN2Je4lx5ljFkzmrS_5HpEt0CL4oCtWdzQwaoO-AWPN7vgN4TJC-dooSMhMza0pwbHtGQDg4R6lGAycXQiuICY8KaZjUcK17jVegOkq_OkjKntMgZolSuQi6NO4KpSj7PBRSbXrCN_JIp1sCVJwthAiQzsT9y8sxLCt8aBADjb1LBw0W4JsFG9HOf8oX_csLMn3en300-qdm08ojj1bRfvq31AmsMXN_Fzq5T8aDKNMvvAX3IZ9mZ05jm3hUCJ9vvF-Deb60VkIRh-DK7ZsYbo90_yjNTypU573k2SXqY-Rdn7k0fo3mbQxBKdV93MMKnhp17yaCWtMSoH7GR6I6pOENKBvfytou_jIQgVULW9hGbWRwI3CTmaHjFKfvQlpD51PhWUBeJ4v-j-vuyy4WwtEsVVw8hMW7Nbe0IPVOTrFkTTRtIOCSIMVCR8iJjBSY_s9ArvZN7MLjqe_QfQMvT2UPnWlm4YnhFwg5BdykEqEZpbM6fR7dfyhlui7mbW9lHu254ZGHLMF6-CX-gGoJbHWoEKh9mGIbMP7xI7NTJkKXFGpS6jb6IC687BkpiOjwPrCYDs44m0HIsjuQK01lQjMyxNhOIO8G0KCPEC2GpuUSItBWj-eemdqtg5GlX2mHxxxYNUIbhzliZVafRqaG_scdA_sSJAP9RWJ7lCYnP-80YlOhN8HOjmRURjz-FYU6JyeA_Bk6qb7uqR0t7E8smtdDzmMXPtop3Ag7pYDWzb9iF_xQ0Ia2joScgVW52Yp6I4l9ro%3D)

And I never imagined I’d be running for public office. But something has changed. Something fundamental.

When a governor can’t say what a woman is...
When “men in women’s sports” becomes policy...
**When the truth becomes optional, and lies become virtue, that’s when I knew I had one more mission.**

I’m running for the United States Senate because I believe America is worth saving. But it’s going to take more than votes — it’s going to take backbone.

I’ve seen the cost of freedom. I’ve watched friends give everything so that future generations could live in a country built on truth. I won’t let their sacrifice be undone by a political class too cowardly to speak plainly.

That’s why I need your help today.

clean_body: Democrats want our girls competing against biological boys in sports, sharing bathrooms with trans individuals, and now the governor of Minnesota can’t even define what a woman is. | Peter, Figuring out where to start writing this email has been tough because the content of the copy you’re about to read is, quite honestly, absurd. I’ve spent most of my adult life in some of the world’s most dangerous places. I’ve seen what happens when governments collapse, the truth is suppressed, and strong men choose silence instead of standing up. But I never thought I’d see it here, not in America. And yet last week, sitting in a congressional hearing, Minnesota Governor Tim Walz was asked a straightforward question: “Can you define what a woman is?” He couldn’t. He wouldn’t. He dodged. He deflected. And then he sat in silence. Let me be clear — this wasn’t a trick. This wasn’t a “gotcha” question. This was the easiest test a leader can face: tell the truth, or bow to the mob. Governor Walz chose the mob. That moment — that silence — wasn’t just embarrassing. It was dangerous. Because when leaders abandon truth to protect their politics, it’s the people who suffer. And right now, our daughters, wives, sisters, and grandmothers are being erased in real time—not just from locker rooms and playing fields but from language itself. My name is Adam Schwarze. I’m a proud Minnesotan and a retired Navy SEAL. I joined the Marine Corps after 9/11 and served in special operations, deploying 13 times to over 70 countries. I didn’t just fight for this country — I bled for it. And I never imagined I’d be running for public office. But something has changed. Something fundamental. When a governor can’t say what a woman is... When “men in women’s sports” becomes policy... When the truth becomes optional, and lies become virtue, that’s when I knew I had one more mission. I’m running for the United States Senate because I believe America is worth saving. But it’s going to take more than votes — it’s going to take backbone. I’ve seen the cost of freedom. I’ve watched friends give everything so that future generations could live in a country built on truth. I won’t let their sacrifice be undone by a political class too cowardly to speak plainly. That’s why I need your help today. The Democratic Party isn’t just drifting — it’s sprinting toward something unrecognizable. First, they said men could use women’s bathrooms. Then they demanded that girls compete against biological males in school sports. Now, they claim they don’t even know what a woman is. And Governor Walz? He’s not a bystander. He’s a willing participant in this charade. His likely successor — my opponent — is even more radical. So if you’re like me — and Stand with me. Fight back. Let’s win this. With gratitude, Adam Schwarze Retired Navy SEAL Republican Candidate for U.S. Senate - Minnesota FOLLOW ADAM ON SOCIAL MEDIA | | Paid for by Schwarze for Senate Adam Schwarze is a former Navy SEAL and Marine. name: Ted Cruz
subject: What Gavin Newsom is REALLY saying
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
These leftists think the American people are stupid.

| | | [](http://trk.tedcruz._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1FLUhvdXNlRmlsZSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249Q3J1el9OZXdzb20tQ0EtRmFpbHVyZV8yMDI1MDYxOSZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPUVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PU5ld3NvbS1DQS1GYWlsdXJlJnV0bV9hZ2VuY3k9UEcmYW1vdW50PTc1In0/D6SXQIoSVjUAH-_YSXApRw)
---
| | Fellow Conservative , Gavin Newsom - the utterly incompetent Governor of California who has ambitions to run for President of the United States - is once again spinning, lying, and crying on camera to appeal to his base of supporters suffering from Trump Derangement Syndrome.

What we've seen from him, no surprise, is a _COLOSSAL FAILURE OF LEADERSHIP_.

**It 's beyond pathetic.**

As Los Angeles has descended into chaos, with radical leftists and anarchists destroying property, looting, and targeting law enforcement with potentially deadly violence, here's what he had to say earlier this week on X: _I have formally requested the Trump Administration rescind their unlawful deployment of troops in Los Angeles county and return them to my command. We didn 't have a problem until Trump got involved. This is a serious breach of state sovereignty -- inflaming tensions while pulling resources from where they're actually needed. Rescind the order. Return control to California._ Here was my response - a translation of what Newsome really means - and I didn't mince words: _I have formally requested the President stop enforcing federal law in CA. In the Golden State, we embrace lawlessness. We welcome rioters & looters, & we want even MORE illegal aliens & gang members. We handcuff our police & celebrate our criminals. And we hate America._ These leftists think the American people are stupid. They think voters will buy their crap.

**They couldn 't be more wrong and they'll NEVER learn...**

Just look at what happened seven months ago on Election Day 2024, when we trounced them at the ballot box!

I'm proud to stand with ICE and President Trump, and I'm never going to let up on calling out Gavin Newsom. The people of California deserve better, and this guy has ZERO business ever coming near the Oval Office. **[WE CAN'T LET UP IN THE FIGHT FOR OUR NATION'S FUTURE >>>](http://trk.tedcruz._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1FLUhvdXNlRmlsZSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249Q3J1el9OZXdzb20tQ0EtRmFpbHVyZV8yMDI1MDYxOSZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPUVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PU5ld3NvbS1DQS1GYWlsdXJlJnV0bV9hZ2VuY3k9UEcmYW1vdW50PTc1In0/S1lu4r0kul_Um44Y2OLM4w)** I'm grateful for your support as we work to expose the radical left and defeat them. We're locked in a battle for the heart and soul of America and it's all hands on deck.

For Liberty,

Ted Cruz

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109893863/552603848/131323729?refcode=em-6.19.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OGE0ZWY1My00ZDRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=svsXIEAabjNiGxUl9ccPmjzmwtNCwj7kWmc2ckzffb0=) Peter, I’m honored to share some big news with you: **Senator Bernie Sanders just endorsed my campaign for Congress!** [**I'll explain why this endorsement is so important, but this news is going to attract a _ton_ of attention from the special interests trying to defeat me. I’m asking you to help us build on this game-changing endorsement by chipping in $3 today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109893864/552603849/131323729?refcode=em-6.19.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OGE0ZWY1My00ZDRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=svsXIEAabjNiGxUl9ccPmjzmwtNCwj7kWmc2ckzffb0=) | | **[Donate](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109893865/552603850/131323729?refcode=em-6.19.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OGE0ZWY1My00ZDRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=svsXIEAabjNiGxUl9ccPmjzmwtNCwj7kWmc2ckzffb0=)**
---
I supported Bernie in 2016, and many of my friends in WI-03 did the same. Most weren’t political junkies, but we all felt that Bernie was the first candidate since President Obama whose authenticity and moral clarity never wavered.
Everything he does is rooted in uplifting the working class. And if you’ve ever sat with Bernie, you know he doesn’t take kindly to empty slogans. If he's backing you, he wants to know you’ve got the backbone to take on the status quo.
That’s exactly what I’ll do in Congress.
Bernie and I both believe in expanding Medicare to cover dental, vision, and hearing. We both support raising the minimum wage and tying it to inflation. We’re both tired of seeing billionaires bankroll elections while regular people are left behind.
I know folks like to stick labels on candidates -- _moderate, progressive_ , this group or that one. The only label I accept is fighter for working families in Western Wisconsin.
We need to move past the purity tests and recognize when we’re rowing in the same direction. And Bernie and I are on the same page that we need our country to work for the people who actually keep it running.
**This endorsement is powerful. It's about unity. It's about truly living up to our values as advocates for the people. But to make this work, it needs to be backed with people power.**
[**We're hoping to see a large number of donations come in before our June 30 deadline. If you're with us, please send in $3 or more today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109893866/552603851/131323729?refcode=em-6.19.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzUwMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3OGE0ZWY1My00ZDRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=svsXIEAabjNiGxUl9ccPmjzmwtNCwj7kWmc2ckzffb0=)

clean_body: | = Peter, I’m honored to share some big news with you: Senator Bernie Sanders just endorsed my campaign for Congress! = | | = I supported Bernie in 2016, and many of my friends in WI-03 did the same. Most weren’t political junkies, but we all felt that Bernie was the first candidate since President Obama whose authenticity and moral clarity never wavered. Everything he does is rooted in uplifting the working class. And if you’ve ever sat with Bernie, you know he doesn’t take kindly to empty slogans. If he's backing you, he wants to know you’ve got the backbone to take on the status quo. That’s exactly what I’ll do in Congress. Bernie and I both believe in expanding Medicare to cover dental, vision, and hearing. We both support raising the minimum wage and tying it to inflation. We’re both tired of seeing billionaires bankroll elections while regular people are left behind. I know folks like to stick labels on candidates -- moderate, progressive , this group or that one. The only label I accept is fighter for working families in Western Wisconsin. We need to move past the purity tests and recognize when we’re rowing in the same direction. And Bernie and I are on the same page that we need our country to work for the people who actually keep it running. This endorsement is powerful. It's about unity. It's about truly living up to our values as advocates for the people. But to make this work, it needs to be backed with people power. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | **= | = **** | | = | = **** | | = | = Thank you, Becca | = = Rebecca Cooke is a small business and nonprofit leader who has focused her career on building community and serving others. She was born and raised on an Eau Claire dairy farm and was appointed by Governor Tony Evers to the Wisconsin Economic Development Corporation. We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Cooke for Congress PO Box 1846 Eau Claire, WI 54702 United States

name: Sign Your Name | Left Action
subject: Sign: Defend Social Security from Trump's stupidity
body:
| | | | |

| | |
# [Don't let Trump's moronic tariffs bankrupt Social Security](https://default.salsalabs.org/T218aeace-3fbb-4916-8649-f2abde7a8d9e/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc14b6c1a-c7ba-4fed-8c1c-4e75e065a57b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Donald Trump's impressively moronic tariffs are already poised to do two practically contradictory things at once -- slow the economy AND drive up inflation. You have to be a special kind of stupid to do both concurrently. It's like causing a flood and a drought in the same place at the same time. **And now he's going for the trifecta -- by[bankrupting Social Security](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta0405d1f-1f2a-4bfb-b0ef-c77270dc4093/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9):** _" Many economists have expressed concern that the risk of higher unemployment and economic contraction has risen significantly because of the tariff chaos — possibly leading to a recession. Lower labor force participation and earnings would lead to lower Social Security revenues, reducing trust fund solvency." ([source](https://default.salsalabs.org/Teb152cdd-e230-4f65-80b4-5da404761b12/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9))_ Social Security reserves are already projected to be [depleted in 2034](https://default.salsalabs.org/Ta42a332c-175b-4343-9c62-1c986b97ea3c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) \-- a year faster than the projtection this time last year. We need to protect it, not push it towards bankruptcy through the asinine and destructive policies of a madman. **Congress has the power to reject these crazed tariffs, and reduce the threat to Social Security. It should do so NOW.** [**Add your name: Don't let Trump's moronic tariffs bankrupt Social Security**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tb255af29-64d1-416d-8fbd-3e965d058dc5/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

---

This early in the race, every dollar – and every donor – carries extra weight. Our allies, opponents, the press, and even party leaders are closely watching to see if a grassroots campaign like ours can generate real momentum.
If we come up short, they’ll assume the dark money groups have the upper hand. But if we come out strong? We’ll prove that this seat is winnable – and that we're building something real.
We’re not powered by corporations or special interests. We’re powered by teachers, nurses, organizers, students – by folks who believe the Central Valley and working families across the country deserve representation that actually works for us.
That’s why I’m personally asking:

name: Rob Bonta
subject: Add your name: Keep discrimination out of public health research
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642]
body:
[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/4?refcode=em250619&t=1001&akid=1831%2E1088799%2E7TH5mA ]Rob Bonta

Earlier this year, my office led a multistate coalition in a lawsuit
against the Trump administration for unlawfully canceling public health
research grants — and this week, a federal judge ruled in our favor,
restoring the grants and getting life-saving research back on track while
our case proceeds.

These weren’t budget cuts. They were discriminatory attacks on projects
Trump doesn’t like, including ones focused on racial equity, LGBTQ+
health, and vaccine access. The cuts put life-saving research that impacts
vulnerable communities in serious jeopardy.

This week, a Republican-appointed judge called out the Trump
administration’s actions as discriminatory and unlawful — in fact, he
called it “one of the clearest cases of discrimination he’s seen in four
decades on the bench.”

Now, thanks to our efforts, those terminated research grants are being
restored — so California institutions can get back to curing disease and
saving lives.

This ruling is a step forward for science, for justice, and for the
diverse communities who rely on this medical research. And it’s a reminder
that when we fight back, we can win.

This decision is particularly meaningful coming in the week of Juneteenth
— a time to reflect on freedom and equality for all people, especially the
Black community who has long been overlooked, undervalued, and
systematically discriminated against. Not just in law, but in health,
opportunity, and access.

I’m more committed than ever to using the law to protect and support
marginalized communities and hold those in power accountable.

[ https://act.robbonta.com/signup/em-signup-nih-250619/?t=1002&akid=1831%2E1088799%2E7TH5mA ]Add your name if you agree that discrimination has no place in public
health policy. Together, we will keep standing up for scientific
integrity, equal protection under the law, and the right of every
community to be seen and served.

ADD YOUR NAME

In solidarity,
Rob

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: Juneteenth
body:
|
#
Folks, Today, we honor Juneteenth – the day in 1865 when the last enslaved Black Americans in Galveston, Texas were finally informed of their freedom, more than two years after the Emancipation Proclamation was signed. This day marks the end of slavery in legal terms – but the long struggle for justice, equality, and true freedom continues. **Today, I hope you’re able to take a moment and reflect on this historic date – what it has meant for the history of our country, and what it means for us today.** In communities across Mid-Michigan – from Flint to Saginaw and across our region – we understand how the past shapes our present. We’ve seen how families and neighborhoods have endured through injustice and inequity, and how people have continued to lead with strength, hope, and determination in the face of it. That resilience is something I see every day in the people I’m proud to represent. It’s a reminder that progress takes all of us – and that the fight for dignity, opportunity, and fairness is far from over. Thank you, Kristen McDonald Rivet

* **TOP NEWS**

"**Former Texas state Sen. Kelly Hancock to become acting comptroller, run for permanent job,** " Texas Tribune's**Zach Despart** and**Alejandro Serrano** - "Republican state Sen. **Kelly Hancock** launched his campaign for state comptroller Thursday shortly after taking a senior position at the comptroller's office.

His appointment as chief clerk to Comptroller **Glenn Hegar** paves the way for Hanock, a North Richland Hills Republican, to become interim comptroller after Hegar leaves his office in June. And it gives Hancock an edge in a growing Republican primary race next year.

"Kelly is a great fit to serve as the chief financial officer of Texas," Hegar said in a statement, who is stepping down to become chancellor of the Texas A&M University System. "As a long-serving member of the Texas Legislature, he helped shape sound financial policy and authored the state's conservative spending cap legislation."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/9f403656-30df-44f9-addd-96532675fc33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Hegar added that Hancock is honest, trustworthy, and an "all-around good guy."

Hancock, in a statement, cited his conservative bona fides on border security, noting he was one of the first Republican senators to call for ending in-state tuition for undocumented immigrants, which the state did [earlier this month](https://substack.com/redirect/63a31550-2835-440e-90f3-167745109e32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/9f403656-30df-44f9-addd-96532675fc33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"For over a decade, I've fought for Texas taxpayers in the Legislature, cutting red tape, passing conservative budget reforms, and holding government accountable," Hancock said in his campaign announcement. "As Comptroller, I'll make sure your tax dollars are spent wisely, transparently, and responsibly."

Hancock resigned from the Legislature on Wednesday. A quirk in state law prohibited Gov. **Greg Abbott** from directly appointing Hancock comptroller. Senators -- even former ones -- cannot be appointed to another position that requires Senate confirmation during their term.

The [state comptroller](https://substack.com/redirect/f7d5e429-96ea-43ad-b777-78889f327f74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is responsible for managing the state's money. Among the job's biggest tasks are collecting taxes, estimating state revenue, and awarding and managing state contracts.

Entering the primary season as the interim will almost certainly give Hancock a leg up in a race that's already getting crowded. Railroad Commissioner **Christi Craddick** and former state Sen. **Don Huffines** , R-Dallas, [are already in the race](https://substack.com/redirect/9e4c7ed9-28d2-4871-83ee-9195e6102875?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and Huffines has been raking in endorsements -- including one from U.S. Sen. **Ted Cruz**.

Abbott was quick to endorse Hancock, in the process throwing an elbow at Huffines, who as a state senator lost his reelection bid to Democrat **Nathan Johnson** [in 2018](https://substack.com/redirect/96bc3ca8-6ff6-49f5-bbb4-3a08924650ea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

"I endorse Kelly Hancock because I want a candidate who will actually win the election, not someone who has already lost an election to a Democrat," Abbott said in a statement.

Huffines also challenged Abbott in the 2022 Republican gubernatorial primary; Abbott drubbed him by 44 points.

Prior to serving in the Legislature, Hancock was a member of his local school board.

The state of Texas operates a $337 billion budget and rakes in more than a quarter trillion dollars of revenue from a variety of taxes, fees and assessments." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/207a56d3-3839-4f98-ab3b-5bbcdbd872ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Former Texas state Sen. Kelly Hancock to become acting comptroller, run for permanent job,** " Texas Tribune's**Zach Despart** and**Alejandro Serrano** - "Republican state Sen. **Kelly Hancock** launched his campaign for state comptroller Thursday shortly after taking a senior position at the comptroller's office.

His appointment as chief clerk to Comptroller **Glenn Hegar** paves the way for Hanock, a North Richland Hills Republican, to become interim comptroller after Hegar leaves his office in June. And it gives Hancock an edge in a growing Republican primary race next year.

"Kelly is a great fit to serve as the chief financial officer of Texas," Hegar said in a statement, who is stepping down to become chancellor of the Texas A&M University System. "As a long-serving member of the Texas Legislature, he helped shape sound financial policy and authored the state's conservative spending cap legislation."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/3b14560a-f4fd-4fdb-83f1-655e637a3b01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Hegar added that Hancock is honest, trustworthy, and an "all-around good guy."

Hancock, in a statement, cited his conservative bona fides on border security, noting he was one of the first Republican senators to call for ending in-state tuition for undocumented immigrants, which the state did [earlier this month](https://substack.com/redirect/b089f24d-b7d0-4af6-b97d-f9295673fcba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/3b14560a-f4fd-4fdb-83f1-655e637a3b01?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"For over a decade, I've fought for Texas taxpayers in the Legislature, cutting red tape, passing conservative budget reforms, and holding government accountable," Hancock said in his campaign announcement. "As Comptroller, I'll make sure your tax dollars are spent wisely, transparently, and responsibly."

Hancock resigned from the Legislature on Wednesday. A quirk in state law prohibited Gov. **Greg Abbott** from directly appointing Hancock comptroller. Senators -- even former ones -- cannot be appointed to another position that requires Senate confirmation during their term.

The [state comptroller](https://substack.com/redirect/7446de72-fa4e-49fb-bfc4-484694b5f118?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is responsible for managing the state's money. Among the job's biggest tasks are collecting taxes, estimating state revenue, and awarding and managing state contracts.

Entering the primary season as the interim will almost certainly give Hancock a leg up in a race that's already getting crowded. Railroad Commissioner **Christi Craddick** and former state Sen. **Don Huffines** , R-Dallas, [are already in the race](https://substack.com/redirect/f1862c81-ba0a-4fed-aa09-be22d78ccd84?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and Huffines has been raking in endorsements -- including one from U.S. Sen. **Ted Cruz**.

Abbott was quick to endorse Hancock, in the process throwing an elbow at Huffines, who as a state senator lost his reelection bid to Democrat **Nathan Johnson** [in 2018](https://substack.com/redirect/cd9906fc-7be3-4c68-a4c6-c600efca5681?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

"I endorse Kelly Hancock because I want a candidate who will actually win the election, not someone who has already lost an election to a Democrat," Abbott said in a statement.

Huffines also challenged Abbott in the 2022 Republican gubernatorial primary; Abbott drubbed him by 44 points.

Prior to serving in the Legislature, Hancock was a member of his local school board.

The state of Texas operates a $337 billion budget and rakes in more than a quarter trillion dollars of revenue from a variety of taxes, fees and assessments." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/408728fc-fbc8-470f-b904-3ffe20e15bef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

Read the most recent FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr . TOP NEWS "Former Texas state Sen. Kelly Hancock to become acting comptroller, run for permanent job, " Texas Tribune'sZach Despart andAlejandro Serrano - "Republican state Sen. Kelly Hancock launched his campaign for state comptroller Thursday shortly after taking a senior position at the comptroller's office. His appointment as chief clerk to Comptroller Glenn Hegar paves the way for Hanock, a North Richland Hills Republican, to become interim comptroller after Hegar leaves his office in June. And it gives Hancock an edge in a growing Republican primary race next year. "Kelly is a great fit to serve as the chief financial officer of Texas," Hegar said in a statement, who is stepping down to become chancellor of the Texas A&M University System. "As a long-serving member of the Texas Legislature, he helped shape sound financial policy and authored the state's conservative spending cap legislation." Hegar added that Hancock is honest, trustworthy, and an "all-around good guy." Hancock, in a statement, cited his conservative bona fides on border security, noting he was one of the first Republican senators to call for ending in-state tuition for undocumented immigrants, which the state did . "For over a decade, I've fought for Texas taxpayers in the Legislature, cutting red tape, passing conservative budget reforms, and holding government accountable," Hancock said in his campaign announcement. "As Comptroller, I'll make sure your tax dollars are spent wisely, transparently, and responsibly." Hancock resigned from the Legislature on Wednesday. A quirk in state law prohibited Gov. Greg Abbott from directly appointing Hancock comptroller. Senators -- even former ones -- cannot be appointed to another position that requires Senate confirmation during their term. The is responsible for managing the state's money. Among the job's biggest tasks are collecting taxes, estimating state revenue, and awarding and managing state contracts. Entering the primary season as the interim will almost certainly give Hancock a leg up in a race that's already getting crowded. Railroad Commissioner Christi Craddick and former state Sen. Don Huffines , R-Dallas, , and Huffines has been raking in endorsements -- including one from U.S. Sen. Ted Cruz. Abbott was quick to endorse Hancock, in the process throwing an elbow at Huffines, who as a state senator lost his reelection bid to Democrat Nathan Johnson . "I endorse Kelly Hancock because I want a candidate who will actually win the election, not someone who has already lost an election to a Democrat," Abbott said in a statement. Huffines also challenged Abbott in the 2022 Republican gubernatorial primary; Abbott drubbed him by 44 points. Prior to serving in the Legislature, Hancock was a member of his local school board. The state of Texas operates a $337 billion budget and rakes in more than a quarter trillion dollars of revenue from a variety of taxes, fees and assessments." “**Former Texas state Sen. Kelly Hancock to become acting comptroller, run for permanent job,** ” Texas Tribune's**Zach Despart** and**Alejandro Serrano** – “Republican state Sen. **Kelly Hancock** launched his campaign for state comptroller Thursday shortly after taking a senior position at the comptroller’s office.

His appointment as chief clerk to Comptroller **Glenn Hegar** paves the way for Hanock, a North Richland Hills Republican, to become interim comptroller after Hegar leaves his office in June. And it gives Hancock an edge in a growing Republican primary race next year.

“Kelly is a great fit to serve as the chief financial officer of Texas,” Hegar said in a statement, who is stepping down to become chancellor of the Texas A&M; University System. “As a long-serving member of the Texas Legislature, he helped shape sound financial policy and authored the state’s conservative spending cap legislation.”

"For over a decade, I've fought for Texas taxpayers in the Legislature, cutting red tape, passing conservative budget reforms, and holding government accountable," Hancock said in his campaign announcement. "As Comptroller, I'll make sure your tax dollars are spent wisely, transparently, and responsibly.”

Hancock resigned from the Legislature on Wednesday. A quirk in state law prohibited Gov. **Greg Abbott** from directly appointing Hancock comptroller. Senators — even former ones — cannot be appointed to another position that requires Senate confirmation during their term.

The [state comptroller](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.dGpFhb-JkjYFYhTtQ9l5jKWK88FTQlqdFaNymLY82ifRxsIdXIoReAqnLKg9Y0oq71K20eBLAsAgBo1YweCgmn-t8kOxUTnNdC5rNcwZbEPgDjkXJndQVpRdU3oIBoyp3z7dxlHYqzSgtY0v5x_IU7_LVO0EIy0RlT5JihTKwW78zBzurBuLtsieawEzK8i6/4hh/nwo52WSQRwipHMggNRW7qA/h13/h001.6auWxIXKgDT7N_Yg3f813GGW4xRvVP-vxPThqIjX7rw) is responsible for managing the state’s money. Among the job’s biggest tasks are collecting taxes, estimating state revenue, and awarding and managing state contracts.

Entering the primary season as the interim will almost certainly give Hancock a leg up in a race that’s already getting crowded. Railroad Commissioner **Christi Craddick** and former state Sen. **Don Huffines** , R-Dallas, [are already in the race](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.xjnMFfva-73dHOne5Rc0eCyDm7YZItPaA-BvJlf4tsAbK0wo5LzeNBd_odqhrg_858GSWXHV9Vbv3phjFrBnZ5D2rq2ZIVfuKAhlPgs3DTvvjtdETREOYX8-Wbd5kSBD95q78NzL_qgJq5H8U3AXXAvJyg9yqAOQyW6OHPZhdbkCMb2JfIXwf6VoAIHkWF3_N9ksa2sPoecgNZV2shzKOQNm-wuxTdRQ-M6RfZCzhzdqqapFBu0ob5DoBP82FhHk3EXVyXPXOkXnicNZhlua9szV7dCAtclWmFEjkL75F0DSVbTe5lKXHiCPmPXIYAnmouIkW2cUMEbTKCPQ9u2s0578SQXaFRN4C0tkD_5-7zXc2D0ewxKP7VC3DuIaRmiHVfQ5as2HMvF70_hAsGiM0KW4oNUU4Klm4oSI-h0kfMgb5BLHFtuCZEwxa8n6IYp7o0O_o_SshYuO3S6q1ldrHT0LP01-Z7M5uhNr6qG99fGzfiEw628LCdatMFmQjvoY8Ci3UAPA6AHLvyzkYK3TZWdcDfn-gM4bx-aYEHMGIK8/4hh/nwo52WSQRwipHMggNRW7qA/h14/h001.WYcR_fWVs4QzgWffSH0iqUwDNbDVm7Epg9bf8L0zCKo), and Huffines has been raking in endorsements — including one from U.S. Sen. **Ted Cruz**.

“I endorse Kelly Hancock because I want a candidate who will actually win the election, not someone who has already lost an election to a Democrat,” Abbott said in a statement.

Read the most recent FREE subscriber email . Become a PAID subscriber for $5/mo or $50/yr | TOP NEWS “Former Texas state Sen. Kelly Hancock to become acting comptroller, run for permanent job, ” Texas Tribune'sZach Despart andAlejandro Serrano – “Republican state Sen. Kelly Hancock launched his campaign for state comptroller Thursday shortly after taking a senior position at the comptroller’s office. His appointment as chief clerk to Comptroller Glenn Hegar paves the way for Hanock, a North Richland Hills Republican, to become interim comptroller after Hegar leaves his office in June. And it gives Hancock an edge in a growing Republican primary race next year. “Kelly is a great fit to serve as the chief financial officer of Texas,” Hegar said in a statement, who is stepping down to become chancellor of the Texas A&M; University System. “As a long-serving member of the Texas Legislature, he helped shape sound financial policy and authored the state’s conservative spending cap legislation.” » Hegar added that Hancock is honest, trustworthy, and an “all-around good guy.” Hancock, in a statement, cited his conservative bona fides on border security, noting he was one of the first Republican senators to call for ending in-state tuition for undocumented immigrants, which the state did . » "For over a decade, I've fought for Texas taxpayers in the Legislature, cutting red tape, passing conservative budget reforms, and holding government accountable," Hancock said in his campaign announcement. "As Comptroller, I'll make sure your tax dollars are spent wisely, transparently, and responsibly.” Hancock resigned from the Legislature on Wednesday. A quirk in state law prohibited Gov. Greg Abbott from directly appointing Hancock comptroller. Senators — even former ones — cannot be appointed to another position that requires Senate confirmation during their term. The is responsible for managing the state’s money. Among the job’s biggest tasks are collecting taxes, estimating state revenue, and awarding and managing state contracts. Entering the primary season as the interim will almost certainly give Hancock a leg up in a race that’s already getting crowded. Railroad Commissioner Christi Craddick and former state Sen. Don Huffines , R-Dallas, , and Huffines has been raking in endorsements — including one from U.S. Sen. Ted Cruz. Abbott was quick to endorse Hancock, in the process throwing an elbow at Huffines, who as a state senator lost his reelection bid to Democrat Nathan Johnson . “I endorse Kelly Hancock because I want a candidate who will actually win the election, not someone who has already lost an election to a Democrat,” Abbott said in a statement. Huffines also challenged Abbott in the 2022 Republican gubernatorial primary; Abbott drubbed him by 44 points. Prior to serving in the Legislature, Hancock was a member of his local school board. The state of Texas operates a $337 billion budget and rakes in more than a quarter trillion dollars of revenue from a variety of taxes, fees and assessments." | FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/109894558/552606714/-896966196?refcode=AU_SMP_FR_GEN_GEN_AC30_20250619_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82ODY3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMmY4ZmUzOC01MjRkLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ty8nn6XpTWmatgdwAHyVR-MvJw-fo0XG2o32Bxq06yI=)
Susan Collins hasn’t held a public town hall in _25 years_. Let that sink in. A whole quarter-century without standing face-to-face with the people she’s supposed to represent. No town halls. No forums. No real conversations. Just closed doors and party-line votes. And now, during Trump’s second term, Collins is walking an even thinner tightrope. She’s publicly claimed she doesn’t support him, but she’s still voted to enable his agenda at every turn. It’s the same old story with Thom Tillis: dodge voters, stay in power, and protect Trump. That’s not real representation. That’s political survival. They’re clinging to power no matter the cost to their constituents. And we’re not going to let them get away with it. **2026 is our chance to flip the Senate and send a message: Senators should work _for_ the people, not just show up for photo ops and checks.** **[But defeating deeply entrenched Republicans like Susan Collins will take serious grassroots power. Democrats everywhere are speaking out to unseat Trump's MAGA enablers, but we haven't heard from voters like you yet! **Our URGENT live poll closes at midnight, so tell us now:**

Sen. Bernie Moreno **[refused to take Kaitlan Collins' bait](https://substack.com/redirect/c1984a9e-f19a-4ef2-855e-68c9004ff800?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** to criticize President Donald Trump during a tense CNN interview about changes made to The Big Beautiful Bill by the senate.
He handled this perfectly:
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/c1984a9e-f19a-4ef2-855e-68c9004ff800?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c1984a9e-f19a-4ef2-855e-68c9004ff800?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
"Sen. Josh Hawley said today that the president told him that the was 'surprised' by changes the senate has made to this,... Do you agree with Sen. Hawley that it departs with what the president wanted to see here?" Collins asked Moreno.
The senator said he hadn't spoken with the president on the matter before reminding Collins that Medicaid abuses needed to be addressed by the legislation.
"We need to get illegal off of Medicaid, some of these states are using it to cover illegal migrants," Moreno said. "That would be categorically insane in any other country, the idea that we would take care of the healthcare of other citizens!"
Collins did not like his response, watch the tense exchange here:
[Click To Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/c1a9ef56-c157-4f8a-b203-ec8d7dae93d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**[Together with Mode Mobile](https://substack.com/redirect/bee213f2-82b8-4f64-80a2-913b15855e7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

They've just been granted their stock ticker by the Nasdaq, and you can still[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/bee213f2-82b8-4f64-80a2-913b15855e7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[invest in their pre-IPO 1 offering at just $0.30/share.](https://substack.com/redirect/bee213f2-82b8-4f64-80a2-913b15855e7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)5**

# Agents shut down Portland anti-ICE riot with nonlethal forces
**[Federal agents used nonlethal munitions](https://substack.com/redirect/767d0a88-daec-4d9f-9ff9-97228efc8250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** outside and I.C.E. facility in Portland Oregon Wednesday evening after anti-I.C.E protesters refused to adhere to multiple verbal warnings.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/767d0a88-daec-4d9f-9ff9-97228efc8250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Portland, Oregon Riots** -- A crowd of about **[250 protesters gathered outside](https://substack.com/redirect/6002ed87-41bf-4c21-8fa1-4400faff12d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement building Wednesday evening, chanting "Whose streets, our streets," and rolling a dumpster in front of the building's entrance.
* **Federal response to rising tensions** -- Officers from the U.S. Federal Protective Service declared an unlawful assembly late in the evening after protesters ignored multiple verbal warnings, deploying tear gas and other crowd-control munitions. Protesters were seen throwing smoke grenades at riot police.
* **Damage and disruption reported** -- The Portland Police Bureau (PPB) reported the arrested of two people. The repeated targeting of the ICE facility is in lieu of last week's "No Kings" protests nationwide against the Trump Administration's deportation process.
* **Broader federal ‐local tension** -- The confrontation comes amid rising debate over the scope of federal law enforcement in Portland, with the PBB stating it does not engage in immigration enforcement, but "will intervene when criminal acts occur."

* * *
# Trump extends TikTok ban deadline for third time
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/da8bf759-b1f7-4761-a0b6-8412a44f96db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
President Donald Trump **[signed an executive order](https://substack.com/redirect/da8bf759-b1f7-4761-a0b6-8412a44f96db?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** Thursday extending the deadline for TikTok to be sold to a U.S. buyer by 90 days, pushing the cutoff to Sept. 17, 2025 and marking the third reprieve for the Chinese-owned app amid national security concerns.
* **Data ‐security tussle continues** -- The extension is tied to legislation demanding TikTok's Chinese parent ByteDance sell ownership to Americans or face a national ban. Lawmakers and security experts warn that without a deal, user data and the app's algorithm remain under foreign influence.
* **Trump 's changing tone** -- After backing a ban in his first presidential term, Trump, who currently has over 15 million followers on the app, has adopted a softer stance, hoping to come to a deal where Americans can still use the video platform.
* **Dealmaking in limbo** -- Negotiations for a sale potentially involving Oracle, Amazon, Perplexity AI, and other billionaire founders have stalled due to U.S.‐China tariff tensions.

| | POLL

Should Trump ban TikTok once and for all?

# Karoline Leavitt FIRES BACK at Jasmine Crocket for calling Trump supporters "sick"
White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt **[mocked Rep. Jasmine Crockett](https://substack.com/redirect/814f2377-d8a3-41d6-8774-d98051adca7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** during Thursday's briefing, sarcastically calling her a "rising star" and saying "I hope that she continues to be a rising star, for the Republican Party at least" after being asked about Crockett's comments calling Trump supporters mentally "sick."
[](https://substack.com/redirect/f5c6e8c8-1230-43aa-8201-d8e4f32530f4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Defense of Trump Supporters:** Leavitt called Crockett's remarks "incredibly derogatory" toward nearly 80 million Americans, arguing that Crockett "couldn't dream of winning such a majority of the public as President Trump did." She described Trump's movement as comprising "hard-working patriots, forgotten men and women, business owners, law enforcement officers, nurses, and teachers" from middle America.
* **Crockett's Original Comments:** The exchange stemmed from Crockett's appearance on Katie Couric's podcast where she claimed widespread Trump support was evidence of a "mental health crisis," saying "everyone -- no matter how you affiliate yourself -- should be against Trump" and that she would prefer "any regular Republican" like George Bush over Trump, calling for bipartisan opposition to the president.

| | POLL
---
Which side of the political spectrum to you think has more mental illness?

---
[Conservatives/Trump supporters](https://substack..J1grbMD06irF4CzTqI9hDAkSUeNZe6N1ju2RF1noVB4?)
[Both equally](https://substack..wmDjkNqrZPDNNolGnchg58cBZ1vGjn6z7r1Jl_FU2x4?)
